On TOP of the still-unbuilt #8/#9 chair-block/package, it adds: **(a)** a **Ranking-Member (always opposing party) un-package / repackage counter** firing under any of **5 trait gates** (Efficient+crisis-trait on a matching crisis bill / higher-Legislative-than-chair / Manipulative-vs-Pliable-or-Predictable-chair / Iron-Fist-vs-Passive-chair / Magician-with-equal-Legislative); **(b)** two **chair-add-bill** powers (5 Legis+Efficient → add a tax/income-tax/tariff to ANY package even off-committee; 5 Legis+Magician → add one extra off-topic proposal); **(c)** **cross-chamber + cross-committee packaging FORBIDDEN** (the chair-add-tax is the lone off-committee exception) + a **Puritan committee-voting rule** (never votes for/against anything that (de)scores its own ideology; on conflict, ignore the Puritan rule for that bill). Makes committee bill-shaping a **chair-vs-ranking-member duel** at two pipeline points (chair block/replace/package/add → ranking-member un-package/repackage → committee vote with Puritan abstention → §12.5 floor). **Binds in the committee runner (`:3463`) + needs `committeeChairs` to grow a ranking-member field + a `Bill.packageOf?: BillId[]` package structure.** **★ Cross-check the 5 gates + chair-add powers vs. `tedchange` BEFORE building** (open Q logged, `game-context.md` #174 / digest §6). **S–M; folds into the committee/bill-packaging epic E14b (#8/#9/#12).** game-mechanics §12.5.1. |
| 55 | **★ #175 `Legislation` has NO `repealable` flag and NO law-class tag — a small data-model add (batch 23 / `pop2012b`, MrPotatoTed designer-authoritative)** | the `Legislation` interface (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has `id/year/title/description/sponsor*/committee/status/effects/votes` and **NO `repealable` and NO `lawClass`**; the floor runner `runPhase_2_6_3_Floor` (`phaseRunners.ts:3498`) resolves only `passed_committee` bills with no repeal/replace branch; **grep `repealable\|lawClass` in `src/` = ZERO** (the existing `Challenge-Legislation-can't-target-REPEAL` filter #132 is a separate guard) | MrPotatoTed (game co-author) ruled (`pop2012b#POST 687-688): *"Every law is marked with whether or not it can be repealed"* — a per-row flag ("Column P", LegisActive sheet) with **three classes**: **repealable** (a Repeal bill removes it through the normal pipeline — most policy bills); **replace-only** (non-repealable but **replaceable** — you cannot remove it, only supersede it with a new same-kind bill; **tax + immigration** laws); **permanent** (irreversible — **statehood**, *"once a state is a state, it's a state"*; no state can be abolished). This is the authoritative resolution of the `pop` §5.5 data-tag hole and explains why some passed policies are "downgrade-only" and why no Repeal-Statehood bill exists. **The fix: add `Legislation.repealable: boolean` + a `lawClass: 'repealable' \| 'replace-only' \| 'permanent'` tag; gate Repeal proposals on the flag; for replace-only laws expose a Replace action that supersedes (not removes) the prior law; mark statehood (and other one-way structural bills) `permanent` so no repeal/replace is offered.** **Pairs with #42 (the bill-relationship graph, §12.9 — `Not repealable`/replace-by-X/amendment-tier) + §27.5 (statehood-by-bill: stamp every admit-state bill `permanent`).** **S — a small data-model + pipeline-guard add inside the legislation/bill-relationship epic; needs an authored repealable/replace-only/permanent per-bill list (open Q, `game-context.md` #175).** game-mechanics §12.9. |

| 63 | **★ DH-71 faction-handover DUPLICATE-politician data-integrity defect — a transfer must MOVE, not COPY (batch 26 / `summer2021`, NEW BUG; a forward-looking CONSTRAINT, not a shipped regression)** | the faction-handover feature is itself UNBUILT: `grep handover\|transfer\|steal\|reassign in src/` = ZERO transfer code path; `partyId`/`factionId` are read-only filters across `src/pages/*` + `eraGraph.ts:42`, never re-assigned by an ownership-transfer function | **A mid-thread faction "steal" moved David Brearley to a new faction WITHOUT deleting the original → two identical records in two factions**; a later governor-nomination **pulled the WRONG one** (career-track flag mismatch — "shows he's on the career track but not on my sheet"; `summer2021 §20.15.1`, ch43 POST 3766). **So DH-71 is a CONSTRAINT on the (designed) faction-handover feature (#1), not a regression in shipped code:** whatever transfer function we build must mutate the SINGLE politician's `factionId` (a MOVE), never clone the record — politician identity must be unique on transfer. The build's owned-state model also removes the manual-spreadsheet ledger artifacts this run exposed (an ideology-tally MISCOUNT "said 20, were 17", ch2 POST 129/132; a crisis flag stuck "on" after the crisis ended, ch4 POST 286-287). **Where it binds:** the (new) faction-transfer function (#1) — enforce single-ownership. **XS (a constraint, not a fix); folds into #1 faction-handover.** game-mechanics §20.15.1. |
| 64 | **★★ DH-72 the election misapplied a `+1` rule as `+3` board-wide → the 492-40 landslide; the FIX is a TUNING PATCH at `calcStateVote`, the cheapest high-leverage win in the corpus (batch 27 / `redbutton`, DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE — vcczar + MrPotatoTed both played + agreed)** | `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723`) is the SINGLE election scorer; the score is `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8` (`:3709-3711`). **Re-verified batch 27, UNCHANGED:** enthusiasm read RAW (`:3696`), applied UNIFORMLY to every state, **NO ±3 cap, NO per-state ideology penalty/gate, raw `Math.random` (NOT a d6 or d3)**, and **no "+1 most-enthusiastic-base" term exists at all** (it is GM-hand-applied in the forum). So the DH-72 bug (a `+1`-cap read as `+3` board-wide + a separate bogus `+3` platform bonus) is **GM-side arithmetic in the hand-driven 1960 run**, not a shipped line — but it pinpoints EXACTLY how the to-be-built election term must be clamped | The forum drove platform→meter→election BY HAND and produced **Bricker (R) 492-40 over JFK** off a `+3`-everywhere misapplication; recompute ladder: remove the bogus `+3` platform bonus → 284-251; correct the per-state `+3`→`+1` → ~281-129-130-tied (near-tie, JFK-slight-favorite) (`redbutton#POST 532, 587, 603-607`). Designers UNANIMOUS: *"platform is way too powerful"*, *"I'll decrease the power"* (`#POST 538-540`). **The fix is a TUNING PATCH to the (to-be-built) election scorer at `calcStateVote`, NOT new structure:** (a) **cap the platform/enthusiasm meter contribution to +2/+1/−1** (highest-scoring ideology +2, 2nd +1, lowest −1) — replaces uncapped per-plank stacking; (b) **change the end-of-election roll d6→d3** — but the SHIPPED jitter is `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` (a continuous ±4 band, NOT a die at all), so this binds as "narrow the jitter amplitude AND route it through `rng.ts` (debt #1/#3)"; (c) **raise the historical state-bias term from +1/+2→+3/+5** (the shipped `baseLean*5` is the bias term — re-weight it) behind a **bias-mode TOGGLE** (historical-plausible vs. "go-crazy"); (d) **HIDE the raw formulas** in the UI (anti-collusion design philosophy — show only which ideologies like a position + polls/maps, never the math). **Where it binds:** `phaseRunners.ts:3709-3711` (the score sum) + the bias term `:3697` + the jitter `:3711` (→`rng.ts`) + a UI surface (hide-the-math) + a `game.biasMode?` toggle. **XS-S as a tuning patch (the cheapest high-value election fix in the corpus) — BUT note the platform engine that FEEDS the capped term is itself UNBUILT (#184), so the cap clamps a term that doesn't yet exist; the d3/jitter/bias-toggle/hide-the-math sub-fixes are independently shippable today and pair with the QW3 ±3 cap (debt #1) + #18 scorer.** **Open Q (designer, still unresolved at the table):** does the ideology-enthusiasm value translate to a per-STATE bonus or only to the meter (`#POST 604-607` got no answer) — same ambiguity as #18/§22.2. game-mechanics §15.3.5, §28.6. |
| 65 | **★★ #184 the PLATFORM ENGINE is OVERPOWERED + entirely UNBUILT — a 4-step plank-comparison + meter-mover that does not exist in code; designer-confirmed nerf SPEC in hand (batch 27 / `redbutton`, DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE)** | **`grep platform\|plank\|conventionPlatform in src/engine` = ZERO** (re-verified batch 27; corroborates #181 — the platform/convention layer is wholly designed-not-built). | 68 | **★★ #186 a late/mid-cycle scenario boot must be DETERMINISTIC — the 1960 boot (a FIFTH BootSheet shape) was entirely improvised; consolidates the boot cluster from the modern era (batch 27 / `redbutton`, the single most build-relevant cluster in the source)** | NO generic boot abstraction (re-verified batch 27): `GameContext.tsx:5-6` imports `build1772Scenario`/`build1856Scenario`; `startNewGame(factionId, scenarioId?: '1772'\|'1856')` (`:24`) dispatches to hand-authored data files; **no BootSheet, no scenarioBoot, no year-keyed incumbent/office table** (`grep scenarioBoot\|BootSheet\|bootScenario in src/` = ZERO); #115 (debt #46-area) captures the 1820 boot-procedure gap, #86 the boot-data shape | The 1960 MP is the **FIRST modern-era boot in the corpus** and the cleanest late-start failure. The 1960 scenario was authored for the **1960-62 GOVERNANCE turn (JFK president, offices for 1961)** but players forced an **Elections-Phase start one turn early** → cascading manual patches: offices/incumbents wrong (Senate seeded 50-50 vs real 66-D Jan-1960); no Blue Party Leader (game requires the Leadership trait → GM hand-picked LBJ); the **party-leader on-election rolls** (+Command/lose-Obscure/+3-primaries/3-term party-pref penalty) skipped; **party-fatigue (#187) never applied**; governor terms-served unknowable (blanket "2 two-year terms / ex-govs 8 yrs"); **delegate weights + primary calendar hand-assigned** (#185); DC EVs invalid until 1961; **mid-cycle = NO Command-earning runway** → roster frozen out of high office, players asked to back-date the last 2-4 draft classes into career tracks (the DH-25 fix from a modern angle) (game-mechanics §28.1, §28.6; `redbutton#POST 64, 73, 84-87, 522, 528, 543-560, 645, 931-938`). **★ Designer verdict:** eras are *"the stoniest"* thing in the game, can't be retrofitted at a button-press → a clean per-year boot TABLE is the only path (`#POST 528`). **The fix (the deterministic late/mid-era boot pipeline, as a 5th `BootSheet` shape):** for any chosen start year — (a) year-correct incumbents/offices/chamber balances (pick the §26.1 #164 election-start vs president-in-place fork EXPLICITLY per scenario, with a verifier); (b) auto-elect a Leadership-gated Party Leader AND apply the on-election rolls (not skip them); (c) **retro-apply party-fatigue (#187)** to whichever party is dominant at the start year; (d) seed delegate weights (Cat 1-5) + the primary calendar (#185) so the first convention is reproducible; (e) back-date recent draft classes into career tracks (DH-25/#163, ACUTE at a mid-cycle start); (f) handle year-keyed EV validity (DC 1961); **(g) ★★ BATCH-35 NEW REQUIREMENT — a deterministic per-office, per-era CABINET/ADVISOR STAT-SEED table.** The `redbutton1960` RESTART is the cleanest LIVE PROOF that boot-stats are LOAD-BEARING, not cosmetic: the original 1960 run was STOPPED because the cabinet was seeded with STARTER-LEVEL stats ("all at starter levels and it tanked all the meters since we didn't have any Cabinet members above 3", `redbutton1960#POST 1`) — the low-Admin officers drove the #179 lingering meter-roll (debt-area, the admin-ladder→meter coupling, re-confirmed at WHOLE-CABINET scale) into crisis at turn 1, and the era was UNPLAYABLE until the GM hand-RE-STATTED the entire cabinet to mid/peak (one 5-Admin, four 4s, rest 3s; ambassadors a 5 [Kissinger] + four 4s; a Key Advisor 2→4). **VERIFIED the gap:** scenario boots pull cabinet members from whoever already holds the office in the hand-authored seed roster, or leave the seat `null` (`scenario1856.ts:127-142`) — there is **NO per-office/per-era stat-seed table** guaranteeing era-appropriate Admin/competence for the sitting government. So a deterministic late/mid-era boot MUST seed cabinet/ambassador/advisor officers at era-appropriate mid/peak strength (not draftee starter levels), or the #179 engine drags every meter into crisis on turn 1. The per-era cabinet-strength curve is still an OPEN Q (the mid/peak reset was eyeballed by the GM). **Where it binds:** the `scenarioBoot(BootSheet)` pipeline + `BootSheet` schema (K4, §9.1.9) — extend with a `startMode: 'electionPhase'\|'governance'` discriminant + an `asOfYear` incumbent table + `seededLeaderTenure` (for #187) + `delegateWeights`/`primaryCalendar` fields + `careerTrackBackfill` + **a per-office/per-era `cabinetStatSeed` table (the batch-35 requirement)**. **M-L; RIDES K4's `BootSheet` + the `scenarioBoot` pipeline (#115); the modern boot is the most-demanding instantiation, so it SHARPENS the K4/scenarioBoot spec rather than adding a keystone.** **Open Q (designer): the per-year incumbent/office tables AND the per-era cabinet-stat curve must be AUTHORED (research-bound, like all era content).** game-mechanics §28.1, §28.6; `redbutton1960#POST 1, 2`. |

DO the boot retro-apply WITH #186.** **Open Q (designer): exact penalty curve (−1 flat at 6yr vs growing); does it key on party-leadership tenure or governing-dominance tenure.** game-mechanics §8.5.3, §28.1. |

| 71 | **★★ The D6-vs-D3 election-die FORK is RESOLVED at code level: our build ships NEITHER die — only a raw `Math.random` ±4 jitter (batch 28 / `completions` + `fixes` — the `completions` "D6 CONFIRMED-SHIPPED" claim REFUTED at `file:line`)** | **The single election swing-randomizer is `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` at `phaseRunners.ts:3711`** (a continuous ±4 band, raw `Math.random`, NOT seeded). **`completions`/game-mechanics §15.5(a) tagged D6 "CONFIRMED-SHIPPED" at `phaseRunners.ts:727` — REFUTED:** `:727` is `factionExpertiseBias`'s `clamp(Math.round(biasedMean), 0, 6)` (a faction-lean clamp, unrelated to elections). **By contrast the TRAIT-CONFLICT d6 IS genuinely shipped AND routed through the RNG seam:** `tryGrantTrait` (`traits.ts:41-59`) resolves a held-conflict on `d(6) >= TRAIT_LIFECYCLE_RULES.conflictD6Threshold` (`:53`; `d` from `rng.ts`, imported `:3`) — **CONFIRMED-BUILT**, though the threshold is **`4`** (`types.ts:638`, i.e. **1-3 cancel / 4-6 keep**), NOT the 2022 sheet's "1-2 cancel / 3-6 keep" (a tuning delta, not a structural gap). | **The fork resolves to a TUNING+SEEDING patch, not a die-swap:** there is no live die to revert from d6→d3 — the move is "**narrow the `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` amplitude to the d3-equivalent compressed band AND route it through `rng.ts`**" (fuses debt #1/#3 + the #184/DH-72 batch-27 sub-fix). The 2022 D6-revert was made *because* state biases were retuned to ±5 for a d6; the `redbutton` D3-nerf wants the *same +3/+5 bias* with a *tighter* die — so **whichever band ships, the per-state bias term (`baseLean*5`, `:3697`) must be re-checked against it** (do NOT adopt a band without re-tuning bias). **Where it binds:** `phaseRunners.ts:3711` (jitter→`rng.ts`) + `:3697` (bias term) — the SAME site as debt #1/#64. **XS-S; this is the QW that "ties off" the fork so the roadmap isn't ambiguous — it is NOT a new subsystem, and it is NOT yet covered by any named QW (QW19–QW21 are referenced in `game-context.md` but UNDEFINED in this guide; this row defines the die half).** **Optional follow-up:** align the trait-conflict threshold `4→3` if the designer 1-2/3-6 spec is adopted (`types.ts:638`, XS one-const edit). game-mechanics §15.5(a), §15.3.5. |
| 72 | **★ #179 the cabinet LINGERING-ROLL meter engine now has CONCRETE NUMBERS (Admin→{upOdds,downOdds} table + Efficient ±2 + JCoS carve-out + 25% lobby-dissat) — the shipped lingering pass is a SIMPLER deterministic drift, designed-not-built confirmed (batch 28 / `to-do` + `fixes`)** | **The shipped `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260-3319`) uses a flat deterministic `drift(skill)` table** (Admin≥4 → +0.5, ≥3 → +0.2, ≥2 → 0, ≥1 → −0.2, else −0.5; `:3266-3273`) applied additively per meter — **NO probabilistic roll, NO Efficient magnitude, NO JCoS exclusion, NO lobby-dissatisfaction.** So #179 is correctly DESIGNED-not-built; the current code is a placeholder. | The §20.10 build-out (now numeric): an **Admin→{upOdds, downOdds} table** (endpoints Admin5 75%/0%, Admin0 0%/100%) **rolled per portfolio-keyed officer** each lingering phase (via `rng.ts`); **Efficient = ±2 magnitude** on a successful move (vs the ±1 base); a **25% per-pick lobby-dissatisfaction chance** feeding faction enthusiasm (the cabinet-as-lobby-appeasement minigame, Key-Advisor weighted highest); and a **HARD exclusion of Senior General / Senior Admiral / JCoS from the lingering roll** (the #49 carve-out — Mil-Prep moves via Sec of War/Navy/Defense, NOT the uniformed chiefs). **Where it binds:** replace the `drift()` body in `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260`) with the roll engine + a portfolio→meter map that EXCLUDES the chiefs + a per-pick lobby-dissat hook into the §22.2 enthusiasm engine. `GM⇒App` (the GM rolled these by hand each turn). **S-M; standalone, NO upstream keystone — ready to build (the spec is now fully numeric); pairs with #49 (the JCoS-tanks-MilPrep pathology this carve-out fixes).** game-mechanics §20.10(g)-(j). |
| 73 | **★ #189–#192 the DOWN-BALLOT (Gov/Sen/Rep) election model — a separate, RICHER modifier stack than the presidential one (#103); PARTLY SHIPPED, the rest designed (batch 28 / `to-do` + `fixes`)** | **`calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723`) + `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts`) already route `governor`/`senatePre17`/`house` contexts and apply SOME traits + the enthusiasm/party-pref meters** (`:3708`), but only the ~5 presidential traits; **the full down-ballot trait table, the expertise/lobby↔industry link, per-seat bias, and the margin→pop-vote-% layer are ABSENT** (no `deviantPartyBias` field, no expertise→industry election hook — verified `grep deviantBias\|votingPower src/` = ZERO). | Four cooperating layers on the scorer: **(#189)** a full down-ballot TRAIT table (Likeable/Charismatic ±1, Leadership +1, Orator/Debater 50%→+1, Propagandist ±1, Puritan ±1 on ideology-match, Not-Obscure 50%→+1, Lowbrow/Egghead situational, **FL +1 / PL +3 in primaries**) — SEPARATE from the presidential `applyTraitElectionBonus` stack; **(#190)** an **expertise↔leading-industry +2 / matching-lobby-card +1 / crisis-expertise +1** modifier (the "Manchin rule," stronger than in presidential races) — the election payoff of the industry-leadership system (#35); **(#191 — ★★ BATCH-33 now FULLY AUTHORED, `housepoll` aed35c6e POST 17-19 supplies the concrete per-seat ENGINE this row only gestured at; ★ BATCH-35 DESIGN-ORIGIN = the `electiondisc` Election-discussion thread (5a5d988f, Apr 2022, PREDATES `housepoll`) where the "Kevin McCarthy problem" was first raised — a strong minority-party pol in a heavily-leaning state can never win — with the leading `+2/+2/+1` per-seat-lean proposal; `housepoll` later authored the concrete engine):** a per-House-seat **`deviantPartyBias`** that supersedes *only the party term* of state bias (ideology/religion biases still apply) + per-seat vote weights, **STATIC** (vcczar ruled no floating — holds). The authored engine: **(1) minority-party-seat guarantee** — any state at **5+ Focus Reps** must have a minority-party seat (started at **Bias +2** for the minority) if none exists (the "≥20% Red Reps in CA" rule); **(2) new-seat bias roll** = 25% +1 toward the state majority (Gov+Sen+Rep, tie→random) / 25% tossup / 50% to the party holding the most **era-keyed growth lobbies** (pre-Progressivism: Big Ag, Big Oil&Gas, Transportation, Isolationist; Progressivism+: Public Housing, Labor Unions, Technology, Welfare); **(3) seat GAIN** re-roll per district: 50% no change / 25% tossup / 25% → most-growth-lobby party; **(4) seat LOSS** re-roll: 50% no change / 25% tossup / 25% → FEWEST-growth-lobby party (shrinking states trend conservative). This is the per-seat population-realignment engine; it RIDES the same per-seat struct as #219's vote-power-with-remainder (debt #87) + the #34 census + the row-74 gerrymander gov-action that SETS the bias. **(#192)** a per-state per-era **max-margin table + margin→popular-vote-% derivation + PV caps** (displayed pop-vote-% DERIVED from the die margin, not raw PV; *"Reagan can win every state but not 90% popular vote"*) — this is the design intent behind the §29 landslide-margin misfire. **Where it binds:** extend `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685`) with a down-ballot-only trait table + the expertise/lobby modifier + a `State.houseSeats[].deviantPartyBias` schema (new field) + a `MAX_MARGIN` per-era const + a margin→% post-pass on the scorer output. **M; builds ON the existing down-ballot routing (not greenfield); #192 pairs with #14/#18/#184 (the swing layer) but is DISTINCT from it; #191's seat bias is SET BY the gerrymander gov-action (row 74).** game-mechanics §15.4(a)-(d). |

| 85 | **★★ THE PV-SYSTEM OVERHAUL — `pv.ts`'s `computePV` ships a PRE-REVAMP formula (flat ±4/−5 per trait, flat `command×10`, magic `Kingmaker +6`, a `Math.max(0,…)` clamp) that the `revampPV` design supersedes; #214 per-trait tiers + #215 per-ability curve + #216 BLOCKING trait-name remap + #220 display-scale + DH-77 (batch 30, `revampPV`/`summer2021`, DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE-but-UNRESOLVED; the headline; `GM⇒App`)** | **All four code sites VERIFIED in `pv.ts`:** traits resolve to a flat `+4` (any positive) / `−5` (any negative) — a per-trait loop with NO coefficient (`pv.ts:75-77`); skills are flat `skill × officeWeight` then `×4` (`pv.ts:70-73`); `command × 10` flat per level (`pv.ts:74`); `Kingmaker` adds a hard-coded magic `+6` on top of its positive `+4` (`pv.ts:79`); the result is `Math.max(0, Math.round(total))`-clamped (`pv.ts:88`). Office prestige / faction-leader `+8` / age penalties (>70, <30) / `flipFlopperPenalty*5` are also in `computePV` (`pv.ts:80-87`). **★ The #216 remap blocker — COUNTED at code level:** of the ~57 traits valued in the `revampPV` P33 tier table (digest §1), **~15 are absent from the shipped `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117`)** — Teflon, Military Leader, Bookkeeper, Geostrategist, Everyman, Lawful/Cop, Jurisprudence, Union Loyalist, Disharmonious, Lackey, Late Bloomer, Illicit, Pliable, Incoherent, Easily Overwhelmed — **plus 2 pure renames** (`Flipflopper`→`Flip-Flopper`, `Two-faced`→`Two-Faced`, both PRESENT under the new key); conversely **12+ shipped traits were NEVER valued in 2023** (Nationalist, Globalist, Reformist, Loyal, Ambitious, Opportunist, Impressionable, Scandalous, Corrupt, Traitor, Outsider, Ideologue). So **~17 of ~57 line-items (~30%) mis-key or drop without the remap.** | **The single most build-relevant DESIGN delta in batch 30; PV drives elections so change carefully.** Four cooperating parts on `computePV`: **(a) #214** replace the flat ±4/−5 with a **per-trait point table seeded from vcczar P33 as TIERS** (25/10/7/3/−3/−7/−10, ±25 extremes — RE-FIT the coefficients, the deferred OrangeP47 pass; vcczar disowned the exact numbers, *"the RANKING is the point"*) and fold Kingmaker + Master-Kingmaker into the table (drop the `:79` magic `+6`); keep the office/age/FF terms (`:80-87`) ADDITIVE, do NOT conflate, and do NOT double-count `flipFlopperPenalty` (`:86`) vs the Flipflopper trait. **(b) #215** replace the flat `skill×weight×4` / `command×10` with a **non-linear first-point-worth-more curve** (Command ≈25/level decreasing; Legislative 10 then +3) + **Admin/Military low-floor thresholds** (Admin +10 from the 2nd point = the cabinet-eligibility bar); leave the UNRESOLVED restrict-born-Command fight OPEN (a balance toggle — it breaks modern starts + 3rd-party rules if forced). **(c) #216 (BLOCKING)** produce an explicit forum→shipped trait-name map (renames / removed / added-since) — #214 is UN-APPLYABLE until this exists. **(d) #220 + DH-77** decide the display layer: replace the hard `Math.max(0,…)` clamp (`pv.ts:88`) with a **constant floor-OFFSET** so sub-zero pols keep ordering for the CPU, and pick human presentation — raw ("pink", revampPV lean) vs 0-100/100-normalized (summer2021 POST 200-205) — normalized acceptable only if PV is hidden/AI-only; UNRESOLVED in both sources. **★ ELECTION COUPLING (the change-carefully part):** `calcStateVote` reads `pv = c.pvCache` (`phaseRunners.ts:3699`) and adds **`pv * 0.1`** to every state-vote score (`:3709`) — so PV directly SHIFTS election outcomes; this binds to the election-balance cluster (#18/#184/DH-72/QW19-21, debt #1/#64/#65/#71). **★★ BATCH-33 RULING — REMOVE `pv*0.1`, do NOT re-tune (`groupthinkpv` c6f6dcc7 POST 36-38; SUPERSEDES this row's prior "re-tune the `pv*0.1` weight after the reweight" note).** The designer + room ruled *PV must NOT feed elections* — pricing PV into the vote DOUBLE-COUNTS the traits/abilities already in BOTH PV and the election roll (a "highest-PV election bonus" proposal was REJECTED; keep the 50%-chance-+1 ability bonus). This is the spec for debt #59/#72 (the lingering-engine placeholder). **#50/#51 (`rulebook#POST 10`):** cards re-evaluated every 2 yrs by PLURALITY of the faction's own pols (ideology card = most-pols-of-that-ideology, ties shared, every faction MUST hold ≥1) + interest/lobby compatibility-ban tables (Civil-Rights ✗ Con/Trad; Nationalist ✗ Lib/Mod; Wall-Street ✗ Prog/Pop; LW-Media MUST have Lib/Prog; etc.) + a **5-pol floor** on interest/lobby cards + a **cascade** to the next-eligible faction when one is ineligible. This is the spec under the enthusiasm engine (debt #30/E16) + the FL ideology-binding. **#179 → M (a real roll engine, rides the meter ladders); #50/#51 → M (the card-assignment pass feeds the enthusiasm reshuffle).** Both `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §9.3.11-adjacent, §27.x, §30.21. |

Each policy entry carries: a **mechanism prefix** (`L-`=Legislation / `P-`=Pres-Action / `G-`=Gov-Action / `S-`=Scripted-event) + an **era-band TAG from the SAME #92/#206 vocabulary** (`Ind/Fed/Rep/Dem/Prog/Gild/Nat/Nuc/Neo/Norm/Ter/Pop/Ide/Fut/Luc`; `-Fut`=Era-of-Future) + a **`Preq:` chain** gated on (i) other policy flags [min-wage ladder 75¢→$15], (ii) scripted-event/world state [AFL→AFL-CIO], (iii) **the shipped `economic` meter** ["Recession or Worse"→Create-Government-Work; "Depression or Worse"→WPA/PWA — read as a gate AND fed back into], (iv) party-type/scenario flags [Communist-major→Nationalize-Unions — **ties the #236 authoritarian kit**]. | 101 | **★ #241 THE ELECTION CADENCE IS HARDCODED `year % 4` / `year % 2` ARITHMETIC, NOT DATA — a variable presidential term length (a passed 6-yr/2-yr-presidency Amendment) would break every predicate; the cadence must become a term-length FIELD the phase scheduler reads (batch 33, `ampuelections`, vcczar's own voice; DESIGNED, 0% shipped; `GM⇒App`)** | **Verified hardcoded.** `isPresidentialYear`/`isDraftYear` = `year % 4 === 0`, `isElectionYear` = `year % 2 === 0` (`phases.ts:53-58`); `shouldRunPhase` (`phases.ts:62+`) gates the 2.9.x election phases off these. **There is NO term-length field anywhere in `src/`** (`grep -i 'termLength\|presidentialTerm\|termYears'` = ZERO); `ConstitutionalArticles.termLimits` (`types.ts:1396`) is a binary `'two_terms'\|'no_limits'` = a term-COUNT, not a term LENGTH; the 4-year length is baked into a LABEL STRING only (`constitutionalConvention.ts:19`). | **vcczar flagged this in his own voice** (`ampuelections` 92f0659b POST 1, 6): he added "6-year term presidency" + "2-year term presidency" Amendments, then warned they "might upset how the election cycles are programmed." A 6-/2-yr or variable term BREAKS every `year%4`/`%2` predicate (and the 2-yr-per-turn rollover). The fix: **replace the year-predicates with a variable election-cadence model** — a per-office term-length field (default 4-yr pres / 2-yr House / 6-yr Senate-class) read by the phase scheduler, so a passed term-length Amendment reschedules the presidential cycle deterministically. **★ This is the SECOND, INDEPENDENT reason the hardcoded cadence must go** — #92's era-as-content-band model ALSO wants the year-predicates replaced (era-advance keys off game-state, not calendar; debt #5, §9.1.5). Build the cadence replacement ONCE and serve both. **★ Couples to #242** (term length is one of the amendable `ConstitutionalArticles` fields — debt #102) + the election cluster #18/#184/#185 (which assume a fixed 4-yr cycle). **★ HUMAN-CALL:** is the 6-/2-yr term a feature to BUILD (variable cadence) or to DROP (vcczar tagged both "may delete")? — an UNRESOLVED designer/programmer tension, not a settled rule. **M (the predicate-replacement is the cost; it's shared with #92's `advanceEra` work, so do it inside K3 — see §9.1.5).** `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §2.3, §30.23. |
| 102 | **★ #242 `ConstitutionalArticles` IS SET ONCE AT THE CC AND NEVER MUTATED — the design wants it AMENDABLE mid-game (a passed Amendment rewrites an article on 75%-state ratification) + several NEW article fields (batch 33, `ampuelections`, corroborates #92/#221; DESIGNED, 0% shipped; `GM⇒App`)** | **Verified set-once.** `ConstitutionalArticles` (`types.ts:1389-1397`, 7 fields) is written EXACTLY ONCE — at the Constitutional Convention (`constitutionalConvention.ts:145` `snap.game.constitutionalArticles = finalArticles`) or seeded in the 1856 scenario (`scenario1856.ts:182`) — and **never mutated by legislation/amendments thereafter**; `articleKey` (`types.ts:1796`) is part of the one-time `ConventionVote` shape, not a mid-game amendment path. So `ConstitutionalArticles` must become a **LIVING, amendable structure**: a constitutional-Amendment Legis-Prop primitive (#221 — debt #97/#98 area) that, on ratification, rewrites the relevant article field; PLUS ADD the missing fields (suffrage/franchise scope, `electoralCollege` on/off, `houseSizeCap`, presidential `termLength` per #241). **★ DEPENDENCY: this is a downstream consumer of #221** (an Amendment IS a Legis-Prop content-primitive) + #241 (term-length is one new field) + #92 + #100 (the parked SCOTUS-overturns-an-Amendment rule also assumes amendable articles). **The data shape (`ConstitutionalArticles`) already exists** — the build is the mutation PATH (an amendment-ratify handler writing the struct) + the new fields + a `DB_VERSION`/`repair()` default for the new fields. **M; GATED on #221; pairs with #241/#92/#100.** `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §14.2, §30.23. |
| 103 | **★ DH-78 the shipped `TraitElectionRule` CANNOT express a same-candidate TWO-TRAIT AND-combo (Celebrity AND Military Leader → +1) — only an OPPONENT-conditional swap (batch 33, `excelautomate`, verified against code; DESIGNED/DEFECT-of-TYPE-SHAPE; `GM⇒App`)** | **Verified.** `TraitElectionRule` (`types.ts:727`) supports per-`trait` + per-`context` + signed `magnitude` + `era?` + an **opponent-conditional** swap (`opponentConditional.ifOpponentHas` → `bumpedMagnitude`); the `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` table (`types.ts:738`) is consumed by `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts:16`) in `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3707-3708`). But there is **NO "this candidate ALSO holds trait X" combo field** — a rule firing only when the SAME candidate holds two specified traits is inexpressible. | **The spreadsheet GM built a combo the shipped rule shape can't represent** (`excelautomate` 79fbbb46 POST 50): a VLOOKUP+COUNTIF detected a candidate holding **both `Celebrity` AND `Military Leader` → +1**. | 104 | **★★ DH-79 SHIPPED BUG: "one naval win ends naval forever" — the naval tally is RECORDED but NEVER READ by the win-check (batch 34, `rethinkwar`, VERIFIED against code; SMALL STANDALONE QUICK-FIX; `GM⇒App`)** | **VERIFIED in code.** `runRevWarBattles` runs the naval phase and records the result — `if (win) war.navalWins++; else war.navalLosses++;` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:202`) — but the war-over check reads **ONLY the ground tallies**: win at `war.currentGroundWins >= war.groundWinsNeeded` (`:254`), loss at `war.currentGroundLosses >= war.groundLossesRemaining && !war.frenchAlliance` (`:259`). `navalWins`/`navalLosses` are **dead state** — they affect NOTHING in the outcome (the war can be won with the navy losing every battle — exactly the `ted1772` "Navy won ZERO at sea, war still won" trace). | **This is the shipped confirmation of the complaint that ORIGINATES the #45 redesign** (`rethinkwar` 0fd0f2e5 POST 1, 16). **★ TECH-LEAD CALL: this is a SMALL STANDALONE QUICK-FIX, not a wait-for-#45 item.** The #45 War-Score rewrite (debt #105) is POST-LAUNCH, but naval being purely cosmetic is a visible "naval never matters" defect that should not sit until then. **Two cheap-fix options (pick one):** (a) **the #45-aligned minimal slice** — make a naval WIN give the next ground general **+1 Mil for that battle only (75% chance), a naval LOSS −1 Mil** (the `revolutionaryWar.ts:212-214` `baseTarget` already sums `general.military`-derived terms, so a per-phase ±1 nudge on the general's effective Mil drops in cleanly and is forward-compatible with the #45 naval-as-difficulty-modifier design); or (b) **feed the naval tally into the win-path** (a small naval contribution toward `groundWinsNeeded` / against the loss cap). Option (a) is preferred — it is the durable mechanic the #45 design lands on, so no rework later. **XS, RevWar-scoped, standalone; route any new roll through `rng.ts` (the naval-win d100 already uses the seeded `d100`, but the casualty picker at `:89,:97` does NOT — see debt #2/#106).** Likely a **QW** (see §9.6 cheap-fixes lane). `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §17.4 (DH-79), §21.1.A. |

trait mods LW-Activist +25 Lib · RW-Activist +25 Cons · Pliable +50 Swing · Lackey +50 toward the active Kingmaker · Puritan −50 Swing; mods can exceed 100% → CPU re-normalize, an open UX Q) + **(A2) Judicial Doctrine** = one/two per-case aye/nay ±% modifiers on TAGGED cases, independent of ideology (the "Scalia joins the liberals on 4th-Amdt grounds" effect; **deny cert on a LANDMARK case → the SC LOSES points** (ties #249's Landmark tier); Manipulative/Iron-Fist/Jurisprudence party leaders may interfere with a granted-cert case. **★ The ONLY thing SHIPPED here is in FORUM-ERA AMPU 1, NOT this browser build** (POST 15/18): a confirmed SC justice has a **10% chance to immediately shift ideology ±1 ON CONFIRMATION**, further shifts only per 10 yrs served, **+5% more likely leftward** (Souter archetype) — a tighter, confirmation-anchored variant of #52's 10-yr drift (drums POST 7533). **★ Also fully ARGUES the #52 player-vs-CPU debate** (see #52's annotation) — CE2's independent-default-court critique + a concrete Roe abuse-vector vs vcczar's keep-the-player-SC; the same fork `pop`/`dem1820`/`oopscpu` leave unsettled. **GM-REPLACEMENT LENS:** this is the REFEREE's-court-half — the engine adjudicating who hears a case (Focus Court vs SC), whether cert is granted (Rule-of-Four), and the ruling outcome + its point/meter effects — all work a GM does by hand today. | **MOSTLY AMPU-2 (a deferred epic — do NOT scope pre-launch):** (A) per-justice/judge **Judicial Philosophy** (Judicial Ideology base-table + trait-mods + CPU re-normalize past 100% / Judicial Doctrine per-tagged-case ±%, hidden-until-revealed via Career-Track/Focus-Court/direct-appt); (B) **Focus Courts** (4 regional appellate courts + Original/Appellate routing + Rule-of-Four cert + affirm/reverse-points-unchanged + cert-denied→Focus-Judge-scores + deny-Landmark→SC-loses-points wiring into #249 + appointing-Pres enthusiasm-drop roll). **★ ONE SMALL SHIPPABLE NEAR-TERM SUB-ITEM:** port the forum-AMPU-1 partial — a **confirmed-justice 10% immediate ±1 ideology shift + per-10-yr further shifts + 5% leftward bias** (XS-S; a tighter variant of #52/drums' 10-yr drift; **MUST reconcile with #52's 10-yr drift rule (25%/10%/5% mid/left/right) so the two do NOT double-fire** — pick ONE drift system: either the #52 per-decade rule OR this confirmation-anchored variant, not both). Resolve the "State Judicial Career Track"→"Judicial Career Track" rename. **Sizing: AMPU-2 epic = L-XL (Focus-Court routing + the two-axis Philosophy model are net-new subsystems, gated behind the SCOTUS docket #25/E25); the 10% confirmation-drift sub-item = XS-S standalone.** Pairs #52/#218/#249/#25/E25/#6/#354/#112/#136. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §22.7.v (#251), §11.6. |

The SAME single-expertise model appears in `TRACK_EXPERTISE` (`types.ts:217-225`, Judicial→`'Justice'`). `Expertise` itself is a flat union (`types.ts:182-191`) with no Civil-Rights/Constitution/crime/terrorism/immigration tags. | **A committee-design hole vcczar CONCEDES (`terminology` POST 40-42).** Players surface that the **Judicial committee grants only "Justice"** whereas other committees offer several expertise options, and want judicial sub-expertises: **Civil Rights, Constitution, crime, terrorism, immigration.** ★ vcczar/Ted RULING: NOT added as their own expertises — *"Civil Rights is an INTEREST, which is different from EXPERIENCE"* (the other four "could be under judicial, military, welfare, etc but don't exist as their own experience thing") → **WONTFIX as separate experiences (the interest≠experience distinction)** — BUT vcczar explicitly concedes the underlying complaint: *"never been happy with how the Judicial committee is just Justice."* ShortKing (POST 42) defends the status quo as flavor (junk judicial assignments simulate undesirable committees / punishing minority factions). Sub-fact (Ted POST 43): the **Senate Judicial committee approves SC nominees** (ties #25/#52 confirmation). So the gap is **acknowledged-but-deferred**: the coarse one-committee→one-expertise mapping stands, with judicial sub-expertise (and the interest-vs-experience distinction) as the deferred refinement. **GM-REPLACEMENT LENS:** referee-half — a content/schema granularity choice, not a GM-hand-adjudication burden; the only live coupling is the Senate-Judicial→SC-nominee confirmation (the referee's court-half, #52). | **Author-time / DESIGNER-DEFERRED (treat as WONTFIX-as-experiences):** do NOT add Civil-Rights/Constitution/crime/terrorism/immigration as new judicial EXPERTISES (designer ruling — interest ≠ experience; those would route to the INTERESTS vocabulary #168 if anywhere). **Optional future refinement (small, if the designer reopens it):** let the Judicial committee fan out to MORE than the single `Justice` expertise (vcczar "never been happy" with Justice-only) — a few-line change to the `COMMITTEE_EXPERTISE` value (e.g. a `Record<…, Expertise[]>` + pick) + matching `Expertise` union additions. | 115 | **★★ #253 THE Red/Blue PER-ERA BENCHMARK SCORER — the concrete data+engine deliverable under #68/#102; NEEDS a per-era SNAPSHOT ARCHIVE on `FullGameSnapshot` (a NEW persisted-state requirement) + an end-of-era scoring pass; GATED on a real 13/15-era band model existing first (batch 37, `benchmarkupd` 217d1977, VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped; `GM⇒App`)** | **VERIFIED 0% shipped on BOTH halves.** (a) **No end-of-era scoring code:** `grep -rniE 'benchmark\|endOfEra\|eraScore\|winCondition' src/` = ZERO; the only game-over path is event-driven `EraEvent.triggersGameEnd` (`types.ts:1476`, consumed `phaseRunners.ts:2871` → `GameOverScreen` `App.tsx:341`; cumulative-score winner per debt #28) — there is no per-era checkpoint, no benchmark predicate evaluator, no party-relative scoreboard. (b) **No per-era snapshot archive:** the only history struct is `HalfTermSummary` (`types.ts:1762`), stored as the per-TURN array `game.halfTermSummaries?` (`types.ts:1645`) — a per-half-term roll-up, NOT an era-boundary archive of economic levers. (c) The shipped `Era` enum is **4 values** (`types.ts:1337`), so there is **no 13-era boundary to score AT** — the scorer is doubly-blocked (no era boundaries + no archive). | **The win/score model from #68/#102 is now CONTENT-COMPLETE as a DATASET** (vcczar, tier-1, sole author): ~**10 benchmarks/era × 13 eras × 2 parties ≈ 260** authored, party-specific state PREDICATES, evaluated **at END OF ERA** with **active-or-issued** semantics (active at era end for lingering policy/state; "issued" during the era for one-shots). DUAL PARTISAN (Red/Blue) MIRRORED sets = each party's per-era ideological win-state. **★ THREE distinct new code surfaces:** **(i) a per-era SNAPSHOT ARCHIVE** — the "tariff/income-tax-bracket higher/lower than PREVIOUS era" comparative predicate class forces the scorer to capture key economic levers at each era boundary, so this is a **NEW persisted-state field on `FullGameSnapshot`** (`game.eraSnapshots?: Record<EraBand, {economicLevers…}>` or an array) + a `repair()` default + `DB_VERSION`/migration discipline (debt #4); it CANNOT read only current state. **(ii) the predicate evaluator** — each benchmark is a state predicate over policy-active / action-issued / per-nation-relations-threshold / war-state-flag / count / identity-record / era-comparative classes; this is a **CONSUMER of the #221 content-primitive surface** (US Bank, TVA, Fugitive Slave Act, Civil Rights Act 1964 must be addressable objects — debt #110/#98) so the scorer cannot precede a minimal #221 primitive registry. **(iii) the end-of-era scoring pass** — fires at the `advanceEra` boundary (debt #5 / K3 per-era point-banking #68), feeds the #102 dual scoreboard (per-era winner + cumulative); **weights are TBD** ("yet to determine how much these score/penalize") and the **OLD benchmark point system is RETAINED, only slightly amended** (this SUPPLEMENTS, does not replace). **★ DETERMINISM:** the scoring pass MUST be deterministic — any tie-break/roll routes through `rng.ts`, NEVER `Math.random` (K0); a benchmark scorer that desyncs breaks replay/MP. **★ HARD GATE: needs the 13/15-era band model to exist first** (debt #116 era-table reconcile + K3/K4 `advanceEra` + era-content registry) — there is no boundary to checkpoint at until the band model lands. **DATA-FIDELITY FLAG:** the CSV export FLATTENED per-era grouping (13 headers then a flat run) → re-source per-era benchmark membership from the original sheet before authoring. **K-TIER-GATED EPIC: the dataset+evaluator+archive = M-L, GATED on K3/K4 + #116 + a minimal #221; a thin slice (the snapshot-archive field + a stub scorer over a few hardcoded predicates) could land WITH K3 to de-risk, but the full 260-predicate model is post-band-model.** Pairs #68/#102/#206/#92/#221/#5. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §27.2.2, §27.1.2, §30.27. |
| 116 | **★★ FOUR AUTHORITATIVE ERA TABLES (15-era rulebook · 14-era half-term · 13-era `benchmarkupd` · ★ batch-42 15-era `benchmarks`-origin) MUST BE RECONCILED before any era-band code — a SEQUENCING BLOCKER for K3/K4 (batch 37 `benchmarkupd` 217d1977 + batch-31 `rulebook` §A + batch-38 `4accc8a8` half-term table + ★ batch-42 `f012e5cc` benchmarks-origin; VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped; sharpens #206/#92; = gap #206)** | **VERIFIED the shipped enum is 4 values** (`Era = 'independence'\|'federalism'\|'nationalism'\|'modern'`, `types.ts:1337`); era trigger predicates are pure calendar arithmetic (`isDraftYear`/`isPresidentialYear` = `year % 4`, `isElectionYear` = `year % 2`, `phases.ts:53-58`); `shouldRunPhase` keys off `year` (`phases.ts:62+`). The forum now has **THREE non-identical authoritative era tables**: (a) the batch-31 **15-era** `rulebook` band-label calendar (date-triggered LABELS / point-bank boundaries; **includes a standalone Era-of-the-Future** + Gilded-Age/Progressivism as separate 1892 triggers; game-mechanics §27.1.2); (b) ★ the **batch-38 `4accc8a8` 14-era table** — the FIRST with **contiguous per-half-term spans** tiling 1772→present with no gaps (Independence 1772–1787 … Populism 2012–present; **NO standalone Future row**, timeline ends at the present), plus a per-era scripted-event total (sum = 1,335 events); (c) the batch-37 **13-era** benchmark table (Federalism 1788 → Populism 2012-2024, EXCLUDES Independence + Era-of-the-Future); **★ batch-42 adds a (d) FOURTH cardinality — the Dec-2021 `benchmarks` (`f012e5cc`) ORIGIN table is a 15-era enumeration** (Independence + 13 + Future as no-benchmark bookends) that MATCHES the 15-rulebook label set (NOT the 13-era `benchmarkupd` it later expanded into); it lists eras as benchmark headers only (NO year ranges → no new boundary dates), so it adds NO new dates but is independent corroboration that the designer's mental era model is the **15-label set, not the shipped 4-enum** (the `benchmarkupd`/#253 dataset is the SAME system later trimmed to a 13-era "main historical run"). Of the 13 benchmark names, **only Federalism + Nationalism map to a shipped enum member**; the 14-era table likewise collides with the 4-enum (it splits the shipped coarse `nationalism` into Republicanism/Democracy/Manifest-Destiny/Nationalism/Gilded-Age and pins **Nationalism to only 1856–1867**, the shortest band — strong evidence the 4-enum over-collapses 1800–1868). | **The 14-era half-term table is, incidentally, the MOST implementation-ready year-spine in the KB (contiguous half-term boundaries land on the even/odd 2-year-turn pair — the engine buckets content by HALF-TERM, not single year), but it DISAGREES on both cardinality (15 vs 14 vs 13) and boundaries (e.g. does Independence start 1772 [half-term table] or label at 1774 [rulebook/benchmark]? is there a standalone Future band [rulebook YES / half-term+benchmark NO]?).** This corroborates #206/#92's direction (a TWO-LEVEL content-band DATA model, NOT a growing hardcoded enum — debt #5 already rules "do NOT grow the enum per band"). **★ THE BLOCKER:** K3 (`advanceEra` + `era.advanceWhen` conditions) and K4 (era-content registry `{bills, eraEvents, draftees, biasTable, advanceWhen}` keyed on `eraBand`) BOTH need ONE canonical band-label vocabulary + boundary set as their key space — building either against an un-reconciled table means authoring era content twice. This is a **DESIGN-RULING prerequisite** (a human/PM reconcile of the **four tables [15-rulebook / 14-half-term / 13-`benchmarkupd` / 15-`benchmarks`-origin] + the playtest-observed bands** into one `EraBand` enum/table — the 14-era half-term spine is the cleanest partition candidate; the TWO 15-era tables [rulebook + benchmarks-origin] are the only ones with an explicit Future row and agree on the 15-label vocabulary), not an engineering task — but it gates the engineering. The #253 benchmark scorer (debt #115) is downstream of it (the scorer checkpoints AT these boundaries). **NO new build surface of its own — it is a SEQUENCING/AUTHORITY blocker that must resolve before K3/K4 era-content authoring begins; the engine cost is already inside K3/K4/debt #5.** Pairs #206/#92/#5/#115/#101/#109; cross-ref gap #206. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §27.1.2, §27.2.2, §30.27, §30.28. |

| 120 | **★★★ #258 PREDICATE / CONDITIONAL-AVAILABILITY FIELD on the content primitives — the schema KEYSTONE the whole content engine (#221/#237/#248) sits on; a LOW-RISK reuse-and-extend of the SHIPPED `Predicate`/`evalPredicate` machinery (batch 38, `9bd91ee2`-civil-rights + `c33d07b8`-agriculture, VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, partially-built primitive, 0% wired to content)** | **★ THE machinery PARTLY EXISTS.** `src/types.ts:1487-1504` ships a serializable `Predicate` tree (`all/any/not`, `yearAtLeast/atMost`, `eventCompleted`, `eventChose`, `meterAtLeast/atMost`, `interestAtLeast`, `diplomacyAtLeast`, `warActive`, `warOutcome`, `stateAdmitted`, `officeControlledByPlayer`, `rosterHasSkill`, `flag` — 16 variants) with a pure recursive `evalPredicate(snap, pred)` (`eraGraph.ts:12-47`). **BUT it is wired ONLY to the 2.4.3 era-event graph** (consumed at `selectEraGraphNode` `eraGraph.ts:114` `n.precondition`; `labels.ts:183/196`). `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has the 4-value `committee` and **NO `subtype`, NO prereq/condition/precondition field**; Pres-Action / Gov-Action primitives don't exist (debt #97/#98/#110). So `evalPredicate` exists but NOTHING reads it for content gating. | **The Civil-Rights + Agriculture genres are the clearest evidence yet that the content schema needs a game-STATE-keyed (not calendar-keyed) availability predicate** — the `Preq:` clause (≈80 CR rows + the Agriculture prereq CHAINS): no-policy-baseline, prior-event-occurred, state-condition-flag, institution-exists, prior-legislation-active (compound AND), 3rd-party-now-major, geographic/per-state, scenario-mode (Constitutional-Convention-Only), tech/future-state. **★ THE BUILD (reuse-and-extend, two parts): (1)** lift the SHIPPED `Predicate` type onto ALL FOUR content primitives (Legis-Prop/Pres-Action/Gov-Action/Scripted-Event) as an optional `precondition?: Predicate` field — exactly the era-event shape — and gate availability through the SAME `evalPredicate`; **(2)** extend the predicate VOCABULARY with the ~9 new classes the era-event enum can't express: `stateFlag` (policy/world flag), `priorEvent` (already ≈`eventCompleted`, reuse), `institutionExists` (Dept/agency), `legislationActive` (single + the compound-AND read via the existing `all`), `thirdPartyMajor`, `geoPerState`/scenario, `scenarioMode`, `techState`, `noPolicyBaseline` (the `*-Ind-Default` "if nothing active" read). Most of these are thin additions to the `Predicate` union + one `evalPredicate` arm each — the engine ALREADY recurses `all/any/not`. **★ THIS IS THE FOUNDATIONAL SCHEMA PRIMITIVE for #221 (registry) / #237 (genres) / #248 (subtype): the prereq field belongs IN the content-primitive schema, and the predicate vocabulary is shared by every genre.** Because the type + interpreter already exist and are battle-tested on the era-event graph, this is **LOW-RISK** — extend, don't invent. **S-M (the field-add + ~9 vocab arms are S; auditing every era-event call site for regressions is the M part).
