| 121 | **★★ #259 PARTY-BRANCH ROSTER + prereq'd FORMATION-EVENT data model — party identity TRANSFORMS over a campaign (Federalist→…→branch), DISTINCT from the cosmetic faction-rename (#40/#78); a NEW engine system riding on #258/#221 (batch 38, `7d0dfeec`-political-parties, VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped)** | **VERIFIED the shipped `Party` is a FLAT 2-value record.** `Party = { id: PartyId; name; leaderId?; color }` (`types.ts:1306-1311`) keyed to exactly two fixed `PartyId`s (`'BLUE' \| 'RED'`, `types.ts:3`). **NO party-branch type, NO branch enum, NO formation-event type, NO prereq fields, NO per-coalition roster data** (grep `partyFormation\|partyBranch\|coalition` = ZERO; no roster file in `src/data/`). A party can only have its single `name` string mutated. **The #221 scripted-event primitive the formation triggers ride on is itself 0% shipped** (debt #97/#98/#110), so the trigger mechanism doesn't exist either. | **vcczar (POST 1): he added scripted events that CREATE new parties, and "all party formations, aside from the first for each party, has requirements that must be met"** — i.e. a campaign moves through a SEQUENCE of branch identities within each fixed coalition, a prereq'd transformation GRAPH, not a flat list. The DATA model touches: **(a)** the `Party` shape (or a sibling `PartyBranch` entity) — a current-branch pointer + branch metadata (platform blurb, BLUE/RED/either assignment); **(b)** a per-coalition branch ROSTER (full Blue + Red lists + the cross-coalition Era-of-Future parties Technocratic/Humanitarian/Earth-First/[Universe-TBD], band-gated to the terminal era — which has NO enum to attach to, debt #116/gap #206); **(c)** party-formation events as **#221 scripted-event payloads carrying a `precondition: Predicate`** (debt #120/#258) — "first formation unconditional, every formation after is gated" + tunable per-branch longevity knobs (the Federalist/Jeffersonian-Republican amendment). **★ CONTRAST the cosmetic path (do NOT conflate):** the engine DOES model era-specific party NAMES + a deterministic faction-rename trigger (#40/#78 — e.g. Whig→"Conservative Party", gated on leader-traits/win-streaks) — but that is relabeling within the two fixed coalitions, NOT formation of a new branch identity with its own platform unlocked by a prereq'd event. This thread's roster + prereq graph is the SUPERSET that #40's rename is a thin shadow of. **★ SIZE M, POST-#258/#221:** the formation-event payload reuses the #221 registry + the #120/#258 precondition field; the genuinely-new surface is the branch entity + the curated roster DATA + a "current branch" pointer the election/identity UI reads. NOT a keystone; rides the content-engine track. Open Qs: Era-of-Future "Universe/space-government" party NAME unsettled; Reform Party BLUE-or-RED uncommitted; several historical renames unruled. Pairs #221/#258/#40/#78/#116/#206. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §7.5, §30.28. |
| 122 | **★★ #260 EVENT→POPULATION-SHIFT + multi-turn SPATIAL-SPREAD — scripted events that mutate per-state demographics over MULTIPLE TURNS, strictly richer than the fire-once `EraEvent`; a NEW engine surface feeding apportionment/lean (batch 38, `c33d07b8`-agriculture, VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped). Cross-ref #247.** | **VERIFIED the shipped event model is fire-once with NO per-state propagation.** `EraEvent` (`types.ts:1466-1482`) is a single year-windowed shape (responses, decider, `resolved`, `chosenResponseId`); its `postEffects` union (`:1478`) has NO population/demographic mutator. **No per-state population/demographic field exists** — `State` (`types.ts:1316-1335`) has only the static `bias: number` scalar (`:1324`); the ONLY dynamic state-lean levers are the ~30%/term governor drift (`phaseRunners.ts:3388-3389`) and a region-keyed anytime-event `stateBias` effect **clamped ±1, un-time-boxed** (`phaseRunners.ts:2608-2616`). No event-spread, no expiry/counter-event, no infestation/effect state, no event→population mechanism anywhere. | **The Boll-Weevil exemplar (`c33d07b8` POST 5/6): a geographic, MULTI-TURN SPREAD with PER-STATE infestation state** (Mexico→TX 1896 → AR/LA 1900 → MS/TN 1904 → AL/GA 1908 → FL/SC 1912 → NC/VA 1916), **variant spread-rates** (slow/never; catastrophic 2-yr-instead-of-4), a later **EXPIRING counter-event** (DDT ~1950 that STOPS WORKING by 1960 on resistance), all DRIVING a South→North/West **population shift** (the Great Migration). Plus the Potato-Blight→immigration± variants (event MAGNITUDE modulates inflow). **★ THE NEW SURFACE (three layers, none shipped): (1)** a per-state infestation/effect STATE field + a multi-turn propagation pass (deterministic via `rng.ts`, never `Math.random`); **(2)** an `EraEvent` POPULATION/demographic effect (event→state-population/EV mover) — the generic case feeds apportionment + lean; **(3)** a time-boxed/expiring counter-event hook. **★ RELATION TO #247 (debt #109):** #247 already specs an EVENT-keyed (not year-keyed) state-lean realignment over the static `bias`, but its current scope (decade-base + event-keyed ±1 region modifier) does NOT spell out a population/EV mover OR multi-turn spatial propagation — #260 is the ENRICHED requirement. **★ SIZE M, POST-#258/#221:** the per-state state + spread pass is the new code; the event-population effect folds into the #247/debt-#109 realignment work, not the static `bias`. NOT a keystone. Pairs #247/#221/#258/§28.9-census. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §14.1.3.e, §11.4.1 (#247), §30.28. |
| 123 | **★ #261 PER-HALF-TERM SCRIPTED-EVENT FIRING BUDGET — the per-half-term event count is itself a content BUDGET the engine should enforce/randomize within a range; COUPLES to the #206/debt-#116 era-table decision (batch 38, `4accc8a8`-historical-scripted-events-by-half-term; DESIGNED, 0% shipped)** | **VERIFIED no firing-budget machinery.** The shipped era-event firing is the per-turn graph walker `selectEraGraphNode` (`eraGraph.ts:107-142`): a ONE-PER-TURN cap (returns the already-queued unresolved event) + `coreSpine` immediate-fire + a probabilistic `fireChance`/`historyPressure` roll (`ERA_GRAPH_RULES`); there is NO per-half-term count BUDGET, no per-band catalog-size target, no randomized N-per-turn range. The Scripted-Event content pool the budget governs is part of the 0%-shipped #221. | **vcczar's per-era half-term audit (`4accc8a8`) names a target band ~6–11 events/half-term and floats "a range of 5–15 for Scripted Events… or something to that effect" (POST 1)** — i.e. the scripted-event count per half-term is a BUDGET the engine should bound, not just a static authored list. The measured baseline (the §1 table, 1,335 events across 14 eras) is the current pool size; the outliers (Independence 1780–83 = 3 each; Gilded-Age 1890–91 = 26 spike; Populism 2022–23 = 2) are the work-items. **★ COUPLING: a per-half-term bucket needs canonical era BANDS first** — the half-term boundaries are the 14-era table's spans, so this DEPENDS on the #206/debt-#116 era-table reconcile (which band-vocabulary owns the half-term spine). **★ OPEN Q (human call): is "5–15" an AUTHORED-POOL floor/ceiling per era, or a RUNTIME cap on how many fire per turn (a budget the engine randomizes within)?** POST 1 is ambiguous. Also note the recent-tail shortfall (Neocons/Terror/Populism thin) is a KNOWN content-SUPPLY hole ("we don't know what'll be famous yet"), not a balance bug. **★ SIZE S as an engine knob (a per-band budget config + a randomized-within-range firing gate on the #221 pool) IF the runtime-cap reading wins; trivial if it's purely an authoring target. GATED on debt #116 + #221.** Pairs #221/#206/#116/#92. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §30.28. |
| 124 | **★ #45 WAR-DECLARATION prep-gated layer (party-pref roll + instigator-attribution + prep-gated war LIMIT + per-theater officers) — explicitly POST-LAUNCH; PLUS the batch-38 ANTI-EXPLOIT GUARDS (cabinet-crisis appointee #172; career-track ceiling #163; holdover-office tenure #66/#170; ★ Kingmaker→Kingmaker ALREADY SHIPPED) (batch 38, `a852d451`-exploits; VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, war-layer 0% shipped)** | **VERIFIED.** (war) NO Military-Preparedness VARIABLE exists — only a `military` METER label (`Meter.tsx:6`); the shipped war system is RevolutionaryWar-specific (`revolutionaryWar.ts`), records `navalWins/navalLosses` (`:202`) but the win-check reads ground tallies only (`:254-264`, = DH-79); NO `declareWar`, no war-count cap, no per-theater officer assignment; `OfficeType` (`types.ts:1111`) has NO Fed-Chair/FBI/CIA/DNI. (cabinet) `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2224`) sets `cabinet[seat]=pick.id` (`:2191`) + logs "confirmed" (`:2198`) with **NO Senate vote / opposition roll / Iron-Fist gate** (= debt #24/#38). (career) the track skill block (`phaseRunners.ts:305-318`) gates on `p.skills[k] < 5`, so tracks CAN reach 5 today; `addSkillPoint` (`abilities.ts:25-30`) caps at 5 globally; `CAREER_ODDS.skill = 0.5` is a FLAT chance (`types.ts:230`) with **NO diminishing-by-current-level**. (holdover) `cabinetSeatsForYear` is year-keyed/fixed (`types.ts:1196-1208`); no term-length/holdover-default/early-replace-risk logic. (kingmaker) ★ `protegeCandidates` (`phaseRunners.ts:1275-1293`) ALREADY excludes Kingmaker protégés at `:1286` (`if (c.traits.includes('Kingmaker')) return false`) on BOTH the player (`assignProtege` `:1304`) and CPU auto-assign (`:1483-1499`) paths. | **★ #45 WAR-DECLARATION (POST-LAUNCH — `a852d451` is the Mar-2022 DESIGN ORIGIN, predating the war-score combat side of #45):** a sliding-scale party-preference roll keyed to a 9-level Military-Preparedness meter at the moment a war is DECLARED (well-prepared war can BOOST your party; only unprepared war reliably hurts — sliding scale AGREED by vcczar over fixed −300/−100 numbers); instigator-FACTION attribution (eats the loss-penalty even under a LATER president; BARRED from winning on game-over); a prep-gated SIMULTANEOUS-WAR LIMIT (0/1/2/3 by prep); per-theater General/Admiral assignment (cross-ref DH-79 + the military-leadership gap #56). This needs a Military-Preparedness VARIABLE + a generic `War` model first → defer to the post-launch #45/#56 war epic (debt #105). **★ ANTI-EXPLOIT GUARDS (small QW-sized hardening, can land INDEPENDENTLY): (a)** cabinet-CRISIS appointee guard (gap #172) — an Admin-1/2 officer appointed to a Lingering-Phase crisis → escalating point loss + party-pref/enthusiasm drops + Controversial-chance, **and is NOT Iron-Fist-auto-confirmable** (an anti-exploit on the confirmation path; SAME site as debt #24/#38 — fold into the confirmation-vote build); **(b)** career-track CEILING (gap #163) — no skill reaches 5 via tracks (cap track gains at 4) + diminishing-returns-by-level gain chance (kill "super commanders") → change the `:314`/`:1269` `< 5` gate to `< 5/<4 by source` + make `CAREER_ODDS.skill` level-contingent; **(c)** holdover-office TENURE (gap #66/#170) — Fed-Chair 4-yr renewable / FBI 10-yr renewable / CIA-DNI indefinite-at-pleasure, incumbents carry over by default (no auto-re-nominate), removing-early → 10% econ-stab/dom-stab/mil-prep hit, **no trait grants permanence** (closes the Iron-Fist-tenure exploit) — but these OFFICES must EXIST first (gap #66/#170, not in `OfficeType`); **(d) ★ Kingmaker→Kingmaker (gap #128/#210) is ALREADY SHIPPED** (`:1286`) — NO build needed; the digest's "no row records this restriction / reverses a chain-reward rule" is **CONTRADICTED by the code** (the only open Kingmaker item is the "National Kingmaker" trait-scope Q). **★ SIZE: war-layer = post-launch (rides #45/#56/E3); guards (a)/(b) = S each; (c) = S BUT gated on the offices existing (#66/#170); (d) = DONE.** Pairs #45/#56/#172/#199/#77/#163/#86/#66/#170/#128/#210/DH-79. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §21.1.B (#45), §9.3.11(13), §5.4 (#163), §9.3.6, §6.5.w, §30.28. |
| 125 | **★★ #264 COUP / INSURRECTION PROCEDURE subsystem — a LARGE, DECISION-GATED new system (two coup types + a "Following the Coup" consolidation package + countercoup + treason trials); the OPEN PRODUCT QUESTION (full two-type subsystem vs. a single Era-of-Populism scripted event) GATES the build (batch 39, `4c14847b`-jan-6-insurrection; VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, ~0% shipped — coups are vanishingly rare)** | **★ VERIFIED ~0% BUILT.** grep `coup\|treason\|insurrection\|loyalist\|countercoup` across `src/` = ZERO engine matches (only UI flavor: a "Loyalist-leaning" ideology band `ContinentalCongressBuilderPage.tsx:49`; the `Insurrection` DomStab meter LABEL `Meter.tsx:17`; a 1772 "loyalist reabsorption" terminal-ending string). The ONLY coup-adjacent hook is **`EraEvent.triggersGameEnd?`** (`types.ts:1476`), a boolean consumed once at `phaseRunners.ts:2871` (`if (event.triggersGameEnd && !snap.game.gameEnded)`) → sets `game.gameEnded` and STOPS — a one-shot LOSS flag, **NOT a coup PROCEDURE**. **★ NO impeachment plumbing exists either** — grep `impeach` = ZERO matches anywhere in `src/`. The DomStab/EconStab/Military METERS ship (`NationalMeters`; `Meter.tsx` labels incl. `Insurrection`), but there is **NO coup-GATE on them** (no `DomStab≤worst AND MilPrep≤slightly AND Economy≤Great-Recession + trait` predicate firing a coup), **no install-loyalists appoint, no treason-trial removal+replace→supermajority, no +3-federal-election buff, no countercoup→reinstate-actual-winner, no Lingering→coup→Legislation turn-loop hook, no CPU coup-odds-by-era curve.** | **The design source (`4c14847b`, Nov 2022) defines the WHOLE subsystem the build lacks: (A) Standard Coup = opposition-leader CHOICE, EVENT-TRIGGERED** (Authoritarian-overthrow scripted event / "Newburgh Conspiracy is Real" / hardened "Attempted Coup" general event; gated DomStab-worst + MilPrep<slightly-prepared + a Controversial/Leadership trait gate); **(B) Populist Coup = incumbent-President CHOICE** (the Jan-6 analogue — an incumbent who LOST re-election launching to stay in power; DomStab worse-than-neutral + MilPrep worse-than-slightly + President-trait gate). **"FOLLOWING THE COUP" consolidation package (designed EXTREME):** install loyalists (President appoints the winner of EACH House+Senate seat that cycle); **treason trials** for opposition not up for reelection (Senate/military/SCOTUS) → losers removed-from-game + replaced → supermajority in all branches; +3 in ALL federal elections for the President's tenure (or ~10 yrs) with opposition challengers who lose removed-from-game; + DomStab + foreign-relations penalties. **Countercoup ruling:** in a Populist Coup the opposition leader launches a countercoup; if it succeeds, the **ACTUAL Presidential-election WINNER is reinstated (NOT the countercoup launcher)** + added to the treason/impeachment rolls (worked example: 2020 → Trump coup → Pelosi countercoup → BIDEN reinstated). **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: this rides EVERY foundational system at once** — the DomStab/EconStab/MilPrep meters (gap #88/#92, mostly shipped) for the GATE; the #221 scripted-event registry (debt #97/#98, 0% shipped) for the Standard-Coup trigger; the #258 predicate field (debt #120) for the multi-meter+trait precondition; **and a treason-trial removal/replacement mechanic that would REUSE impeachment plumbing — which DOES NOT EXIST (DH-29..DH-35 are the broken-impeachment holes; the treason trial = a coup-driven removal+appoint, so impeachment must be authored/built FIRST or the two co-build).** Plus a NEW turn-loop hook (coup resolves AFTER Lingering, BEFORE Legislation, so a treason trial runs the same turn). **★ THE OPEN PRODUCT QUESTION GATES THE BUILD: full two-type subsystem vs. a single lightweight Era-of-Populism scripted event** — vcczar floated scrapping it (POST 1); both designers note coups are rare-to-DEAD (no Populist-eligible President in ANY playtest; the only near-miss, Benedict Arnold, declined; CPU odds cap ≤25%; the only coup ever to FIRE in the whole corpus was an Autocratic Coup in `terror2000`). **DECIDE FULL-VS-SCRAP FIRST.** **★ SIZE L (or XS as a single scripted event); POST-LAUNCH / DECISION-GATED — do NOT schedule near-term.** Rides #88/#92 (meters) + #221 (scripted events) + #258 (predicate) + impeachment (DH-29..DH-35). Pairs #88/#92/#221/#258/#207/#188/DH-29..DH-35. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §26.4.1 (coup), §30.29; cross-ref gap #264. |
| 126 | **★★ #263 SETTINGS / NEW-GAME OPTIONS system — a start-of-game options screen + per-toggle ENGINE PLUMBING that gates rules; the sanctioned channel for ahistorical/"unrealistic" play and the home of #182's escape hatch; a self-contained UI+plumbing layer that can land INDEPENDENTLY (batch 39, `f735601c`-rules-update; VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped as a framework)** | **★ VERIFIED the FRAMEWORK is absent (a settings PAGE exists, but it is NOT this).** `SettingsPage.tsx` (registered `registry.ts:98`) is **save-management + draft-CSV-import + about ONLY** — Export/Import/Reset save, custom-draft-class CSV, no rule toggles. **`startNewGame(factionId, scenarioId)`** (`GameContext.tsx:264`) takes ONLY a faction + a `'1772'\|'1856'` scenario and calls `build1772Scenario`/`build1856Scenario` (`:267`) — the two scenarios are **FIXED data files**, no options object threaded through. **`GameState` (`types.ts:1558-1640`) has NO `settings`/`options`/toggles field** (grep `settings?\|options?\|gameOptions\|toggles` on the type = ZERO). So every canonical toggle has no UI and no engine home. | **The design source (`f735601c`, Aug 2021) is the ORIGIN of an umbrella options model — vcczar's framing: "no more overhauls," changes accepted ONLY as game settings, game stays ≥50% realistic.** Each canonical toggle gates a specific rule: **start-date randomization** (you never know when a politician arrives — vs the fixed `draftYear` = nearest mult-of-4 to `birthYear+25`); **relocation restriction OFF** (move to any state — removes the home-state guard, `Politician.lastRelocationAttemptYear`/`RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN`); **rookie-trait assignment OFF** (rookies enter trait-less); **suffrage-draft** (women/minorities draftable pre-suffrage — gap #238); **faction-by-party vs draft-anyone** (toggles the party-affiliation draft constraint — gap #171/#4); **what-if pool** (keep ahistorical figures — gap #24/#115); **ability-requirement bypass / "unrealistic mode"** (disables the command/admin office-eligibility gates — vcczar's ONLY concession on #182, see debt #62/§ command-gate). **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: a clean two-layer build — (1)** a `GameState.settings?: GameSettings` field (additive; `repair()` backfills defaults so old saves load) threaded from a new-game options screen through `startNewGame`; **(2)** each consumer reads its flag at the relevant gate (draft eligibility, relocation guard, rookie-gen, suffrage filter, faction-draft filter, what-if pool inclusion, ability-gate). **It is SELF-CONTAINED** — it does NOT block the content engine and the content engine does not block it — so it can land **independently** whenever the unrealistic-mode escape (#182) or the suffrage/what-if toggles are wanted. **★ SIZE M (the screen + the field + ~7 read-site guards); INDEPENDENT, can land any time.** Pairs #182/#171/#238/#24/#115/#4/#213. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §29.1.1 (settings); cross-ref gap #263. |
| 127 | **★ #66 OFFICE-LIFECYCLE data model (create / split / elevate / revert / merge / abolish + undeletable flag + Minister-per-nation) — the cabinet is NOT a fixed seat list but a lifecycle managed by content rows; a concrete DATA-MODEL delta the content engine needs; size M, on the content-engine track (batch 39, `fa452d38`-civil-service SHARPENS #66 with the verb set; VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped)** | **★ VERIFIED.** `cabinetSeatsForYear(year)` (`types.ts:1196-1208`) is a HARDCODED year-keyed function returning a fixed `OfficeType[]` (≤7 seats: State/Treasury/War, +Navy≥1798, +AG, +Interior≥1849, +PMG≥1829). **`OfficeType` (`types.ts:1111-1134`) is a CLOSED 23-value union** — NO Commerce/Justice/Energy/HEW/HUD/Transportation/DHS/etc.; **a single generic `Ambassador`, NO Minister-per-nation type; NO "department" entity as data; NO create/split/merge/abolish/elevate/revert machinery; NO undeletable flag.** Cabinet seats are filled by `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2224`) against this fixed list. | **Civil-Service (`fa452d38`) makes the office/department LIFECYCLE the HEADLINE content — the cabinet GROWS well beyond 7 (~20 departments/offices creatable by law) via a 6-verb state machine: create (Dept of Justice/Navy/Energy/…); split (Commerce&Labor→2; HEW→2); elevate-to-cabinet (PMG; Dept of VA); revert-to-independent-agency (Postal); merge (War+Navy→Defense); abolish = REPEAL the creating law/action — with vcczar's "some can't be removed" UNDELETABLE flag. Plus Minister-per-nation office typing (today only generic `Ambassador`) and the OPM→confirmation-buff / CoS-in-conjunction-with-Key-Advisor adds. **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: the build replaces the hardcoded `cabinetSeatsForYear` with a DATA-DRIVEN office registry** — a `departments`/`offices` entity carried on `GameState` (additive; `repair()` seeds the era-appropriate baseline) that content rows mutate via the 6 lifecycle verbs, each office carrying an `undeletable?` flag + a `createdByRowId` back-pointer (abolish = check repealability). The verbs are #221 content-primitive effects (Legis-Prop/Pres-Action), so this **rides the #258 predicate field + the #221 registry** (the lifecycle rows are prereq-gated: "parent dept exists," "War AND Navy exist → merge"). **NOT a keystone; a content-engine-track DATA delta.** **★ SIZE M (the office registry + 6 verb handlers + the undeletable flag + Minister typing; the seat-fill consumer rewrites to read the registry).** Pairs #221/#258/#32/#33/#43/#170. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §24.6 (office lifecycle); cross-ref gap #66. |
| 128 | **★ #107 DIPLOMACY data-model delta — grow the flat per-nation relation SCALAR into per-nation `{relations, recognized?, aidLevel, trade/embargo}` + per-nation war (today war is a global `string[]`); a concrete DATA-MODEL delta the content engine needs; size M, on the content-engine track (batch 39, `b8aecb83`-diplomacy; VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped)** | **★ VERIFIED the shipped state is a flat SCALAR.** `GameState.diplomacy: Record<string, number>` (`types.ts:1574`), clamped −5..+5; war is a **separate GLOBAL** `wars: string[]` (`types.ts:1575`), NOT per-nation. **NO recognition flag, NO aid level, NO per-nation at-war, NO per-nation trade/embargo status.** The shipped phase `runPhase_2_7_1_Diplomacy` (`phaseRunners.ts:3585-3591`, turn-loop phase 2.7.1) only nudges each nation's relation scalar by ±0.5 on a 20% roll; SoS admin adds a small drift. **No Legis-Prop / Pres-Action diplomacy catalog is wired** — the authored doctrines/embargoes/recognitions have no engine home. There IS a `Predicate` arm `diplomacyAtLeast: {nation, value}` (`types.ts:1498`) and an `applyEffect` `diplomacy: {nation, delta}` channel, but both read/write only the flat scalar. | **Diplomacy (`b8aecb83`, ~55 rows) is the strongest case yet that the data model must track per-foreign-nation STATE: at-war (per nation), relations level (thresholded scalar), recognition flag, aid level, trade/embargo status — the `Req:` clauses key off all of these (`At War with this country`; `Relations worse than neutral`; the POST-6 Recognize↔Do-Not-Recognize + 4-way Aid/Increase/Decrease/Withdraw/End sets per nation). **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: grow `diplomacy` from `Record<string, number>` into `Record<string, NationDiplomacy>`** where `NationDiplomacy = { relations: number; recognized?: boolean; aidLevel?: number; trade?: 'open'|'embargoed'; atWar?: boolean }` (additive shape; `repair()` migrates the old scalar → `{relations: n}` so saves load — note this is the FIRST repair that RESHAPES a field rather than backfilling, a small migration-discipline precedent). This **widens the #258 predicate vocabulary** with per-nation-state + war-outcome-flag classes (WWII-won, any-WW-active, WWI-won) the era-event enum can't express, and the doctrines/embargoes/recognitions become #221 Pres-Action/Legis-Prop rows. **NOT a keystone; a content-engine-track DATA delta** (boundary Q: Ministers-per-nation overlaps #66's office typing; embargoes "arguably Trade"). **★ SIZE M (the field reshape + repair migration + the predicate-vocab arms + the per-nation effect channel; the 2.7.1 phase rewrites to drift `relations` within the struct).** Pairs #258/#221/#107/#66/§13.3.4. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §13.3.4 (diplomacy); cross-ref gap #107. |
| 129 | **★ #258 PREDICATE-VOCAB widening (batch 39 = 2nd + 3rd witnesses: Civil Service + Diplomacy) — adds new predicate CLASSES the era-event enum can't express; #258 REMAINS the content-engine keystone (see debt #120), this row records the vocab deltas only (batch 39, `fa452d38`-civil-service + `b8aecb83`-diplomacy; VERIFIED against code; the FIELD is debt #120)** | **VERIFIED (same as debt #120).** The shipped `Predicate` (`types.ts:1487-1504`, 16 variants) + pure `evalPredicate` (`eraGraph.ts:12-47`) are wired ONLY to the 2.4.3 era-event graph; `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has NO `subtype`/precondition. This row does NOT re-state the field-add (that is debt #120 / the keystone) — it logs the NEW predicate CLASSES these two genres demand. | **Civil Service + Diplomacy are the SECOND and THIRD strong witnesses for #258 (after batch-38 Civil-Rights + Agriculture), widening the vocabulary the field must support: institution-exists (Dept-X exists / National Security Act active); multi-office COMPOUND (Sec-of-War AND Sec-of-Navy → merge — reads via the existing `all`); **cross-primitive mutual-exclusion** ("Not Already Created via Legis Prop / via Pres Action" — EOP/CEA can be created by EITHER an L- or a P- row, and one path disables the other → the predicate must reference "was this office created, by ANY primitive"); scenario-mode (Constitutional-Convention-Only); **per-nation diplomatic-state** (Israel Recognized; Trade Open with China/Japan; At War with this country; Relations worse than neutral); **war-outcome flags** (WWII won by Allies / any-WW-active / WWI won + US involved); 3rd-party-status (Authoritarian/Theocratic/Communist/Fascist now major); anti-prereq (merit-law NOT active → gates Spoils System). **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: these are thin additions to the `Predicate` union + one `evalPredicate` arm each (the engine already recurses `all/any/not`), BUT two are notable: (i)** the per-nation diplomatic-state classes DEPEND on the #107 data-model delta (debt #128) landing first (you can't query `recognized?`/`atWar?` until the field exists); **(ii)** the cross-primitive mutual-exclusion class needs a "created-by-any-primitive" office-state read that DEPENDS on the #66 office registry (debt #127). So #258's vocab now has explicit DEPENDENCIES on the two data-model deltas this batch. **#258 (the FIELD) remains THE content-engine keystone — schedule WITH/just-ahead of K4 (debt #120); this batch's witnesses confirm and widen it, no re-sequence.** **★ SIZE: the vocab arms are S (folds into the debt-#120 build); the per-nation + office-state arms are gated on debt #128/#127.** Pairs #221/#237/#248/#66/#107/#258/debt-#120. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §14.1.3.d, §14.1.3.f, §13.3.4; cross-ref gap #258. |
| 130 | **★★ #266 "METER-AT-FLOOR = NO DETERRENT" systemic balance flaw — a penalty against an already-floored meter is a SILENT NO-OP; the SMALL, high-value balance fix that can land EARLY and INDEPENDENTLY (batch 40, `a2312dd2`-foreign-affairs/treaty; VERIFIED against code; the FLAW *is* the shipped behavior)** | **★ VERIFIED — the flaw is the build.** All 7 `NationalMeters` (`types.ts:1399-1407`: revenue/economic/military/domestic/honest/quality/planet) are bare numbers, and EVERY application path clamps them to **`[-5, 5]`** via `clamp(n,min,max)=Math.max(min,Math.min(max,n))` (`rng.ts:49-51`): era-event/legislation effects `applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3213` `clamp(meters[k]+v,-5,5)`; domesticStability `:3219`), anytime-events (`:2588-2591` via `ANYTIME_EVENTS_RULES.meterClampLow/High`), era-event modulation (`:2991`), Pres-action stub (`:3637-3640`). So a `-1` against a meter sitting at `-5` is absorbed by the clamp and registers **nothing** — `#266` confirmed real, shipped, game-wide. **★ CRITICAL CORRECTION to the digest's fix-attachment claim:** the digest (and #266/#88) assumes a *multi-year game-over COUNTDOWN clock* exists that Largo's "8yr→6yr" fix would modify — **it does NOT.** `game.gameEnded` (`types.ts:1635`) is a one-shot `{year,reason,templateId}` set INSTANTLY by `event.triggersGameEnd` (`phaseRunners.ts:2871`); there is NO meter-driven loss ROLL and NO countdown clock in `src/` (debt #28 / #88 = the FIRST proven loss state is still UNBUILT). So the "shorten-the-countdown" fix has no clock to attach to *yet* — it must be built ALONGSIDE the #88 meter-driven game-over (debt #28), OR ship as the alternative **spill-over** fix (a penalty against an at-floor meter redistributes into other not-yet-floored scores), which DOES attach cleanly TODAY at the clamp sites above. | **Largo833's systemic reframing (`a2312dd2` POST 16, Ted-concurred POST 1): the ignore-losing-treaty exploit (#267) is "partly just a symptom" of this game-wide flaw — once DomStab / party-pref hit their worst value, further penalties do nothing → the incumbent acts recklessly with nothing left to lose** (a nation in open rebellion at DomStab=1 wouldn't think "at least it can't get worse"). Two floated fixes (design spitballs, no tier-1 pick in-thread): **(a) SPILL-OVER** — a penalty against an at-minimum score redistributes into other scores; **(b) SHORTEN THE LOSS-COUNTDOWN** — a floored meter that would drop has a % chance to accelerate the game-over window (DomStab=1 → 50% 8yr→6yr). **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: this is a SMALL, high-VALUE, INDEPENDENT balance fix that can land EARLY (QW-to-S) — IF fix (a) spill-over is chosen.** Spill-over is a localized change at the ~4 clamp call-sites: detect "delta would push below floor AND meter already at/near floor → redirect the un-absorbed remainder to a chosen other meter (or a generic dissatisfaction sink)". It does NOT need any unbuilt system. Fix (b) countdown-shorten is BLOCKED on the unbuilt #88 meter-driven game-over (debt #28) and should co-build there. **The home is the meters/game-over subsystem, NOT the war/treaty engine** — it will recur anywhere a penalty hits a floored meter (notably the #45 loss-debuff package, which dumps onto already-floored party-pref — exactly why it was toothless in the Butler playtest). **★ SIZE: QW–S (spill-over, independent, do early); the countdown-shorten variant folds into debt #28 (#88).** ★ OPEN (human): one gap or a sub-row of #88; pick spill-over vs countdown-shorten. Pairs #88/#188/#45/#267/debt-#28. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §18.x.266; cross-ref gap #266. |
| 131 | **★★ #267 TREATY subsystem — auto-PROPOSED win/loss treaty + Senate CONFIRM-OR-RENEW-THE-WAR vote + war-length UNPREDICTABILITY + SecState/ambassador negotiation; a LARGE WAR-TRACK item, POST-LAUNCH, that must FIRST reconcile the two war-end models; the "ignore the losing treaty" exploit is a DESIGN-GAP, NOT a shipped-code bug → NO DH ROW (batch 40, `a2312dd2`-foreign-affairs/treaty; VERIFIED against code; 0% shipped as a subsystem)** | **★ VERIFIED 0% as a treaty subsystem — AND the ignore-exploit is NOT in `src/`.** The shipped war code is `revolutionaryWar.ts` (1772-RevWar-ONLY): `runRevWarBattles` ends a war on hard ground-tally thresholds — WIN at `currentGroundWins>=groundWinsNeeded`(=7) (`:254`), LOSS at `currentGroundLosses>=groundLossesRemaining`(=16) `&& !frenchAlliance` (`:259`) — and a LOSS simply sets `war.active=false; war.outcome='loss'` (`:259-264`) with no treaty at all; the WIN is consummated by the **scripted `treaty_of_paris` EraEvent** (`eraEvents1772.ts:375`) calling the one-shot `applyTreatyOfParis` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:279`: ends war, grants territory, disbands forces). The generic 1856+ resolver (`phaseRunners.ts:3593-3627`) ends at `warScore ±50` with a bare log, NO treaty. **There is NO auto-proposal on win/loss, NO Senate up/down treaty vote, NO white-peace, NO rejection→backlash→war-renew, and — decisively — NO LOSING-TREATY CODE PATH to ignore.** grep `treaty\|whitePeace\|ratif`(treaty-context) in the war engine = only the RevWar `applyTreatyOfParis` + the unwired anytime `foreign-treaty` flavor event (`anytimeNationalEvents.ts:169`). **★ DH DETERMINATION (the task's gating question): the "Secretary can ignore / Senate can reject a losing treaty penalty-free at floored meters" exploit is a FORUM-RULES-ONLY concept — it describes the *designed* treaty system, which is not in `src/`. It is therefore a DESIGN-GAP, NOT a confirmed shipped-code defect → NO DH ROW (no DH-81).** (Its root cause, the meter-floor no-op, IS real and shipped — but that is debt #130/#266, not a treaty bug.) | **The war-END side of #45 (combat is debt #105/#16), Ted's merged tier-1 direction (`a2312dd2` POST 15/17):** (1) **AUTO-PROPOSAL** on win/loss in the next legislative session (White Peace by SecState only BEFORE resolution); (2) **SENATE CONFIRM-OR-RENEW** — Senate votes to confirm; failure → backlash vs the Senate Majority Leader + the war RENEWS + anti-treaty NAY senators penalized (walks back option-3's "un-rejectable": *automatic = auto-PROPOSED, not un-rejectable*); (3) **WAR-LENGTH UNPREDICTABILITY** — guaranteed first two battles, then 50% chance of more (or roll a d6), resolved by tier-scaled net-score thresholds (Skirmish +1 / Minor +3 / Major +5 → win; opposite → loss; else White Peace), with a ~4-yr (2-phase) cushion before penalties escalating at ~6 yr (playtest baseline: 6 wars, RevWar ~8yr / NW-Indian 12yr / Barbary 6yr / War-of-1800 4yr / Canada 4yr); (4) **SecState + ambassador NEGOTIATION** (admin adjusts victories-needed <5↑/>7↓; 5%/phase loss-threshold roll + 33%/phase white-peace prompt; trait-weighted Geostrategic/Egghead vs Lowbrow/Disharmonious; Integrity gates refusing white peace — co-own with #26/#265); (5) successor-keeps-agency refinement on #45's instigator-attribution (Butler→Blue case). **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: LARGE, on the WAR TRACK, POST-LAUNCH (war is "a side show," vcczar, debt #105). The hard prerequisite is reconciling the TWO war-end models BEFORE building: this thread's Apr-2022 tier-scaled net-score thresholds vs the Sept-2022 `rethinkwar` `|raw-score|×10%-per-phase roll` ALREADY on #45/debt #105 — chronology says the %-roll superseded the thresholds (a tier-1 call).** Build needs: the generic `War` model (the unused `War` interface w/ `warScore` ships at `types.ts:1532` but is parallel/unwired to RevWar) + a treaty-proposal phase wired into the legislative session + the Senate-vote machinery + the war-renew loop. **CO-RESOLVE the #267 ignore-exploit by construction** (a confirm-or-renew vote with penalties for prolonging removes the "ignore is strictly better" incentive; the meter-floor no-op is separately fixed by #266/debt #130). Plug the #178 treaty→TERRITORY path INTO this confirmation flow. AMPU-2 defers a month-to-month NEGOTIATED back-and-forth treaty (POST 3). **★ SIZE: L; POST-LAUNCH; folds into the E3/#45/#56 war epic when it lands.** Pairs #45/#56/#178/#26/#265/#266/#88. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §21.1.C; cross-ref gap #267. |

The per-battle do-while (`:211-236`, `battleCount < 3 && d100() <= 66`) only INCREMENTS the cumulative tallies — it has **NO game-over check inside the loop**, and `revolutionaryWar.ts` never sets `gameEnded`/`triggersGameEnd` (grep = ZERO). **So a single lost battle CANNOT trigger an immediate war-loss in shipped code — the digest's "instant game-over after Trenton" is the Google-Sheets MANUAL run (06fbb2e5#POST 75, VOIDED live), not the engine.** The REAL shipped gap: the count-threshold (7 wins / 16 losses, `:16-18`) is precisely the **stale per-battle-threshold model the designer says was REPLACED** — Ted (POST 88): *"Wars are no longer won by winning or losing a certain number of battles but by using the war score and momentum chart after each term."* The shipped engine still uses the deprecated count model; the §21.1.A redesign (`|score|×10%` war-end roll + momentum swing + End-War multiplier) is the intended replacement and is 0% shipped. | **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: a SIZED, concrete fix on the war track — NOT a keystone, NOT decision-gated (the rule is GM+designer CONFIRMED).** Two distinct deliverables, do the small one first: **(a) [S, cheap-fix] correct the cadence framing + delete the stale columns** — the cadence is ALREADY per-term in code, so the only "cadence" work is replacing the count-threshold WIN/LOSS conditions (`:254-264`) so they read the war-score+momentum chart (§21.1.A) instead of `groundWinsNeeded`/`groundLossesRemaining`; | 142 | **★ DH-82 — doubled-officer "Planning" can read 6 (above the 0–5 ability cap) because the ×2 derive is unclamped; a tiny QUICK-WIN clamp (NEW b43, `06fbb2e5`; live-confirmed, the same edge Ted flagged in `rookie1772`; VERIFIED against code; = gap DH-82)** | **★ VERIFIED — the unclamped ×2 is a DERIVED battle-target value, NOT a stored-stat cap violation.** When no Secretary of War exists, ground "Planning" = `general.skills.military * 2` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:212`) and naval Planning = `(secWar?.skills.admin ?? 0) + admiral.skills.military` (`:187`); both feed `baseTarget`/`target` (`:189,214`), the d100 roll target. With a 3-Mil senior officer the doubled term is **6**, above the 0–5 cap (06fbb2e5#POST 73-74). NB this is a **derived combat coefficient, not a persisted skill** — the actual stored 0–5 skills are clamped correctly by `addSkillPoint`/`loseSkill` (`abilities.ts:6-30`, both `Math.min(5,…)`/`Math.max(0,…)`). | 143 | **★★ #273 IMPEACHMENT SUBSYSTEM — a LARGE, DECISION-GATED subsystem (Legislative-proposal → House Judicial Committee → full House → Senate 2/3 trial; non-Presidential scope; resign-at-any-stage; trait-gated vote cascade; meter/point packages; manager +1-Command); ★ DO NOT BUILD before vcczar RATIFIES (he DEFERRED the whole thread); it is the plumbing #264 (coup treason-trials) DEPENDS on + the system #112 INSTANTIATES; consolidates DH-33/DH-54/DH-66 (NEW b43, `ae435b5f`-fourteen-points-on-impeachment [DESIGN-SPEC]; VERIFIED 0% built; = gap #273)** | **★ VERIFIED — `impeach` is ABSENT from `src/` (case-insensitive grep = 0 occurrences across the whole tree).** No legislative-proposal-to-impeach, no House Judicial-Committee article step, no House/Senate trial pipeline, no resign-at-any-stage flow, no Controversial/<3-judicial gate, no impeachment-manager Command grant, no meter-effect package, no `game.impeachment` state. The succession side it pairs with is also thin: death only sets `presidentId=null` at `vacateOffice` (`phaseRunners.ts:2449`) with no VP-succeeds chain (debt cluster #61/#112). The §24.1.1 broken-impeachment flags (DH-33 `rep1800` "rules outdated, don't work"; DH-54 `nuke` "never in the rules doc at all"; DH-66 `ideo1928` trial VOIDED mid-run) are now CONSOLIDATED under #273 — they are symptoms of the SAME 0%-built subsystem, not separate gaps. | **★★ ENGINEERING TAKE: this is a LARGE first-class subsystem AND it is DECISION-GATED — vcczar DEFERRED the entire `ae435b5f` thread (POST 47/48: *"I'm not going to have time. Remind me when Anthony gets closer"*); the consensus is Ted-led tier-1 design, vcczar's final sign-off explicitly PENDING. Treat the spec (the "Fourteen Points" 13-offense machinery vs Ted's simpler counter-proposal that became the working consensus) as DESIGN-INTENT-IN-PROGRESS, NOT canon — DO NOT build before ratification.** When it IS ratified it is a NEW phase/sub-pipeline in the legislative phase (impeachment resolves in 2.6, before sign/veto — see §20.12) plus a `game.impeachment` state machine: article-generation → trial-trigger (General/Scripted-event auto-fire timing) → House vote → Senate 2/3 → removal/acquit → DomStab/Honest-Gov meter + point packages → manager +1-Command. **★ THE DEPENDENCY EDGE (load-bearing for the roadmap): #273 is the plumbing #264 (coup → treason-trials) is specced to REUSE, and the system #112 (modern institutional layer) had to IMPROVISE — so #273 GATES both. It also rides #221 (the General/Scripted-event tier auto-firing the trial) + #258 (the trait-gated/precedent conditions).** **★ SIZE: L (a full subsystem), but BLOCKED on (i) vcczar ratification [DECISION-GATE, must precede any build] and (ii) the #221 scripted-event tier for auto-fire. NOT on the near-term queue — it is post-keystone and decision-gated.** Pairs #273/#112/#264/#61/DH-33/DH-54/DH-66/#221/#258. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §24.1.1a (#273 spec home), §24.1.1, §20.12, §30.33; cross-ref gap #273. |
| 144 | **★ #274 SKILL-GAIN ACCESSIBILITY — the anti-floor-TRAP balance lever: low-Admin/low-Legis pols can't climb because the skill-EARNING path is itself stat/seat-gated (a chicken-and-egg dead end); a balance-track change DISTINCT from the #163 ceiling / #190 election-modifier / #252 committee-coarseness (NEW b43, `ddd179cb`-questions-for-playtesters; the most-requested Q10 survey change; VERIFIED against code; DESIGNED, 0% shipped; = gap #274)** | **★ VERIFIED — the career-track skill roll is FLAT, not stat-gated; the trap is the SEAT-gated growth paths AROUND it.** The career-track gain (`rollThreshold`, `phaseRunners.ts:299-318`) grants a skill at a FLAT `chance(CAREER_ODDS.skill)` = 0.5 (`types.ts:229-234`) at each 4-year threshold (`:460-461`) — the ROLL is NOT stat-gated. BUT the path INTO a useful track is the chokepoint: CPU track assignment is `bestAvailableTrack` = the pol's HIGHEST below-cap skill (`phaseRunners.ts:392-399`), so a floor-stat pol (admin 1, all else low) is shunted to whatever it's "least-bad" at, not where it's needed; tracks are CAPPED at `CAREER_TRACK_CAP = 5` per faction (`types.ts:237`), so the desirable tracks fill up (the digest's "career track to develop them is filled up"); and the OTHER skill-up source — committee/cabinet seats (`OFFICE_ADMIN_GRANT`/`OFFICE_EXPERTISE`, `types.ts:597-605,1137`; cabinet-confirm admin grant `ABILITY_EARN_RULES.cabinetConfirmAdmin`) — requires WINNING a seat, which PV (`pv.ts`) gates behind the very skills the pol lacks → the chicken-and-egg dead end ShortKing/Euri describe. There is **NO non-electoral, non-track Admin/Legislative growth source for sub-floor pols** in `src/`. | **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: a BALANCE lever (M) on the career-track / skill-economy, NOT a keystone and DISTINCT from the adjacent rows.** It owns the FLOOR of the skill economy — #163 (debt §5.4) caps the TOP (super-commander), #190 is an election modifier (not a growth source), #252 refines committee→expertise granularity (not who can GET a seat), and #163 pre-placement (§26.1) seeds the starting cohort (not in-play growth). An anti-floor-trap change touches: (a) [most likely] a NEW non-electoral growth source — random-event Admin/Legis grants, or a low-office "apprenticeship" track that earns skill without an election — keyed on a stat-FLOOR predicate so it relieves only the stuck (preserving Ted's "no safe dumping ground" pride, POST 14); and/or (b) loosen the CPU `bestAvailableTrack` shunt + `CAREER_TRACK_CAP` for floor-stat pols. Couples to §5.2 (career-track rolls), the committee pipeline (§9), and the #163 pre-placement seed. **★ SIZE: M (a balance/economy change with new growth-source content; not blocked on a keystone, but should land WITH or after the content engine so the new grants are authored data, not hardcoded).** Pairs #274/#163/#190/#252/#163-pre-placement. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §5.5 (#274 home), §5.4, §30.33; cross-ref gap #274. |
| 145 | **★ FLAVOR-EVENT TIER (#221/#261) — a per-event `isFlavor` flag + a flavor on/off toggle + a ≤40%/phase fire-cap; 0% shipped, POST-LAUNCH; rides the #221 content engine (NEW b43, `b73925a4`-ampu-flavor-event-poll; VERIFIED against code; = gaps #221/#261)** | **★ VERIFIED — `EraEvent` has NO `isFlavor` flag and NO per-event fire-probability; there is a GRAPH-WIDE base chance, but no flavor system.** `EraEvent` (`types.ts:1466-1482`) carries `id/templateId?/year/title/description/responses/decider/…` and **NO `isFlavor` / `flavor` / per-event probability field**. There IS a graph-wide `ERA_GRAPH_RULES.fireChance = 0.85` (`types.ts:1104`) — the 1772 era-event-GRAPH walker's base P that an eligible non-core node fires — but that is a single graph-wide constant, **NOT a per-event flavor probability, NOT a flavor on/off toggle, and NOT a ≤40%/phase cap.** So the flavor TIER (a low-stakes cosmetic event class the player can disable, capped so it can't crowd out substantive events) is 0% shipped. | **★ ENGINEERING TAKE: a SMALL feature that RIDES the #221 content engine — POST-LAUNCH, not near-term.** It adds: (a) an `isFlavor` boolean on the event type (the scripted-vs-flavor tier #221 already distinguishes — this is the FLAVOR half made first-class); (b) a per-event or per-tier fire-probability + a ≤40%/phase cap in the scheduler; (c) a player on/off toggle in settings (a `game.settings` flag the event scheduler reads). All three are trivial INSIDE #221's event registry + scheduler, meaningless before it. **★ SIZE: S, folded into the #221 content engine; POST-LAUNCH (flavor is the lowest-priority content tier).** Pairs #221/#261/#260. `GM⇒App`. game-mechanics §27.1.1, §14.1.3.e, §30.33; cross-ref gaps #221/#261. |

### 8.1 Confirmed shipped bugs + GM-confirmed design holes

Code defects (`BUG-*`: BUG-0 from `hd`, BUG-1/2/3 from `fed`) and GM-confirmed
design holes (`DH-*`: DH-1/2 from `modern`, DH-3..DH-11 from `hd`,
**DH-12..DH-23 from `drums`**, **DH-24..DH-28 from `pop`**, **DH-29..DH-35 from
`rep1800`**, **DH-36..DH-44 from the two batch-8 1772 threads: DH-36..DH-40 +
DH-43/DH-44 from `new1772`, DH-41/DH-42 from `tea1772`**, **DH-45..DH-57 from the
batch-9 `nuke` Cold-War/modern corpus**, **#115/#115a/#115b + the
DH-24/DH-25/DH-27/DH-36/DH-53 re-corroborations from the batch-10 `dem1820`
1820-start**, **#116/#119/#61-extended + DH-59/DH-60 from the batch-11 `arkzag`
1820→1840 full-arc**, **★ #120..#142 from batch 12 — the `tedchange` +
`smallbugs` designer rulings (now AUTHORITATIVE — `tedchange` is the official
rules-doc rewrite channel; the rulings SUPERSEDE prior GA calls where they
conflict). Many batch-12 items CLOSE long-standing OPEN items: QW0 closes,
#51's #18 sub-fork settles its algorithm half (state-scope still user-gated),
Pres-blunder rule canonicalizes the cf82a7d3 §5a #3 fuzzy "hybrid," CC
composition is rewritten, Hale/Frail/ex-Pres death schedule pins #85**.
**`BUG-*` are fixes,
not features** — small and high-value;
**BUG-0 (the relocation-cap stale constant) is the cheapest win in the roadmap**
and BUG-1 is a hard blocker the moment a federalism/1800 scenario ships
(**batch 7 LIVE-confirmed it** + merged the era-events-predating-start hole into
it). **`DH-*` are *rules gaps / balance flags*, not crashes** — the forum
rulebook had no answer and a human improvised; many **need rules *authored*
(a PM/design task) before they can be built**, several are **balance dials**
for an existing-but-unbuilt system, **DH-19/20/21/22 are architectural CPU
gaps that need K5's persistent state**, and **DH-34 (static-vs-policy-reactive
era-biases) is a ROADMAP DECISION, not a quick fix**. Verified against the
codebase where quick.

**Classification by where each `DH-*` lives:**
- **CPU-AI handler (folds into the K5 handler suite, §6.6.1):** DH-19 (gov
  menu industry-stack-aware), DH-20 (reciprocity), DH-21 (meter-guarding),
  DH-22 (cascading scandals), DH-23 (cabinet 36% bug — but smaller than
  expected because the system isn't built yet). **★ Batch-13 OC-* sub-rules fold
  into specific handlers as field-validated sub-rules (NOT new epics):** **OC-1**
  (scandal-resignee re-appointment cooldown — instance of DH-22) + **OC-5**
  (court-as-firing loophole gate) → **handler #4 (cabinet, §25.5.4)**; **OC-2**
  (one canonical `chamberControl` definition shared by leadership-select + the
  ≥60% proposer gate) → **handler #3 (leadership, §25.3)**; **★ OC-3
  (BALANCE-CRITICAL — crisis-vote ideology-floor + secession check)** →
  **handler #2 (legislation, §25.6)** as its **highest-priority sub-rule**;
  **OC-6** (kingmaker multi-protégé pairing tiebreak = highest Com+Leg+Gov) →
  the kingmaker handler (§25.11). All six are now spec-complete + field-validated
  (`oopscpu`); see §6.6.1 + §9 batch-13 lead.
- **Per-subsystem owns it (the subsystem epic must address it):** DH-5
  (Kingmaker-pairing dissolution on flip — conversion epic), DH-7 (R/D
  threshold asymmetry + Iron-Fist unilateral lower — convention epic), DH-8
  (CPU convention unstable — convention epic + handler #5), DH-9 (exec/gov
  ability stat — action-libraries epic), DH-10 (blunder still scores — action
  data flag), DH-11 (3rd-party bias + inelastic lobbies — #48/#5 balance),
  DH-17 (convention auto-drop-out + Command-limited interballot — convention
  epic), DH-18 (dark-horse resignation rolls — convention epic), DH-19 (gov
  menu — gov-actions epic + handler #10).
- **Parking lot (author rules before build):** DH-1 (filibustered MUST-pass),
  DH-12 (white-peace), DH-13 (faithless-elector trigger cap), DH-14 (era-aware
  bill ideology), DH-15 (small-state action multiplier), §25.9 Iron-Fist split
  exact-shape, divergence #10 contingent-election rules, **DH-25 (career-track
  bootstrap — BLOCKS modern scenario)**, **DH-33 (impeachment rewrite, batch 7)**,
  **DH-41 (★ batch 8 — the general SCOTUS-ruling → downstream-statute cascade is
  UNBUILT and was explicitly DEFERRED by `vcczar`: a genuinely undesigned cascade,
  author the policy before building it)**.
- **Per-subsystem owns it (batch 7):** **DH-29 (★★ Reconstruction solo-blocker —
  a BUILD REQUIREMENT on E3b: a CPU-passable readmission path; ties to K5 handler

- **Quick-wins (XS):** DH-3 (career-track pres bar), DH-6 (top-2-vs-top-3

- **Per-subsystem owns it (batch 8):** **DH-38 (no late-ratification / "Rogue

  legislature-state vote count undecided — Kingmaker votes vs Gov+Senators+focus-Reps

- **META justification, NOT a discrete fix:** **DH-36 (★ batch 8 — GM burnout
  from manual bookkeeping ABANDONED a 12-turn multiplayer 1772 game, `new1772`
  POST 3607).** This is the **single strongest piece of "why build this at all"
  evidence in the corpus** — the entire value proposition is that the computer
  owns the deterministic upkeep a human GM cannot sustain. It is not a row to
  schedule; it is the motivation behind the whole roadmap (especially the
  scaling-wall items #19/#20 and the CPU handler suite). Cite it, don't queue it.
- **Multiplayer / parking-lot (batch 8):** **DH-37 (no politician-to-politician
  trading — the #1 AMPU-2 wishlist item).** A genuine small feature (a trade
  window between factions) but **not on any critical path**; it pairs with the
  era-boundary faction-trade window (§27.2 step 1, which DOES exist in spec) and
  with multiplayer. Park it.
- **Convention CPU handler guards (batch 8):** **the lone-ideology minor-candidate
  convention exploit (#104) — a single ideologically-isolated minor candidate can
  exploit the convention; fold into the convention CPU handler (E9 handler 9e /
  §6.6.1) as a known exploit to guard against.** **stat-collapse → forced
  presidential resignation (#105) — a small rule (all-Command + "most" skills
  hitting a floor forces a sitting President to resign); lands NEAR the succession
  work (#61) as a one-rule addition.**
- **Small additive mechanics folded into existing epics (batch 8, the new #100-#103):**
  **#100 SCOTUS-overturns-a-ratified-Amendment + threshold-amendable** → folds into
  the amendments item (#39, the Gov-requested judicial-review-of-an-amendment path +
  the mutable ratification-threshold field) and the SCOTUS docket (E25). **#102 dual
  era-scoring (per-era winner + cumulative "winner so far")** → the WIN-CONDITION
  scoreboard; folds into K3/K4's per-era point-banking (#68 banks the cumulative
  total) — the win condition is **dual** (per-era + cumulative); relevant when the
  era system + endgame are built. **#103 presidential-vote modifier stack +

- **MERGED:** the batch-7 "era-events-predating-start-DROPPED" hole is the

  §5a #4 in §8.1's existing carry).
- **Major rework (M, the headline structural change):** **#124 cabinet
  enthusiasm REWORK** — a teardown of E16's existing lobby→enthusiasm path.
  **(a)** LOBBY satisfaction now writes POINTS (to Pres + matching-lobby
  factions, via the `Faction.score?` ledger), NOT enthusiasm; **(b)**
  IDEOLOGY COMPOSITION drives enthusiasm (≥50% cabinet of an ideology =
  +enth; ≤20% = −enth); **(c)** the 3-shifts/half-term cap holds. Land
  AFTER K2 + K5 (the rework requires the conditional-vote-rules primitive
  + the new score path); **re-scope E16 from "build the confirmation
  system in the right shape" to "build confirmation + #124 enthusiasm
  rework TOGETHER in the right shape from day one."** The actual
  percentages are DESIGNER-GATED OPEN.
- **Pres-implementation rule (S):** **#126 2-step Admin-then-Command

  challengeable** (`smallbugs#POST 250-269`, vcczar) **OVERRIDES**
  `tea1772`'s #100 "SCOTUS can overturn an amendment." **E5 keeps the
  strike-a-statute path + the mutable-threshold field but DROPS the
  SCOTUS-overturns-amendment branch; E25 keeps the docket + Justice
  drift + court size + DH-32 state-guard but DROPS Gov-requested
  judicial-review-of-an-amendment.** This is a sequencing simplification.
- **Designer-gated OPEN items (Decision-gated SUB-BUCKET):** the 9
  open `tedchange` items per game-mechanics §30.2 — Mil-Prep lvl 4 fix,
  UEM (#125), Crisis trait consolidation, term-limit Gov pre-Senate,
  faithless-elector rewording, party rename PL-vs-EraEvo, VP-must-be-
  same-party-on-resignation, cabinet enthusiasm percentages, cabinet
  ideology weighting; **★ batch-13 ADDS TWO: OC-4** (CPU draft off-ideology
  gate — Ted wants "a better third way" than draft-strong-pol-off-ideology
  vs. uncontested; do NOT silently ship a hard gate; `oopscpu#POST 227-228,
  234`) **+ OC-8** (define "office" for the Scandalous-Non-Office-Holder
  forced-out event + rewrite the contradictory event text; flagged to
  `@vcczar`; `oopscpu#POST 334, 336`). **NOT ready-to-build until Ted/vcczar
  closes them in `tedchange`/`smallbugs`/a follow-up.** These join the
  "designer-gated" sub-bucket of the PARKING LOT (alongside the user-gated
  bucket). The cabinet OC-3/OC-1/OC-5/OC-2 sub-rules, by contrast, are NOT
  designer-gated — they fold straight into the E9 handler rows as
  field-validated sub-rules (above).
- **Per-subsystem owns it (batch 13):** **DH-61 (★ scenario-boot must seed
  era-active wars — folds into the `scenarioBoot` pipeline / K4 + the generic
  `War` model; a HARD prerequisite for `scenario1788`; `oopscpu#POST 338-344`)**,
  **DH-62 (★ pre-12A two-votes-per-state / no-ticket EC mode — folds into the
  per-state-EC + 12A-toggle epic #93/#5 as an era-keyed election-mode variant;
  also a HARD prerequisite for `scenario1788`; `oopscpu#POST 192-199`)**. Both
  sequence WITH E1 / `scenario1788`. Also **#143 (post-election Command-decay)**
  is an XS standalone quick-win (above), and **OC-1/OC-2/OC-3/OC-5/OC-6** fold
  into the E9 handler rows (above).

|---|---|---|---|
| **BUG-0 (batch 4) — relocation cap is STALE: shipped `5`, design `4`** (divergence #9) | **VERIFIED:** `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` at **`src/types.ts:247`**. The designer (`vcczar`) changed the cap to **4** non-alt-state relocations mid-thread and it went **LIVE in the running playtest** (`hd` POST 7062–7066, 7555); the browser engine never caught up. **★ BATCH-12 (`smallbugs#POST 734-735`, 12-30-25, vcczar-APPROVED — designer-AUTHORITATIVE):** *"A faction is limited to FOUR total attempted moves per half-term. A politician that moves to an ALT-STATE does NOT count against the FOUR total moves."* — the cap is now fully **SETTLED** by the designer rules-doc patch. No further design or human review required. | **One-line edit:** `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 4`. **+ a guard at the accumulator so alt-state moves don't decrement the budget** (verify the alt-state accounting in `runPhase_2_1_4_Relocations`). (The full feature — auto-Carpetbagger + 10-yr expiry — is divergence #2 / a separate consistency PR; this row covers the const + alt-state exemption.) | **XS — the cheapest win in the whole roadmap. Do it FIRST. ★ BATCH-12: now fully RULED (was "open-design + build" through batch 11; the alt-state exemption is the only addition).** No dependency, no migration. |
| **BUG-1** — era events never deactivate by era for non-1772/1856 start years (an 1800-start wrongly loses the Louisiana Purchase; stale events also dilute the roll table) | `buildEraEventsForYear(snap.game.year)` is called with **no era-vs-start-year filter** at `phaseRunners.ts:2817`. Latent today (only 1772/1856 ship; the 1772 graph path at `:2801` is separate), but a blocker once a 3rd scenario lands | Add an "era-lock" filter: an event whose era is past the scenario's start era is treated as already-happened and excluded from the roll table (`fed` 524-535). **Resolve together with DH-2 and divergence #4 — same scheduling surface.** | **XS.** Land *before/with* the federalism scenario — it directly gates it. |
| **BUG-2** — `Chisholm v. Georgia` needs an "11th Amendment not ratified" gate | **No SCOTUS case data contains it** (grep: 0 hits in `src`). This is a *forward requirement on SCOTUS-case content*, not a live crash | When the federalism SCOTUS case file is authored, gate `Chisholm` on `!amendmentPassed('11th')`. Bundles into the SCOTUS subsystem (divergence #7). | **XS.** Bundles into SCOTUS content; needs the amendments field (#39). |
| **BUG-3** — no fallback when no viable PM-General candidate exists | `GeneralInChief` is a real seat (`types.ts:1121`, granted via `cabinetSeatsForYear` `:1145`) filled at `phaseRunners.ts:2255`; the **no-candidate path is uncovered** → potential crash | Define the empty-pool behavior (leave vacant + log, or auto-generate a stopgap officer — ties to procedural pol generation, scaling wall a). | **XS.** Defensive guard; do alongside the war/cabinet work. |
| **DH-1** — a filibustered "MUST-pass" bill has no rules remedy | Filibuster itself is unbuilt; no deadlock rule exists. GM improvised a 4-leader special-committee auto-pass with a per-day "shutdown" penalty clock (`modern` 640-716). **A rules gap, not a code bug.** | **Author the deadlock rule first** (forced-compromise vs shutdown-clock vs fallback auto-pass — a design call), then build it into the filibuster epic. | **Design task + S build.** Blocks the filibuster epic's completeness. |
| **DH-2** — modern era-event deck fired off-year cards (2008 cards in 2018) | Possible scheduling defect or intended shuffle/backlog (`modern` 2221). **Reported, not verified.** | Resolve **together with BUG-1 + divergence #4** (era-event scheduling) — all three are the same scheduling surface. | **Investigate within the scheduling-fork decision.** |
| **DH-3 (`hd`)** — career-track pols can run for President | A career-track pol is barred from Gov/Rep/leadership/Kingmaker but **no rule bars a presidential run** (`hd` 8205–8219). GM-acknowledged, intended-to-fix. | **quick-fix / author.** Bar career-track pols from the presidential candidate pool (and from CPU presidential selection). Relates to #63 primary. | **XS rule + S build.** Add a guard at candidate eligibility. |
| **DH-4 (`hd`)** — "Extend Credit to all nations" near-auto-win foreign loop | Extending credit to all 7 powers "works amazingly every time" → stacked EconStab/industry/relation bonus (`hd` 7346). Diplomacy itself is unbuilt (#26). | **balance.** Needs a diminishing-returns / cap rule. Author the cap **into the diplomacy-actions epic**, not separately. | **Balance dial; folds into diplomacy library.** |
| **DH-5 (`hd`)** — Kingmaker-flip grabs the protégé(s), "insanely OP" | Converting/flipping a Kingmaker also seizes his protégés + their +1 election standing (`hd` 7589, 8762). Kingmaker/conversion exist (`KINGMAKER_RULES` `types.ts:295`; `CONVERSION_ODDS` `:268`). | **balance / quick-fix.** Add a pairing-dissolution-on-flip rule (or no protégé transfer). Relates to #29/#30. | **S.** A rule in the conversion path; **also note for Reconstruction** (#57 prunes broken Kingmaker pairs — same code area). |
| **DH-6 (`hd`)** — contingent-election top-2 vs top-3 deviation | GM uses **top 2** vs the 12th Amendment's **top 3**, overruled "Game rules" (`hd` 5720–5721). No contingent path exists. | **author-before-build.** Build must **pick a stated cutoff** (config it: `contingentTopN`). Bundles into the contingent-election addition (#62). | **Design call + bundled into #62.** |
| **DH-7 (`hd`)** — R/D convention-threshold asymmetry + Iron-Fist unilateral change | Dems run **3/4**, GOP **2/3→½+1**; an **Iron-Fist PL can unilaterally lower** the threshold (`hd` 4726, 623–630, 5594–5713). No convention thresholds exist. | **author-before-build.** Document a chosen rule (era/party-fixed vs temporary) + re-gate the rules-change power. Bundles into the **convention epic**. | **Design call; part of the convention subsystem.** |
| **DH-8 (`hd`)** — **CPU convention AI unstable + ballot-shift rule ambiguous** | GM-acknowledged: the CPU convention AI is "rough/awful, needs a 2.9 rework" and **cannot perform some actions humans can**; the ballot-shift rule is genuinely ambiguous (`hd` 4686–4690, 2025–2038). No convention AI exists today. | **CPU-AI (known-unstable) + author.** **Any conventions build must OWN the CPU side** — a player-only convention is not shippable for a single-player game where most factions are CPU. Firm both specs (ballot-shift: GM ruled next round) inside the epic. | **Owned by the convention epic; do not treat the CPU as optional.** |
| **DH-9 (`hd`)** — exec/gov-action ability stat inconsistent | The ability stat differs across actions/events (exec=Command in build, gov=Gov); designers think it should all be Command; a live "all-Admin implementation" rebalance was applied (`hd` 2274, 2279–2282, 3097–3098). | **author-before-build.** One canonical decision before the action libraries (K2) author their `resolve` stat. Relates to #22/#23. | **Design call; resolve *before* the action-library epics fix the stat per row.** |

Refines #22. | **S; a data-flag convention for the era-event/action content.** |

| **DH-12 (`drums`)** — white-peace rules MISSING from war system | War system unbuilt (#45/#56); designer Tyler had to dig up old treaty docs (`drums` 6533-6541). | **author + balance.** Spec: Moderate Implementation (Pres + Sec State + Sec War), 75% −1 Party Pref, −100 Mil-Industry, −500 Expansionists/President, antebellum status quo. **Author into the generic-war epic (#6).** | **Design call + S, folds into #6.** |

| **DH-29 (`rep1800` → ★ STRUCTURAL reframe `hd1`)** — ★★ Reconstruction readmission DEADLOCKS → solo (AND deadlocked-human) Reconstruction UNRESOLVABLE | GM-verified (`rep1800` POST 9170): *"only 3 factions would ever consider voting for it… in a single player game it basically can never pass."* **★ Batch-16 (`hd1` POST 2678) ESCALATES this from CPU-only to STRUCTURAL:** the FIRST run with HUMANS on both sides still deadlocked — *"The Congress could never agree on a Reconstruction guideline… NEITHER PASSED and states just started coming back into the Union with NO reconstruction plan at all."* Mutual filibuster → null drift. So it is NOT a CPU-heuristic gap; it is a both-chambers-must-agree paralysis. Reconstruction still unbuilt (#57; 0 hits in `types.ts`). | **BUILD REQUIREMENT on E3b — now with the AUTHORED FIX in hand (#156).** vcczar's restart rewrite (`hd1` POST 2693) resolves it: a **4-plan model on BOTH Pres + Congress** gated by *"a plan adopted by Congress OR by the President"* → **the President can adopt a plan UNILATERALLY**, so solo AND deadlocked-human Reconstruction always resolves. The §9.1.6 options 1/2/3 are SUPERSEDED by this authored prerequisite. See #156 + §9.1.6. | **A hard gate on E3b's readmission half; the FIX = #156 (the unilateral-adopt prerequisite). DH-29's "author the rule" step is now DONE — build the authored design.** |

Lands with BUG-1 / the era-event work — same scheduling surface. | **XS; pair with BUG-1.** |

Lands in the bill-typing epic (#42). | **XS-S; in the bill-typing epic.** |

Lands in the SCOTUS docket epic. | **XS; a guard in the SCOTUS ruling-effect path.** |
| **DH-33 (`rep1800`) → ★ now 3-THREAD-CONFIRMED with DH-54 + DH-66 (`ideo1928`)** — impeachment ruleset broken/outdated | `rep1800` §A 465-474 / §B 3594, 3620: the mini-flow runs but the canonical rules are flagged non-functional + improvised (corroborates `hd`'s "impeachment super outdated" across a 2nd campaign). **★ Batch-18 (`ideo1928`, DH-66) is the THIRD thread:** the "Improper SC Justice" general event triggered an impeachment trial that Jimmy + Ted **VOIDED MID-RUN** (POST 816-861) because the rules under-specify article generation, trial-firing, and the Controversial-vs-<3-judicial inconsistency — and **Ted drafted a rewrite that is NOT yet final.** Verified: **0** `impeach` hits in `src/` — no subsystem exists at all. | **author-before-build (PARKING LOT).** The spec is NOT final (Ted's rewrite is pending). Do NOT build against the current under-specified rules; await Ted's authored rewrite, then build it into the legislative-depth epic (Phase-2 #29). | **Design task; parking lot — await Ted's pending rewrite. Now 3-thread (DH-33/DH-54/DH-66).** |
| **DH-34 (`rep1800`)** — ★ static era-biases are unfixable ("AMPU 2.0") → the Red-unwinnable hole | The single most-repeated `rep1800` complaint: `State.bias` is a static per-era table that doesn't react to policy (abolish slavery → the South should swing Red) → Federalists acknowledged-unwinnable 1800-1840; players quit. GM + designer: dynamic biases "too complicated / not part of the AMPU vision… maybe AMPU 2.0." | **ROADMAP DECISION (not a quick fix).** Ship static biases (accept the imbalance — the forum's own stance) vs. invest in a large new policy-reactive bias system. **My call: ship static**, revisit only if balance becomes a release blocker. Pairs with #21/#34 (bias as a field) + DH-29's solo-balance theme. | **A roadmap-level call; no cheap fix.** |
| **DH-35 (`rep1800`)** — thin early-era presidential agency ("this era is a bore") | `rep1800` §A 2756-2760, 2930, 3110: in the pre-primary eras the President's only exec actions are flavor tours; the modern toolkit isn't unlocked yet, feeding the "Blue auto-wins, governing is dull" sentiment. | **era-gating-with-enough-early-agency.** The exec-action / primary libraries must be era-gated **with enough EARLY-era agency** to keep the pre-convention eras engaging. Pairs with #23 (exec-action library) + #63 (primary-era unlock). | **A content/era-gating constraint on the action libraries.** |
| **DH-36 (`new1772`)** — ★★ GM/manual-upkeep burnout — now a **4-thread cluster + a designer-run SUCCESS that proves the ceiling** (the META-justification for the entire build) | `new1772` POST 3607: the first captured multiplayer 1772 campaign (10 humans, ~12 turns) collapsed when the first-time GM could not sustain the manual bookkeeping. **★★ Batch-24 raises this to FOUR documented burnout deaths in the recent corpus** + sharpens the CAUSE: **(1)** `new1772` (the original), **(2)** `dem1820#POST 900` (2nd), **(3)** `modernday`/`pop2012b` (Rodja resigns — *"freaking hard to be the literal computer"*), and **(4) the two batch-24 1772 runs, BOTH of which died to upkeep load, NOT a loss/win:** `grass1772` died SPECIFICALLY because 2 humans couldn't hand-run 8 CPU factions ("didn't have the time to run the two player factions AND all 8 CPU factions", `#POST 328`) → fixed by ADDING humans to offload the CPU work (`#POST 348`); `rookie1772` found the solo run "a part time job" (`#POST 36`), opened it to 4-5 helpers, then died to GM time-burnout (`#POST 1444`). **★ The NEW angle the batch-24 1772 pair adds: the load-bearing cost is specifically the MANUAL CPU-FACTION SIMULATION** (run 1 faction, not ten) — the exact work #114/E9 must own. **★★ Batch-25 (`principle1772`) ADDS THE OTHER END OF THE ARGUMENT — a designer-paid SUCCESS that quantifies the ceiling.** It is the FIRST 1772-cluster run to reach a natural ~1800 endpoint WITHOUT a burnout death — but ONLY because **@MrPotatoTed (the game co-author) himself hand-simulated all NINE CPU factions** (cabinets, draft lists, leadership votes, event responses; he even REWROTE the CPU career-track heuristic mid-run, POST 323) while @vcczar live-ruled. So the cluster now carries BOTH ends: 4 burnout deaths (the cost of NOT having the CPU-AI) AND a designer-paid completion (the proof that the per-faction sim is exactly what makes the shipped solo mode completable). **Not a code bug — the META-justification for the entire build.** | **NOT a discrete fix.** The strongest single "why build AMPU at all" datum: the computer must own all deterministic upkeep AND the per-faction CPU sim (the human GM cannot). Cite it as the motivation behind scaling walls #19/#20 + the **CPU handler suite (E9/#114) + the DH-69 rules/legal-move surface (debt #53)** — batch 24 makes these the load-bearing "make it playable solo" investments, and **batch 25 ESCALATES E9/K5/#114 from "reduces upkeep" to "the LOAD-BEARING PREREQUISITE for the shipped solo mode to be playable to completion"** (the designer-run reached its end only because a human did the CPU-AI's job). | **Motivation, not a roadmap row. Don't queue it — but it now carries the strongest justification in the corpus for E9/K5 + DH-69, with batch 25 the decisive escalation.** |
| **DH-37 (`new1772`)** — no politician-to-politician trading (#1 AMPU-2 wishlist) | `new1772`: players wanted to trade individual pols between factions; not modeled. Pairs with the era-boundary faction-trade window (§27.2 step 1, which IS specced — but that trades whole *factions*, not pols). | **multiplayer / parking-lot.** A small trade-window feature; **not on any critical path.** Park it next to multiplayer + the faction-trade window. | **Parking lot / multiplayer; small.** |
| **DH-38 (`new1772`)** — no late-ratification / "Rogue Island" / amendment-window path | `new1772`: the founding thread had no model for a state abstaining or ratifying late within a multi-year window — decliners were pulled in instead (corroborates `rep1800`/`tea1772` stragglers-ratify-late, #39). | **folds into the Constitutional-Convention era system (E1 federalism / founding).** Model abstaining + a multi-year amendment-ratification window rather than force-including decliners. | **Folds into the federalism/founding era epic (E1).** |
| **DH-39 (`new1772`)** — all-human Convention deadlock unhandled | `new1772`: with every faction human, a Constitutional/nominating Convention can deadlock with no resolution path (the all-CPU and solo paths dodge this). **Multiplayer-relevant.** | **convention machinery.** A deadlock-resolution rule for the all-human case (timeout / forced-compromise) — owned by the convention epic; matters once hot-seat multiplayer (M1) exists. | **Convention machinery; multiplayer-relevant.** |
| **DH-40 (`new1772`)** — SCOTUS justice-count bill not auto-packaged with establish-court → game STALLS | `new1772` POST 2317, 3053, 3096: "Establish Federal Judiciary" + "Set the Number of Supreme Court Justices to 6" are **two separate bills**; GM ruled they are NOT auto-packaged — if the court passes but the count bill fails, **the game cannot advance** (the SCOTUS phase is skipped every turn until justices exist; both failed repeatedly). | **bug-fix in the bill-packaging / SCOTUS-establish path.** Either package the two bills, or guard the stall (the establish-court bill carries a default count, or the SCOTUS phase no-ops cleanly until a count is set). Lands in the bill-packaging work + the founding offices-by-law layer (#101). | **XS-S bug-fix; in the bill-packaging / SCOTUS-establish path.** |
| **DH-41 (`tea1772`)** — ★ general SCOTUS-ruling → downstream-statute cascade is UNBUILT and DEFERRED | `tea1772` POST 124-126: a spectator argued **Prigg v. Pennsylvania** should auto-void the Fugitive Slave Act; `vcczar` deferred — *"V will need to think about it."* Distinct from the *built* strike-a-single-law (POST 770-784) and overturn-an-amendment-on-review (#100). **Today a contradicting ruling leaves the law operative.** | **author-before-build (PARKING LOT).** A genuinely undesigned cascade: does a ruling that contradicts a law on the books void/neuter that law, or leave it operative (current default)? Author the policy before building it. Cross-refs §24.4's *Pollock*→income-tax coupling (which IS a designed gate). | **Design task; PARKING LOT. The headline batch-8 author-before-build item.** |
| **DH-42 (`tea1772`)** — national meters swamp per-state lean → no disputed/close elections | `tea1772`: national meters dominate the per-state `bias` so heavily that elections are rarely close/disputed — a realism hole. | **balance.** Re-weight national-meter vs per-state-lean contributions in the election math so close races can occur. The reader (#58 gate) is separate engine work.** |

## 9. Build-sequencing advice

> **★ Framing: this whole section sequences the GM-replacement program.** Per the
> north-star (`game-context.md` → "The app must subsume the human GM"), every
> `GM⇒App`-tagged gap is the engine taking over work a human GM does by hand —
> the referee/calculator half (election/platform math, conventions, scenario
> boot, lingering rolls, decision-event/treaty adjudication, end-conditions,
> in-app rules) and the opponent half (the E9/K5 CPU faction AI). Order below is
> dependency-first; the `roadmap.md` "GM-replacement lens" groups the same gaps
> by which half of the GM's job they retire.

> **This section is written for the roadmap-planner to lift directly.** It is my
> engineering opinion on order, dependencies, and rough size/risk for the
> game-pm gap log (~176 rows across 5+ eras + A1–A9 presentation + the CPU-AI
> cluster #70–#85 + the **batch-6 scenario-boot / endgame cluster #86–#91** + the
> **batch-7 early-republic cluster #92–#99** + the **batch-8 founding/era cluster
> #100–#105** + the **batch-12 designer-rulings cluster #120–#142** + the
> **batch-13 `oopscpu` CPU-validation cluster #143–#146 + OC-1…OC-8 + DH-61/DH-62**
> + the **batch-14 `gild1868` gilded-era content cluster #147–#150 + DH-63**),
> the design divergences (mechanics §19.1, now **#1–#21**), the confirmed bugs
> (incl. BUG-0), and the GM design holes (DH-1/DH-2 + DH-3..DH-11 + DH-12..DH-23 +
> DH-24..DH-28 + DH-29..DH-35 + **DH-36..DH-44 + DH-61/DH-62 + DH-63**). Source: codebase +
> `gilded` + `fed` + `1772s` + `modern` + `hd` + `drums` + `pop` + `rep1800` +
> **`new1772` + `tea1772` + `dem1820` + `arkzag` + `tedchange` + `smallbugs` +

> > just designed-and-walled. LIFT THE TWO `eraGraph.ts` WALLS.** Prior batches had
> > the federalism foreign-affairs spine as *static designed content* (the `fed`
> > spec + `principle1772`'s endpoint). `summer2021` is the **first source to run
> > the whole federalism TURN LOOP forward** (cabinet appointment → era/decision
> > events → cabinet-driven lingering → diplomacy → legislation+veto+override →
> > presidential action; §20.9), proving the walled content is not hypothetical — it
> > is the *actual gameplay* of the next era. **★ Shipped-state CONFIRMED unchanged
> > (re-verified):** `eraGraph.ts::validate()` still throws via the same TWO guards —
> > `:153-155` rejects any node with `chartIndex >= 49` ("French Revolution /
> > Federalism is out of scope") and `:150-152` rejects any `president`/`cabinet`
> > decider ("no President/Cabinet pre-1789"); **every event in §20.9-§20.15 is a
> > post-1788 president/cabinet decision-event.** Authoring federalism STILL requires
> > lifting both walls (gate president/cabinet deciders on `year >= 1789` instead of
> > forbidding them) + per-power diplomacy-relation meters on the snapshot (none
> > exist — `grep relation|diplomacyMeter|foreignRelations in src/` = ZERO; the
> > shipped `snap.game.diplomacy` is a flat 8-power score map mutated only by the
> > §11.1 lingering drift, not a relation-event meter). **No re-sequence; this is
> > the highest-confidence "federalism is the next-era build" signal in the corpus.**
> > game-mechanics §20.9, §20.3.1. debt #57.
> >
> > **(b) ★ #179 (NEW) — the cabinet LINGERING-ROLL meter engine (the per-turn
> > input to the economy; designed, hand-rolled).** The federalism counterpart to
> > the shipped §11.1 lingering: each lingering phase, **every cabinet member rolls
> > to move their portfolio meter ±1, and it can BACKFIRE** (Treasury rolled badly →
> > EconStab dropped to Great Recession; War+Navy both succeeded → MilPrep maxed).
> > **★ Shipped-state CONFIRMED unbuilt-as-designed (verified):** the shipped
> > `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260`) is a **deterministic,
> > skill-keyed DRIFT** — `drift(skill)` (`:3266-3273`) maps a cabinet member's
> > `admin`/`military` skill to a fixed ±delta with NO roll, NO backfire, NO
> > Efficient/Pliable/regional hook; the cabinet pick `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet`
> > (`:2158-2224`) is a flat score-sort with NO ideology gate, NO Speaker/PPT/advisor
> > decider override, NO bill-coupling. So #179 is a **redesign of the existing
> > deterministic drift into a roll-based engine** PLUS five sub-systems. It bundles
> > **≥6 sub-rules that are ≥2 distinct systems** (§20.10): (a) per-portfolio ±roll
> > w/ backfire + **Efficient = auto-+2-if-it-moves** [vcczar verbatim, ch99 POST
> > 8949]; (b) **Pliable Pres → Speaker/PPT/Key-Advisor decider override** incl.
> > SCOTUS nomination; (c) **cabinet-bill double-points coupling** [propose-in-
> > portfolio → carried to law = 2× for both, dropped = SecXX −50]; (d) **ideology-
> > gated confirmation by office** [RW-Populist SecWar rejected]; (e) **regional-
> > representation meter penalty** [DomStab fell — no Deep-South Sec]; (f)
> > **executive-actions-implemented-by-cabinet-with-BLUNDER rolls** [the §10.4.1/#22/
> > #126 implementation-blunder layer in a cabinet context]. **★ Flag for the human:
> > SPLIT #179** — (i)+(f) is the meter/blunder engine, (b)+(d) is the appointment/
> > confirmation layer, (c) is a legislation-coupling, (e) is an election-feedback
> > input; they have different dependencies. **Where it binds:** `phaseRunners.ts`
> > `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`:3260`, the roll engine) + `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet`
> > (`:2158`, the gate+decider-override) + the §10.4.1 multi-decider/blunder layer +
> > the bill-scoring path (`:3508`-area double-points) + the §22.2 enthusiasm input.
> > **Size: L (bundles ≥2 systems); gated on the federalism era existing (#177) +
> > the diplomacy/EconStab meters it moves; couples to #67 meter ladders + #116/#160
> > EconStab cascade.** game-mechanics §20.10. debt #59.
> >
> > **(c) ★ #182 (NEW) — `command` = the President's PER-TURN EXECUTIVE-ACTION
> > BUDGET.** Verbatim (ch95:159): *"Osgood can do up to 4 actions each turn because
> > he has 4 command."* So a President's `command` value = his executive-action count
> > per Presidential-Action phase — generalizing the §24.1/#61 "0-command acting
> > president is inert" read into a positive budget (0 = inert, N = N actions/turn),
> > each action then implemented by the relevant cabinet member with a blunder roll
> > (#179(f)). **★ Shipped-state CONFIRMED unbuilt (verified):** `command` today is
> > ONLY a PV input (`pv.ts:74`, `total += p.command * 10`), a kingmaker-graduation
> > reward (`phaseRunners.ts:1441/1465`), a cabinet/leader/CC grant (`addCommandPoint`,
> > `abilities.ts:33`), a decay-eligible stat (`:2398/2409`), and a candidacy gate
