> these threads are policy-genre content drops (Healthcare/Crimes/Military/Expansionism) that land once #258 (the predicate
> field, debt #120) + #221 (the registry) ship — they CORROBORATE the content engine and WIDEN its schema; the treaty thread
> (`a2312dd2`) is the war-track referee adjudicating war-end; #265 (cabinet agency) is the GM giving each officer a turn.
> **Bottom line: NO new keystone, NO re-sequence; top of queue UNCHANGED** (QW0 → K0/K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1).
>
> **★ THREE PRIORITY BUILD-STATE DETERMINATIONS (the task's headline verifications):**
> **(1) ★ #267 treaty + the "ignore the losing treaty" exploit → DESIGN-GAP, NOT a shipped bug → NO DH ROW (no DH-81).** The
> war engine (`revolutionaryWar.ts`) is 1772-RevWar-ONLY; a LOSS just sets `war.active=false; outcome='loss'` (`:259-264`) with
> NO treaty, and the WIN is consummated by the scripted `treaty_of_paris` EraEvent → `applyTreatyOfParis` (`:279`). **There is
> NO treaty-confirmation machinery and — decisively — NO losing-treaty code path to ignore.** The "Secretary ignores / Senate
> rejects a losing treaty penalty-free at floor" exploit describes the *designed* (forum-rules-only) treaty system, which is not
> in `src/`. So it is a design-gap (debt #131/#267), NOT a confirmed shipped-code defect. (Its root cause, the meter-floor
> no-op, IS shipped — but that is #266/debt #130, not a treaty bug.)
> **(2) ★ #266 meter-at-floor = no deterrent → CONFIRMED REAL + SHIPPED.** All 7 `NationalMeters` (`types.ts:1399-1407`) clamp
> to `[-5,5]` via `clamp` (`rng.ts:49-51`) at every application path (`applyEffect` `phaseRunners.ts:3213`; anytime events
> `:2588-2591`; modulation `:2991`), so a `-1` against a meter at `-5` is a silent no-op. **★ CRITICAL CORRECTION: the digest's
> "shorten the 8yr→6yr countdown" fix assumes a multi-year game-over CLOCK that does NOT exist** — `game.gameEnded`
> (`types.ts:1635`) is a one-shot flag set instantly by `event.triggersGameEnd` (`:2871`); there is NO meter-driven loss roll
> and NO countdown clock in `src/` (debt #28/#88 still UNBUILT). So the countdown-shorten fix has no clock to attach to yet
> (co-build with debt #28); the **SPILL-OVER** fix DOES attach cleanly today at the ~4 clamp sites → the SMALL, high-value,
> INDEPENDENT, do-EARLY balance fix.
> **(3) ★ #265 cabinet-officer agency → CONFIRMED flavor.** `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2224`) is
> appointment-ONLY (`cabinet[seat]=pick.id` `:2191`); officers produce only PASSIVE effects (cabinet-expertise meter modifiers
> `:3301-3367`; confirm-admin; SecWar planning in RevWar). The ONLY player-choosable per-turn officer-action library that ships
> is the governor's (#20, flat/state-agnostic). The President executive-action phase (`:3632-3645`) is an AUTO-firing
> (`chance(0.5)`) pick of 4 HARDCODED actions, NOT per-officer. Generalizing #20 to AG/SecState/SecDef touches: a per-seat
> action registry + a player-choice UI surface + #258 predicate-gating + the #221/#237 meter/policy-flag effect layer (debt
> #132, M).
>
> **(①) ★ #258 (the predicate field, debt #120) REMAINS the content-engine keystone — batch 40 WIDENS its vocab AGAIN (3 new
> predicate KINDS), no re-sequence.** Healthcare/Crimes/Military/Expansionism are all #237 genre instances whose `Preq:` blocks
> demand new predicate classes: **court-precedent-exists** (`e456b6b3` Miller-test-exists → content tier), **prior-policy-active**
> (Federal-Death-Penalty-active → method bills; program-X-exists/expanded), **meter≥threshold** (corruption-level →
> prosecutorial discretion), **occupation-status** + **territory-status** (`4e76e6c3` annex-occupied-X). Crimes' POST-1 `Preq:`
> block is the WIDEST predicate vocabulary captured to date. The FIELD-add is unchanged (debt #120); this batch's witnesses
> confirm + widen it (joins debt #129). game-mechanics §14.1.3.g.
> **(②) ★ #266 meter-floor (debt #130) = a SMALL, high-value SYSTEMIC balance fix that lands EARLY/INDEPENDENTLY (QW–S) — IF
> the SPILL-OVER variant is chosen.** Confirmed no-op-at-floor (above). Spill-over is a localized change at the ~4 clamp
> call-sites and needs no unbuilt system → cheap-fixes lane. The countdown-shorten variant is blocked on the unbuilt #88
> meter-driven game-over (debt #28). game-mechanics §18.x.266.
> **(③) ★ #267 treaty (debt #131) = a LARGE WAR-TRACK item, POST-LAUNCH — keep it on the war track and FIRST reconcile the two
> war-end models.** This thread's Apr-2022 tier-scaled net-score thresholds (Skirmish+1/Minor+3/Major+5) vs the Sept-2022
> `rethinkwar` `|raw-score|×10%-per-phase roll` ALREADY on #45/debt #105 — chronology says the %-roll superseded the thresholds
> (tier-1 call). Co-resolve the ignore-exploit by construction (a confirm-or-renew vote with prolonging-penalties removes the
> "ignore is strictly better" incentive; the meter-floor no-op is separately fixed by #266). Plug #178 (treaty→territory) into
> the confirmation flow. War is "a side show" → defers to the E3/#45/#56 war epic (debt #105). game-mechanics §21.1.C.
> **(④) ★ #265 cabinet-agency (debt #132) = M — generalize the Gov-Actions library (#20) to per-cabinet-officer.** Design it
> TOGETHER with #20 (one action-library abstraction scoped per-office: governor / AG / SecState / SecDef / …); rides the content
> engine (the action catalogs ARE #221 primitives, gated by #258). Content-engine-track, NOT a keystone. game-mechanics §11.3.A.
> **(⑤) ★ #268/#269 = the EXPANSIONISM-track engine core (M each), DOWNSTREAM of the content + territory systems (debt #133).**
> The admit/statehood HALF already ships (`admitState` + `expansionStates.ts` + `TerritoriesPage`) — these are the parts with NO
> engine home: **#268** per-territory occupation-status + occupy→annex/independence/client-state transitions + hypothetical-nation
> recognition (depends on the war system #45/#56 to SET occupation + the #107/debt-#128 diplomacy data-model for recognition);
> **#269** state-FISSION (depends on apportionment #34/#55/#219) + player-legislated secession-right/abolish-states amendments
> (depends on making `ConstitutionalArticles` mutable, #242/debt #102). Secession ships ONLY as the scripted 1856 `secession-winter`
> event today (`phaseRunners.ts:2836`). game-mechanics §17.6.2, §17.6.3.
> **(⑥) ★ #221 RNG primitive + precedent-flags + program-lifecycle = content-engine EXTENSIONS (debt #134).** The
> roll-for-effect Gov-Action (Military tech-testing) MUST route through seeded `src/rng.ts` — which is NOT actually seeded today
> (`rng.ts:1-5`; debt #3 must land first/with it). The Crimes obscenity timeline (Hicklin→Roth→Miller) needs a persistent
> precedent-flag store beyond the fire-once `EraEvent`; the shipped SCOTUS is a coin-flip (`phaseRunners.ts:3397-3414`, debt
> #18) with no precedent flag (#52). Healthcare's Create→Expand→Cut needs a program-state model. All are #221/#258 extensions,
> not keystones.
>
> **Cheap-fixes-lane delta this batch:** the **#266 meter-floor SPILL-OVER fix (debt #130, QW–S)** joins the lane (small,
> independent, high systemic-balance value — IF spill-over is chosen over the clock-blocked countdown variant). Everything else
> is bigger or downstream: #267 (L, post-launch war track); #265 (M, content-engine track); #268/#269 (M each, expansionism

> **★★★ Batch-41 lead block (5-thread CONTENT/DESIGN batch, NO playtest, NO historian — `964b8857`-future-sc-cases [authoring] /

> profile; the app subsumes that once the content engine (#258/#221) + a real court ship.
> **★ THE MILESTONE (lead with it) — ★★ CORRECTED by batch 42, the corpus is NOT closed.** `2cddc161` opened "this is the
> last policy section" and Welfare looked like the 12th-and-last genre, but that was a snapshot of the FIRST authoring pass, NOT
> the final count: **batch 42 adds Regulations + Banking + a Currency subtype** (`d4cd2ee6`/`beb258f9`), the threads route a
> Stock-Market set to a **Trade** genre and reference still others, and vcczar himself (`beb258f9` POST 10) calls the
> genre/subtype taxonomy "more complex than the pokemon type chart" — open-ended, larger than any fixed count (Currency +
> Copyright were already named at #237's origin). So restate it as: **the MAJOR genres are largely captured but the corpus is
> NOT closed — Regulations, Banking, Currency, Trade, … remain.** The CONCLUSION is UNCHANGED and actually STRENGTHENED:
> the remaining policy work is NOT new content authoring — it is the 0%-shipped CONTENT ENGINE (#221 registry + #258 predicate
> field) + per-genre tagging/coverage. **The #221/#258 content-engine build is the DOMINANT remaining lever REGARDLESS — now
> even more so, since more authored content keeps arriving and keeps waiting on it.** game-mechanics §14.1.3.h, §14.1.3.i.
> **Bottom line: NO new keystone, NO re-sequence; top of queue UNCHANGED** (QW0 → K0/K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1).
>
> **★ THE PRIORITY BUILD-STATE DETERMINATIONS (the task's headline verifications):**
> **(1) ★ #270 SC-case schema + generator → 0% shipped; the shipped court is a coin-flip; the #115 name DB it reuses DOES ship.**
> `runPhase_2_5_3_Court` (`phaseRunners.ts:3397-3415`) is `chance(0.5)` → 4 hardcoded title strings (`:3399-3404`) → headcount
> ruling (`:3407-3409`) → `partyPreference ±0.1` (`:3412-3413`). **NO `ScotusCase` type, NO yes/no `question`, NO `{ifYes,ifNo}`
> direction-keyed faction-effect payload, NO generator, NO Landmark/tier field, NO precedent overturn/reinstate links.** The
> `pendingCourtCases: SupremeCourtCase[]` field EXISTS (`types.ts:1587`) but is **dead** — never written/read by the court phase.
> The generator's two inputs ARE available: the #115 human-name DB (`public/standard-draft-classes.json`, `loadStandardDraftClasses`
> `standardDraftClasses.ts:13`) + the scenario state roster. This is the **BIGGEST SCOTUS-content deliverable** — it replaces the
> coin-flip with a real docket (debt #135, M-L).
> **(2) ★ #271 elected judiciary → 0% shipped; it adds a NEW ELECTION TYPE.** No elected-judge / vacancy-election / retention-ballot
> path exists; justices are appointed+confirmed only. `ElectionResult.type` is the closed union `'presidential' | 'senate' |
> 'house' | 'governor'` (`types.ts:1525`) — a retention ballot is a genuinely NEW type (single-candidate up/down tally, distinct
> from the multi-candidate margin model). Court SIZE is also fixed (`supremeCourtIds: string[]`, no court-packing variable) and
> `OfficeType` is a CLOSED union (`types.ts:1111-1134`, only ChiefJustice/AssociateJustice — lower courts would extend it, #66)
> (debt #136, M, downstream of the elections engine + #251).
> **(3) ★★ #4/#171 faction-default — CRITICAL DIGEST CORRECTION. A SOFT, scenario-LIMITED per-faction ideology-priority draft
> restriction ACTUALLY SHIPS (the `8189b724` digest's "no ideology gate / grep → ZERO" claim is WRONG).** `Faction`
> carries an `eligibleIdeologies: Ideology[]` field — **typed locally in `factions1772.ts:7` (NOT on the `Faction` interface,
> `types.ts:1293`)** — populated for all **10 1772 factions** (e.g. `fact_blue_lw_1772 → ['LW Populist','Progressive']`) and
> ZERO in `factions1856.ts`. The draft CONSUMES it: `pickBestForFaction` (`phaseRunners.ts:33-53`) applies it **ONLY when
> `scenarioId==='1772' && year===startYear`** (`:38-39`, the inaugural/expansion draft), as a **SOFT two-tier preference** —
> `strict = eligible.filter(in eligIdeos); pool = strict.length>0 ? strict : eligible` (`:42-43`) + a `+50` score nudge (`:49`).
> **That IS exactly #4's "priority pool first, fall back when exhausted" shape — so the mechanic is partially built, just
> 1772-inaugural-ONLY, NOT era-keyed, NOT on 1856, NOT a per-era "FactNumber" table, and the field isn't on the canonical type.**
> What #4/#171 still need: generalize `eligibleIdeologies` onto the `Faction` interface + populate it for 1856/all eras + a
> per-era "FactNumber" profile (data) + apply it on EVERY draft (not just 1772-start) + the era-keyed ON/OFF toggle (#171/#263).
> **(4) ★ Faction COUNT — already 5/party (confirmed).** Both scenarios ship exactly **10 factions = 5 per party**
> (`factions1772.ts`/`factions1856.ts`, 10 `fact_` ids each; `partyId: 'BLUE'|'RED'`). The build already lives in default-mode
> (5/party); there is NO REDCPU/BLUECPU catch-all and NO 6-party alternate mode. The COUNT delta is resolved; #4's remaining
> data is the per-era FactNumber draft-profile, and #263's remaining work is the 6-party-alternate-mode TOGGLE. **#76
> charismatic-leader poach (10%/same-ideology/intra-party/cap-1-per-faction) is NOT built** (`Charismatic` is a bare trait,
> `types.ts:208`; no poach logic) — a small conversion sub-rule.
> **(5) ★ #38 relocation — the Relocate phase + alt-state + cap SHIP; the historical-date DATA + auto/random MODES do NOT.**
> `runPhase_2_1_4_Relocations` (`phaseRunners.ts:623-679`) ships: a one-shot seed-altState pass (`:628-642`), a manual player
> path (`attemptPlayerRelocation` `:614`), a CPU pass, the cap (`RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN=5` `types.ts:247`,
> `RELOCATIONS_CAP=200`), the Carpetbagger ladder (`:250`), and `altState`/`altStateSeeded` on `Politician` (`types.ts:1256-1257`).
> **NOT shipped:** historical-relocation-date DATA, an auto-move-at-date scheduler, the random-alt-state-via-general-event mode,
> the realism setting (#263, 0% built), and the auto-move officeholder-CONFLICT guard (the resolver simply skips pols with
> `currentOffice` `:571` — no cancel/resign-on-conflict logic). So #38 is a **data-layer + modes ADD on top of the shipped
> Relocate phase** (S-M); cross-ref #247/#260 (state lean/pop) + #263 (the realism/random/any-pol toggles).
>
> **(①) ★ #258 (the predicate field, debt #120) REMAINS the content-engine KEYSTONE — and the complete corpus makes the
> #221/#258 content-engine build the DOMINANT remaining lever.** Courts/Welfare are #237 genre instances whose `Preq:` blocks
> demand the predicate field + two NEW predicate KINDS this batch: **institution-FLAG** (Judicial-Review in-effect/not-active;
> Court-Packing active; SCOTUS exists/not) and **territory-ownership / expansion-extent** (US-controls-Philippines+Brazil+Mexico+
> Canada; at-MAX-expansion → gate the 21/31 justice counts, tying Courts to the expansion system #268). `Legislation`
> (`types.ts:1506-1520`) still has NO `subtype` and NO prereq/condition field (only the 4-value `committee`) → the field-add is
> unchanged (debt #120; joins debt #129/#134). With all 12 genres authored, this build is now what unblocks the largest body of
> waiting content. game-mechanics §14.1.3.h.
> **(②) ★ #270 SC-case schema+generator (debt #135) = the BIGGEST SCOTUS deliverable (M-L), on the SCOTUS track.** Replaces the
> coin-flip; reuses the #115 name DB (shipped); its rolls MUST route through seeded `src/rng.ts` (NOT seeded today, debt #3, so
> co-land with real seeding). Build order: `ScotusCase` type + live docket (finally use the dead `pendingCourtCases`) + yes/no
> ruling→direction-keyed effect onto the interest/faction layer (#50/#51) → generator (name DB + state-name list) → 5% Landmark
> + tier field (#249) → precedent overturn/reinstate links + in-force flag (#258 precedent KIND). Sibling to #271; pairs
> #52/#249/#218/#251. game-mechanics §22.7.aa.
> **(③) ★ #271 elected judiciary (debt #136) = M, DOWNSTREAM of the elections engine + #251.** The load-bearing build is the NEW
> retention/vacancy election TYPE (`ElectionResult`/`calcStateVote` need a single-candidate up/down retention tally); it COMPOSES
> with the 8-yr term-limit amendment (1/4-of-Court-per-cycle staggered election) and rides #20 (the elected-judge selector is a
> per-state Gov-Action). game-mechanics §22.7.bb.
> **(④) ★ #38 relocation (ENRICH) = S-M, a data-layer + modes ADD on the shipped Relocate phase.** Build: the historical-move-date
> DATA on pols, an auto-move scheduler + its officeholder-conflict guard (cancel-vs-resign is an OPEN human ruling), and the
> random-alt-state-via-event mode; the any-pol-relocate + realism toggles are #263. Cross-ref #247/#260.
> **(⑤) ★ #4/#171 faction-default (ENRICH) = the per-era "FactNumber" draft-profile DATA + the 6-party-alternate-mode TOGGLE.**
> 5/party already ships; a soft per-faction ideology-priority restriction already ships for the 1772-inaugural draft only
> (correction above) — generalize `eligibleIdeologies` onto the `Faction` type, populate per-era, apply on every draft, and add
> the era-keyed ON/OFF toggle (#171) + the 6-party alternate toggle (#263). **#76 charismatic-poach = a small conversion sub-rule**
> (leader-trait-triggered, 10%, intra-party, same-ideology, cap-1-per-allied-faction, with its immunity list).
>
> **Cheap-fixes-lane delta this batch: NONE.** Every batch-41 delta is content-engine-track (#258/#221 — the dominant lever),
> SCOTUS-track (#270 M-L / #271 M), or a data-layer-plus-modes add (#38 S-M / #4 data + toggle). Nothing is an XS independent
> quick-win. **★ ONE digest claim was CONTRADICTED by the codebase this batch: `8189b724`'s "the draft has NO ideology gate /
> grep eligibleIdeolog → ZERO" is WRONG — `eligibleIdeologies` ships (typed in `factions1772.ts:7`, populated for the 10 1772
> factions, consumed as a SOFT 1772-inaugural-only priority in `pickBestForFaction` `phaseRunners.ts:38-49`); the per-faction
> ideology-priority restriction is PARTIALLY built, not 0%.** All other findings confirmed: the SC court is the coin-flip
> (#270/#271 0% shipped), the Relocate phase ships but the historical-date data + auto/random modes do not (#38), 5/party already
> ships (#4 count resolved), #76 not built. **Corpus-coverage: 1840-start remains the top still-needed start year** (no playtest
> this batch). game-mechanics §30.31, §14.1.3.h, §22.7.aa, §22.7.bb, §6.2.y, §4.1.v.

> **★★★ Batch-42 lead block (5-thread CONTENT/DESIGN batch, NO playtest, NO historian — `d4cd2ee6`-regulations / `beb258f9`-banking

> [Mar-2022 brainstorm/ORIGIN]; FOUR new debt rows #137-#140; cross-ref NEW gap #272 (what-if entry) + earlier-origin folds onto
> #253/#270/#226/#206; verified against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-28).** **GM-replacement lens:** two MORE policy-genre drops + a
> benchmark-scorer origin + a SC-case-generator origin + the what-if-entry mechanic — all work the GM does by hand (authors policy
> content, scores eras at the boundary, synthesizes court cases, adjudicates which counterfactual pols enter play); the app subsumes
> it once the content engine (#258/#221) + the K3 per-era banking step + a real court ship.
> **★★ THE MILESTONE — CORRECTED (lead with it): the content corpus is NOT complete; the batch-41 "12-genre sweep done" claim is
> SOFTENED.** Batch 42 adds **Regulations + Banking + a Currency subtype** (`d4cd2ee6`/`beb258f9`), routes a Stock-Market set to a
> **Trade** genre, and vcczar himself (`beb258f9` POST 10) calls the genre/subtype taxonomy "more complex than the pokemon type
> chart" — open-ended (Currency + Copyright were already named at #237's origin). So restate the milestone as: **the MAJOR genres
> are largely captured but the corpus is NOT closed — Regulations, Banking, Currency, Trade, … remain.** **The CONCLUSION is
> UNCHANGED and STRENGTHENED: the #221/#258 content-engine build is still the DOMINANT remaining lever — now even more so, since
> more authored content keeps arriving and keeps waiting on it.** game-mechanics §14.1.3.i, §30.32. (The §9.1 lead-block milestone
> and the §9.6 batch-41 milestone are both softened to match.)
> **Bottom line: NO new keystone, NO re-sequence; top of queue UNCHANGED** (QW0 → K0/K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1).
>
> **★ THE PRIORITY BUILD-STATE DETERMINATIONS (the task's headline verifications, all codebase-confirmed):**

> pols are indistinguishable dataset rows; grep `whatif`/`playable`/`counterfactual` in `src/` finds ONLY the unrelated era-event
> spine (`eraEvents1772.ts`). No `ScriptedEvent` type/registry exists (draft = `runPhase_2_1_1_Draft`) → #221 0% shipped. The new
> surfaces: a draftable-but-not-yet-PLAYABLE state; an entry scripted-event with a WAR-GATED kill branch (kill majority gated on an
> active war / returns-to-politics minority → playable; resolves IN PLACE, vcczar rejected re-draft); condition-gated availability
> (Castro→Cuba, Tubman→suffrage, …) REUSING the era-event `Predicate`/`evalPredicate` (#258) on a NEW surface + NEW predicate kinds
> (suffrage-by-demographic, territory-held); and a Frail DE-TAG dataset rule (#226 earlier origin, `scripts/seedDataset.mjs`).
> Speculative-draft rules are OPEN ("I need to make draft rules for this," POST 1). Build on the content/draft track, NOT a
> standalone epic. game-mechanics §4.4.2 #272.
> **(2) ★★ Regulations + Banking + Currency → 0% shipped CONTENT-SCHEMA features the #221 engine must support (debt #138, S-M
> INSIDE the #221/K4 build).** `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has the 4-value `committee` and **NO `subtype` / prereq / level
> field**; there is **NO `Level N` / graduated-intensity construct, NO banking-institution-state model, NO currency-standard store**
> anywhere in `src/`. Three concrete new schema demands: the stateful **Banking institution-LIFECYCLE** (Bank→re-found→Bank-War-kill
> →Independent-Treasury→National→Fed→Glass-Steagall→repeal-by-GLB — the richest institution ladder in the KB, the bank twin of the
> Social-Security/Medicare entitlement ladders + the #66 office lifecycle); the **EPA graduated-intensity `Level 1/2/3` TIERS**
> (a stringency ladder DISTINCT from create→expand→cut scope); the **Currency SUBTYPE** (gold/silver/bimetalism/fiat, a #248
> mutually-exclusive set, GM-ruled sibling to Banking, POST 9-10) + Stock-Market→Trade routing (POST 6). The **`economic` meter the
> GLB `Recession`-or-worse prereq reads DOES ship** (`NationalMeters.economic`, `types.ts:1399-1407`) — but nothing wires proposal
> availability to a named meter tier (the #258 meter-threshold predicate gap; Banking is the most explicit `Preq:` evidence yet,
> ~9 blocks). **FOLD these into the engine build, NOT separate epics.** game-mechanics §14.1.3.i.
> **(3) ★ #253 benchmark era-end scorer → 0% built; a POINT-VALUE RESCALE DECISION must PRECEDE the build (debt #139).** No
> era-end benchmark scorer ships (grep `benchmark` = ZERO engine hits; `game.gameEnded` is a one-shot, not a per-era bank; the K3
> #68 point-banking home is unbuilt; no "party-leader faction" concept at era-end). The Dec-2021 `f012e5cc` ORIGIN settled
> **1,000/faction × 5 factions/party = a ~10,000-pt party swing** for **ONE benchmark/party/era** — but the 2022 `benchmarkupd`
> expanded to **~10 benchmarks/era**, and #253 says weights are still "TBD." **If each of ~10 still paid 1,000/faction the per-era
> swing balloons ~10×** → the per-benchmark value was almost certainly rescaled; reconcile the **1,000/faction (Dec-2021) vs
> ~10-benchmarks/era (2022)** rescale with vcczar/Ted BEFORE building (build against the wrong weight = re-tune the whole score
> economy). Also UNDEFINED: the **"party-leader factions"** that lose when neither party accomplishes its benchmark. The scorer
> itself is the K3 per-era banking step (debt #5; it IS the #102 dual-scoring scoreboard) and checkpoints AT era boundaries — so it
> is ALSO blocked on the era-table reconciliation (debt #116, now a 4th 15-era enumeration). game-mechanics §27.2.2.
> **(4) ★ #270 SC-case GENERATOR → design-origin pushed BACK to the Mar-2022 `07fa6116` brainstorm; NO re-sequence — refines debt
> #135 (debt #140).** The shipped court is the coin-flip (`phaseRunners.ts:3397-3415`, `chance(0.5)` → 4 hardcoded titles →
> headcount → `partyPreference ±0.1`); `pendingCourtCases` (`types.ts:1587`, `SupremeCourtCase` typed `:1548-1556`) is DEAD —

> **(5) ★ #206 era-table — a FOURTH cardinality (debt #116 updated).** The Dec-2021 `f012e5cc` benchmark ORIGIN is a **15-era**

> **★★★ Batch-43 change (4-thread batch: 1 GENUINE ERA-PLAYTEST [`06fbb2e5`-lars-and-ark, the first real era-run in many batches] + 1 DESIGN-SPEC [`ae435b5f`-fourteen-points-on-impeachment] + 1 POLL [`b73925a4`-flavor-event] + 1 SURVEY [`ddd179cb`-questions-for-playtesters]; FIVE new debt rows #141-#145; NEW gaps #273 [impeachment] + #274 [skill-gain accessibility]; bugs DH-81 [war-end cadence] + DH-82 [officer cap]; verified against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-28. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence.):**
> **GM-replacement lens:** a live war-cadence misrun + an impeachment design-spec + a flavor-poll + a balance survey — the app subsumes the manual-sim pain (the per-battle GM die-rolls, the hand-run impeachment trials that get VOIDED, the bookkeeping the §9.x port thesis #114 exists to kill) once the war model, the (ratified) impeachment subsystem, and the content engine ship.
> **★ THE PRIORITY BUILD-STATE DETERMINATIONS (codebase-confirmed; lead with the war finding):**
> **(1) ★★ DH-81 war-end cadence → the SHIPPED engine is ALREADY per-term (NOT per-battle); the digest's instant game-over is the MANUAL SIM, not the code (debt #141, S).** `runRevWarBattles` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:175`) runs ONCE per Military Phase 2.7 (`phaseRunners.ts:3593-3596`), and the win/loss check (`revolutionaryWar.ts:254-264`) is evaluated ONCE after the battle loop, NOT inside it; the per-battle do-while (`:211-236`, `d100()<=66` continuation) only increments tallies, and `revolutionaryWar.ts` never sets `gameEnded`/`triggersGameEnd` (grep=0). **So a single lost battle CANNOT immediately lose the war in shipped code** — the `06fbb2e5#POST 75` "United States has failed… after Trenton" is the Google-Sheets run (VOIDED live), GM+designer-confirmed against the SHEET. The REAL shipped gap: the count-threshold win-path (7 wins / 16 losses, `:16-18,254-264`) is the **stale per-battle-count model Ted says was REPLACED** by the war-score+momentum chart (§21.1.A). Fix = (a) [S, cheap-fix] swap the count-threshold WIN/LOSS conditions for the war-score chart + delete the stale columns (the per-term cadence is already correct; the 50/50 "another battle?" roll already exists as the `:236` continuation, ~66% — a tuning delta); (b) [M, post-launch] the full §21.1.A war-score model. game-mechanics §21.1.A (authoritative home).
> **(2) ★ DH-82 officer cap → an unclamped DERIVED battle-target, not a stored-stat violation; a one-line clamp QW (debt #142, XS).** No-SecWar "Planning" = `general.skills.military * 2` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:212`) feeds the d100 target unclamped → reads 6 with a 3-Mil officer (`06fbb2e5#POST 73-74`); the naval sum at `:187` is similarly unclamped. The PERSISTED 0–5 skills ARE clamped (`abilities.ts:6-30`) — so this is a derived combat coefficient over-buffing the no-cabinet case, not a corrupted stat. Fix = wrap in `Math.min(5,…)` (+ the one-line design call: does the no-SecWar fallback INTEND to exceed 5?). game-mechanics §21.1.A.
> **(3) ★★ #273 impeachment → 0% shipped AND DECISION-GATED; a LARGE subsystem that vcczar must RATIFY first (debt #143, L, BLOCKED).** `impeach` (case-insensitive) = 0 occurrences in `src/`; no proposal-to-impeach, no Judicial-Committee article step, no House/Senate trial pipeline, no resign-at-any-stage, no Controversial gate, no `game.impeachment` state. vcczar DEFERRED the entire `ae435b5f` thread (POST 47/48: *"I'm not going to have time. Remind me when Anthony gets closer"*) → the spec is DESIGN-INTENT-IN-PROGRESS, **NOT canon — DO NOT build before ratification.** ★ THE DEPENDENCY EDGE: #273 is the plumbing **#264 (coup→treason-trials) reuses** and the system **#112 (modern institutional layer) instantiates** — so #273 GATES both; it also rides #221 (event auto-fire) + #258 (trait/precedent gates). The broken-impeachment flags DH-33/DH-54/DH-66 (§24.1.1) now CONSOLIDATE under it as symptoms of the same null subsystem. game-mechanics §24.1.1a (#273 spec home), §20.12.
> **(4) ★ #274 skill-gain accessibility → 0% shipped; a BALANCE lever on the career-track/skill economy (debt #144, M).** The career-track skill ROLL is a FLAT `chance(CAREER_ODDS.skill)=0.5` (`phaseRunners.ts:299-318`, `types.ts:229-234`) — NOT stat-gated. The TRAP is the SEAT-gated paths around it: CPU track assignment shunts a floor-stat pol to its least-bad skill (`bestAvailableTrack`, `:392-399`), tracks cap at 5/faction (`CAREER_TRACK_CAP`, `types.ts:237`) so good tracks fill, and the committee/cabinet skill-ups (`OFFICE_ADMIN_GRANT`, `types.ts:597-605`) need a WON seat that PV gates behind the missing skills → ShortKing/Euri's chicken-and-egg. There is NO non-electoral, non-track Admin/Legis growth source for sub-floor pols. Distinct from #163 (caps the TOP), #190 (election modifier), #252 (committee granularity), #163-pre-placement (seeds the start cohort). Fix = a stat-floor-keyed non-electoral growth source (random-event grants / a low-office apprenticeship) that does NOT flatten the ladder. game-mechanics §5.5 (#274 home).
> **(5) ★ Flavor tier (#221/#261) → 0% shipped; POST-LAUNCH, rides #221 (debt #145, S).** `EraEvent` (`types.ts:1466-1482`) has NO `isFlavor` flag and NO per-event fire-probability; the only firing constant is the graph-wide `ERA_GRAPH_RULES.fireChance=0.85` (`types.ts:1104`) — NOT a flavor probability, toggle, or ≤40%/phase cap. The flavor on/off toggle + the ≤40%/phase cap + the `isFlavor` boolean are trivial INSIDE #221's event registry/scheduler, meaningless before it. game-mechanics §27.1.1, §14.1.3.e.
> **(6) ★ Playtester FOLDS — corroboration, NO re-sequence.** The `06fbb2e5` 1772 run is fresh 3rd-source proof of **#155** (the 1772 RevWar is brutally, repeatedly losable — Navy ~0 wins, momentum −7/−9 — so any war-hardening must keep the 1772 floor playable) and **#176** (MilPrep stuck at 2 the whole war despite the Army+Navy bills passing). The `ddd179cb` survey reaffirms the dominant direction without re-ordering keystones: **#114 — the Lingering manual-bookkeeping phase IS the "make it a computer game" PORT THESIS (the whole browser build's reason for being)**; **#262 — repeal-legibility (#175/#132) + too-few-Moderate bills**; #107/#267/#23/#20/#251 tuning asks. **★ Q9 STRENGTH CONSTRAINT (load-bearing for the port):** the legislative/Congress pipeline + primaries + elections are the **crown jewels** — the port must PRESERVE their depth/legibility, NOT automate them away (don't fold them into the bookkeeping the port removes). game-mechanics §30.33.
> **NO digest claim CONTRADICTED, with ONE important sharpening: the `06fbb2e5` "war-loss roll fires after EVERY battle → instant game-over" describes the MANUAL Google-Sheets sim, NOT the shipped engine — the SHIPPED loss-check is per-term (`revolutionaryWar.ts:254-264`, once per `runRevWarBattles`). The codebase delta the digest UNCOVERS is real but different: the shipped win-path is the STALE COUNT-THRESHOLD model (7/16), not the war-score+momentum chart the designer intends.** The DH-82 doubled-Planning=6, the #273 0%-impeach, the #274 flat-roll-but-seat-gated trap, and the flavor-flag absence all verified clean.

> **GM-replacement lens:** the changelog is the app's spec source-of-truth — it pins the cabinet-enthusiasm %s, the era-keyed retirement/death charts, and the Pres-implementation 2-step that the manual sim runs by hand and the build must encode deterministically. The 2.3 rework + the Kagan backlog + the Canada conquest are all the manual-process pain the app subsumes once appointments, the content engine, and a territory-acquisition mechanic ship.
> **★ CODE-VERIFIED BUILD-STATE (this batch's headline verifications — all codebase-confirmed; the digests assert, I confirm):**
> **(1) ★★ Appointments 2.3 = a thin DETERMINISTIC CPU auto-fill — NO confirmation vote, NO lobby→enthusiasm, cross-party gate is a FLAT 10%-relax / −3-penalty (NOT the designed ±1-ideology gate).** `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2224`) iterates `cabinetSeatsForYear` (`types.ts:1196`), and for each empty seat does: one `chance(CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_RATE)` roll (= `0.1`, `types.ts:1233`) to *relax* the same-party filter, then a pure `scoreCabinetCandidate` (`:2229-2241`, admin/governing/secondary/expertise, no RNG) with `CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_PENALTY` (= `-3`, `types.ts:1234`) added to cross-party scores, then takes `scored[0]` and writes `cabinet[seat]=pick.id` + `addLog("confirmed as ${seat}")` (`:2191-2198`). **There is NO Senate/committee vote, NO NAY/AYE, NO lobby→enthusiasm channel, and the cross-party gate is the flat 10%/−3 — NOT the ±1-ideology window the designer specced.** (Same site as debt #24/#30/#38 — this batch PINS the constants.) Both 2.3 phases are **skipped entirely in the independence era**: `phases.ts:89-90` (`if (phaseId === '2.3.1' || phaseId === '2.3.2') return false;`). **2.3.2 Military** (`phaseRunners.ts:2246+`) is a separate flat GiC auto-pick. game-mechanics §30.34, §25.5; gaps #124/#229/#275/#112.
> **(2) ★★ Canada — structure SHIPS, real data does NOT; no war-acquisition mechanic exists.** `src/data/expansionStates.ts` registers every Canadian province as a `region:'Canada'` annexable seed (ALB/MAN/NBR/NFL/NCN/NSC/ONT/QBC/SAS/VAN + PEI; "Central Canada" correctly ABSENT) — but the seed factory (`expansionStates.ts:173-183`) stamps **EVERY** expansion seed with placeholder `electoralVotes: 4, bias: 0, industries:{agriculture:2,trade:1}, isSlaveState:false` (uniform, not per-province). `admitState` (`territories.ts:8-23`) is a straight `{...seed, admissionYear, governorId:null, senators:[], representativeIds:[], isColony:false}` copy — **NO census/EV/bias computation at all.** There is **no acquisition-by-war/event path** (admit is via the Territories page / God Mode / era graph only). The curated draft fallback has **ZERO** Canada-region entries. Confirmed exactly as the digest claims. game-mechanics; gap #277.
> **(3) ★ The mid-game EV-MUTATION path any Canada-EV fix must not break — LOCATED.** The shipped EV-writes are: the **federalism slave-compromise plank** `constitutionalConvention.ts:210` (`s.electoralVotes = Math.max(3, (s.ccDelegateSlots ?? 2) + 1)` — the one engine path that REWRITES EVs at an era transition), the per-state EV *reads* in `calcStateVote`/EC tally (`phaseRunners.ts:3692` turnout scale, `:3767-3768` `blueEv/redEv += s.electoralVotes`), and the **God-Mode editable EV** in `StatesPage.tsx:14`. The Kagan thread's "events that change EVs mid-game" (`f64a522c` POST 6) are **authored EraEvent content on the forum census sheet, NOT shipped engine** — `EraEvent`/`applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3223` clamps meters `-5..5`) has no typed EV-delta effect today. So the EV-mutation "primitive" is design-intent (content), and the only live EV-write to preserve is the CC plank + the God-Mode editor. game-mechanics §17.3.y; gaps #34/#277.

NO new keystone, NO re-sequence — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1. ★ THE HEADLINE for the planner: ONE source (`97aa3f6f`) is an **AUTHORITATIVE designer CHANGELOG** — three already-LANDED rulings with canonical NUMBERS (not proposals) — and it lands those numbers on items the queue ALREADY sequenced: **Change 1** (military-preparedness → a FLAT per-tier battle bonus) sharpens the war track (debt #205 = #85 live war-casualty + #45/#56 greenfield war loop, debt #105/#124); **Change 2** (state-movement cap = 4 SUCCESSES/half-term, alt EXCLUDED) is a RE-MODEL of relocation (debt #38) that ESCALATES the standing BUG-0 (the "XS one-line `5→4`" framing is now WRONG — see the correction below); **Change 3** (election trait → 50%±1 / ability → 20%×ability) is a CONCRETE K0/#296 dependent that FOLDS into the #184/#292 election-rework epic + the debt-#1 `Math.random` die-fix (SAME `calcStateVote` lines). The other three substantive sources are net-new DESIGN with 0% shipped: **#330** congressional investigations (a greenfield L system — the designed PRODUCER of the dead `forceRetire` executor), **#331** the nomination era-selector + King Caucus (the human convention rulebook + the 12A P+VP ticket, on the empty 2.9.2 stub), **#329** the bundle/omnibus bill (a net-new bill TYPE atop the single-effect `Legislation`). ★★★ The build-state CORRECTIONS the planner needs: (1) **Change 1 is a NET-NEW coupling, not a TUNE** — the Rev-War combat resolver reads NO preparedness meter today (`grep meters.military revolutionaryWar.ts` = 0); the `military` `NationalMeter` feeds only era-events/display/gov-action, never the to-hit roll. When built it lands in the NEW FLAT form (add the tier bonus to `target`/`baseTarget+diffMod`). (2) **Change 2 ESCALATES BUG-0 from "XS one-line edit" to a RE-MODEL** — shipped counts ATTEMPTS (increment precedes the success roll), per-YEAR (yearly reset), alt-state INCLUDED — THREE semantic mismatches vs the ruling's 4-SUCCESSES/half-term/alt-EXCLUDED; there is no half-term success counter in `src/` (grep = 0). (3) **`forceRetire` is RESOLVED but ZERO templates emit it** (re-confirmed from a 3rd angle, QW49) — and **#330 is its designed PRODUCER**; the near-term #140 fix (add `{ kind: 'forceRetire' }` to the `scandal-federal-investigation` template `anytimeEvents.ts:258`, which already carries `Scandalous`+`pvHit`+`scandalScaled`) stands INDEPENDENT of the full #330 build. (4) **`vicePresidentId` is NEVER set by any election path** (re-confirmed) — the 12A ticket is entirely unmodeled; `runPhase_2_9_4` sets `presidentId` and never touches VP.

> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority; NO digest/gap-log/§30.48 claim contradicted on substance — ONE standing-row CORRECTION to flag: BUG-0's size framing):**
> - **★★ CHANGE 1 — military-preparedness→combat is ENTIRELY UNBUILT; it is a NET-NEW coupling (NOT a tune of an existing term). CONFIRMED.** The combat resolver (`revolutionaryWar.ts:187-218`) reads no preparedness meter: naval `target = (secWar.admin + admiral.military) + admiral.military*10; win = d100() ≤ target + 30` (`:187-191`); ground `baseTarget = planning + general.military*10 + (frenchAlliance ? 25 : 0)`, `diffMod ∈ {−20,0,+15}`, `win = d100() ≤ baseTarget + diffMod` (`:212-218`). **`grep meters.military src/engine/revolutionaryWar.ts` = 0.** `military` IS a real `NationalMeters` field (`types.ts:1402`, label "Military Preparedness" `Meter.tsx:8`) but combat ignores it — it feeds only era-event modifiers (`phaseRunners.ts:2925,2928`), the `milPower` display/threshold (`:3602`), and a "use the army" gov-action (`:3638`). → there is NO preparedness term in combat to tune; the canonical NEW FLAT ladder (9 banded tiers −10…+20, immediate-defeat branch DELETED) must be ADDED to `target` at `:191` and `baseTarget+diffMod` at `:218`. **Sharpens war-casualty debt #205 (#85, the live `applyCasualties` tune+determinism row) + the greenfield 1856+ war loop (#45/#56, debt #105/#124).**
> - **★★ CHANGE 2 — relocation: shipped is ATTEMPTS/YEAR/alt-INCLUDED; ruling is 4-SUCCESSES/HALF-TERM/alt-EXCLUDED — a 3-axis RE-MODEL. CONFIRMED, and it CORRECTS BUG-0's size.** `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` (`types.ts:247`). The accumulator increments the per-faction count at `phaseRunners.ts:579` **before** the success roll at `:584-585` (so a FAILED move still consumes budget = ATTEMPTS, not successes); the counter resets when `g.relocationAttempts.year !== g.year` (`:536`) = **per-YEAR**, not half-term; alt-state has **NO special-case** at the cap gate (`:577`) — `altState` is consumed only on success (`:585`) and only affects the odds, so an alt move STILL counts. There is **no half-term success counter** in `src/` (grep = 0; `halfTermSummary.ts` tracks deaths/retirements, not moves). → honoring POST 4 is a re-model: a per-faction **success** counter scoped to the **half-term**, capped at **4**, with **alt-state excluded** — NOT the const flip the BUG-0 row plans. **Sharpens #38; ⚠ CORRECTS the standing BUG-0 / divergence #9 row** (`technical-guide.md:1903`, which says "One-line edit: `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 4` + a guard"; that closes only the VALUE axis — the unit (attempts→successes) + window (year→half-term) mismatches remain, so BUG-0 is now S, not XS).
> - **★★ CHANGE 3 — election scoring is on a THIRD model (deterministic fixed-point bands + STILL opponent-conditional + NO ability term); the new 50%±1 / 20%×ability rolls REQUIRE seeded `chance` and supersede the `:3711` jitter. CONFIRMED.** `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723`) score = `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()−0.5)*8` (`:3709-3711`), with `traitBonus` from `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts:16-39`). Three divergences from the ruling: (a) **traits are DETERMINISTIC fixed points** — `TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS = {SMALL:2,MEDIUM:4,LARGE:8}` (`types.ts:721-725`) added whenever held, no roll (e.g. Charismatic-presGeneral = flat **+4** every time vs the ruling's **50% chance of +1**); (b) **opponent-conditional rows STILL EXIST** — exactly what the ruling removes: `applyTraitElectionBonus` swaps to `bumpedMagnitude` when an opponent matches (`electionEffects.ts:29-32`; Integrity SMALL→MEDIUM vs Scandalous/Controversial/Corrupt, `types.ts:748-758`); (c) **NO Gov/Legis-ability term** in the state-vote scorer (`grep skills.governing|skills.legislative` over `:3685-3723` = 0). **★ The `Math.random()` jitter is at `phaseRunners.ts:3711`** (re-confirmed = the flagship determinism leak, debt #1); the rebuild's 50% / 20%×ability rolls **must use seeded `chance(...)` (K0/#296)** and the ±8 jitter is **superseded entirely** by the per-trait rolls. **Sharpens #189 (trait-scoring owner) / #292 (ability-input); CONFIRMS #287/#296 (the `Math.random` offender, line-anchored). FOLDS into the #184/#292 election-rework epic (debt #184) — SAME `calcStateVote` lines.**

The debt-#1 `Math.random` jitter removal at `:3711` co-lands in the same touch (XS). **Order: K0/#296 → the #184/#292 election rework (now incl. Change-3's 50%±1 traits + 20%×ability term + opponent-conditional removal).**
> 2. **★★ CHANGE 2 (relocation 4-successes/half-term, debt #237) ESCALATES BUG-0 — re-classify it from XS to S, but still DO IT FIRST among the relocation work.** The standing BUG-0 row (`technical-guide.md:1903`) plans a one-line `5→4`; the ruling re-models the UNIT (attempts→successes), the WINDOW (year→half-term), and alt-state EXCLUSION — three changes the const flip does not cover. Scope alongside party-switch #302/#203 (same dataset/era-model surface).

It rides the war-balance cluster (debt #205 = #85, debt #105/#124 = #45/#56); route any new roll through K0 first (the casualty picker `:89,:97` still uses `Math.random`, debt #2/#106). **Order: K0 → the #45/#56 war loop + #205 casualty tune (Change-1 lands inside this).**

Neither moves the queue.

> **★★★ Batch-57 change (5-DIGEST batch, all DIGEST-ONLY — `0ff5e582`-cpu-governor-action-selection/#20+#328+#294 (+#323+#296), `2bb66197`-futures-so-bright-event-chains/#206+#221+#258+#261, `326c33dd`-disgraced-trait/#327+#112+#130+#140+#216, `0dc883c4`-menendez-illicit-prison/#140+#216, `4df5941d`-ousted-incumbency-advantage/#287+#297; I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-30 and am the build-state authority; FIVE new debt rows #231-#235 (continue after b56's max #230); references this batch's NEW gap rows #327 (Disgraced trait system) + #328 (CPU gov-action selection structure) + sharpens #20/#294/#206/#221/#258/#261/#112/#130/#140/#216/#287/#297/#323/#296; updated game-mechanics §30.47. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1. ★ This batch adds NO shipped behavior; every delta is DESIGN-ONLY (0% shipped OR a designed-vs-shipped divergence), re-verified. FOUR of the five sources are DESIGN-Q&A / suggestion / feature-request threads; only `2bb66197` narrates any play (a phases-skipped Era-of-the-Future SPEED-RUN to test event chains). ★★ THE HEADLINE for the planner: the ONE genuinely-buildable item this batch is **#328 — the CPU GOV-ACTION SELECTOR** (the multi-bucket %, trait-gated, expertise-as-guide, industry/election-law/special-passion structure that REPLACES the shipped flat-30% governing-skill `bias` stub). It is the **governor-action TWIN of the #323 CPU bill-scorer**, and it RIDES three prerequisites: the #20 gov-action LIBRARY (the action set the selector picks from — itself a greenfield L epic) + the governor-trait/expertise WIRING + a designer call on the **#294 DIRECTION CONFLICT** — and sits behind K0/#296 (every bucket roll is seeded RNG). ★★★ The build-state corrections the planner needs: (1) **the predicate/prereq-gating MACHINERY is already SHIPPED** (`Predicate` + `evalPredicate` + `GraphNode.precondition`) — but deployed in the **1772 era graph ONLY** (35 preconditions; 1856 + both anytime pools = 0); so #258 is now a **CONTENT-AUTHORING REQUIREMENT** (extend gating to 1856/anytime/future), NOT a new engine epic. (2) **`forceRetire` is RESOLVED in the engine** (`phaseRunners.ts:2723-2728`) but carried by **ZERO templates** — so the #140 scandal→removal near-term fix is purely adding `{ kind: 'forceRetire' }`/`death` to a scandal template's `effects[]`, no new mechanism. (3) the code's `incumbencyAdvantage` (`types.ts:462`, `phaseRunners.ts:2070`) is the **faction-leader CHALLENGE**, NOT electoral incumbency — do not conflate with #287/#297.

> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority — TWO digest discrepancies to flag at the gate; all brief pins + game-master §30.47 + gap rows #327/#328 confirmed against `src/` HEAD; NO substantive contradiction):**
> - **★★ CPU GOVERNOR-ACTION = a flat 30% governing-skill `bias` nudge — CONFIRMED, reads NO traits/expertise/cards/crisis/industry (#20/#328).** `runPhase_2_5_2_Governors` (`phaseRunners.ts:3382-3392`) is the ENTIRE governor turn: 11 lines; for each state with a governor, `chance(0.3)` (seeded) → `s.bias` nudged by `(gov.skills.governing − 1) * 0.05`, signed by party (`BLUE` −, else +), `clamp(..., -5, 5)` (`:3389`). It reads ONLY `gov.skills.governing` and `gov.partyId`. NO 25/25/50 rule, NO trait/expertise gate, NO industry/election-law/special-passion path. The building blocks DO ship as data/infra — `Trait` union incl. Integrity/Controversial/Manipulative (`types.ts:62-117`), the 19-tag `Expertise` axis (`:182-192`, `EXPERTISE` array `:188-192`), `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` (`:398-414`), convention-only `termLimits` (`:1396`), and seeded `chance`/`pick`/`pickWeighted` (`phaseRunners.ts:9`) — so #328 is WIRING existing pieces into a new selector, not greenfield infra. The selector still needs the #20 action LIBRARY to pick from (that IS greenfield).
> - **★★ #294 DIRECTION CONFLICT — CONFIRMED. The shipped `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` runs lobby⇒industry; the thread wants industry⇒lobby.** The ONLY consumer of `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` is the 2.1.8 alignment-drift pass (`phaseRunners.ts:1638-1656`): for each state, each faction with a local member, each held lobby card bumps the matching `s.industries[key]` +1 (capped at 5, `:1649`). That is **held-lobby-card ⇒ state-industry +1** — the REVERSE of the thread's **governor-improves-industry ⇒ matching-lobby-card-POINTS** (Agriculture→Big-Ag, Finance→Wall-Street). Same table (`LOBBY_INDUSTRY` `types.ts:398`), opposite causal arrow; the gov-action use case is unbuilt. ⚠ This is a DESIGNER CALL (industry⇒lobby, lobby⇒industry, or both-directional) before the gov-action-industry-expansion path is built.
> - **★★ FUTURE era / scenario — CONFIRMED 0% (#206); meters all present incl. Earth's-health=`planet` (#221).** `Era = 'independence' | 'federalism' | 'nationalism' | 'modern'` (`types.ts:1337`) — **NO `future` value**; only `scenario1772.ts` + `scenario1856.ts` exist (the `'future'`/"future" grep hits are flavor strings in `expansionStates.ts:5`/`SettingsPage.tsx` only). All 7 `NationalMeters` are modeled (`types.ts:1399-1407`) including **`planet` (=Earth's-health, no new meter needed)**; `partyPreference` is the separate top-level field. So the future-era CONTENT is missing but the meter substrate it targets is complete.
> - **★★ PREDICATE-GATING MACHINERY shipped, deployed 1772-ONLY — CONFIRMED (#258 is now a CONTENT-AUTHORING requirement, not a new engine epic).** The serializable `Predicate` tree (`types.ts:1487-1504`) + interpreter `evalPredicate` (`engine/eraGraph.ts:12`) + the optional `GraphNode.precondition` (`eraEvents1772.ts:25`) are real and shipped; selection gates on it at `eraGraph.ts:114`. **BUT it is deployed in the 1772 era graph ONLY:** `grep -c precondition` → `eraEvents1772.ts` = **35**, `eraEvents1856.ts` = **0**, `anytimeEvents.ts` = **0**, `anytimeNationalEvents.ts` = **0**. The GM's two no-prereq complaints (half-the-animals-extinct firing twice; citizens-in-space with no enabling state) are the exact symptom of un-gated content. Delta = extend the EXISTING machinery to 1856 + both anytime pools + future content (a once-only/`eventCompleted` guard for the repeat-firing case), as an authoring DISCIPLINE — corroborates the b40-era #60/DH-60 territory-gating finding from a 4th angle.
> - **★★ "Disgraced"/"Illicit"/"Disharmonious" ABSENT from the trait union; impeachment 0%; retirement age-only; Integrity-d6 not categorical (#327/#112/#130/#216) — CONFIRMED.** `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117`) = **55 traits** (awk count of `| '...'` lines); `grep disgrac|illicit|disharmon src/` = **0**, `grep impeach src/` = **0**, `grep expel src/` = **0**. Retirement is purely age-driven: `runPhase_2_4_1_Deaths` (`phaseRunners.ts:2351-2380`) rolls `retireRateFor(p.age) * cfg.retireMult` — NO scandal/unpopularity/impeachment input (so vcczar's "already have most with retirement" describes the FORUM game, not the build). Integrity does NOT categorically block disgrace: `Integrity:'Corrupt'` is a `TRAIT_CONFLICTS` pair (`types.ts:663-664`) with `conflictD6Threshold:4` (`:638`), so during a scandal-scaled event `tryGrantTrait(p,'Corrupt')` displaces on a **d6**, logged "Integrity weathers the scandal — Corrupt does not take, on a d6" (`phaseRunners.ts:2746-2750`) — probabilistic, scandal-specific, NOT a 100% lock.
> - **★★ `forceRetire` RESOLVED in the engine but ZERO templates carry it — CONFIRMED (the #140 near-term fix is purely additive).** The effect kind is EXECUTED — `case 'forceRetire': p.retiredYear = ...; recordRetirement(...); markPoliticianRetired(...)` (`phaseRunners.ts:2723-2728`), and the effect-walker logs `retireCause:'event'` when a template has it (`:2767,:2775`). But `grep forceRetire src/data/anytimeEvents.ts` = the **type declaration only** (`:28`) — **no template's `effects[]` includes it**. `death` is wired to exactly 2 templates, both violence/accident (small-plane-crash `:170`, assassination `:236`); the scandal-* templates (240-386) grant `Scandalous`/`Corrupt` + `pvHit` and never remove. So vcczar's "random events that can remove controversial pols already exist" (`0dc883c4` POST 4) describes the DESIGNED game; the BUILD has the removal PRIMITIVE wired-and-working but no scandal/corruption→removal EVENT — the fix is adding `{ kind: 'forceRetire' }`/`{ kind: 'death' }` to a scandal template's `effects[]` + a trigger probability.
> - **★★ INCUMBENCY — `calcStateVote` has NO incumbency/prior-office term; `Politician` has no ousted flag; the code's `incumbencyAdvantage` is the faction-leader CHALLENGE (#287/#297) — CONFIRMED.** `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723`) score = `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8` (`:3709-3711`) — NO incumbency, NO prior-office, NO returning-candidate term (a returning officeholder gets only office-weighted `pv*0.1`). `grep ousted|priorSeat|previousOffice|lostElection|officeHistory src/` = **0** — `Politician` (`types.ts:1251-1291`) has `currentOffice` only. The ONLY `incumbencyAdvantage` in code is `LEADERSHIP_RULES.eraConfig.*.incumbencyAdvantage` (`types.ts:462/466/470/474/479`, values 30/20/15/8 by era), the faction-leader-challenge tenure term consumed at `phaseRunners.ts:2070` + `FactionLeaderPage.tsx` — intra-faction leadership, NOT electoral seat incumbency. NOTE the `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` at `:3711` is the pre-existing determinism leak (debt #1, folds into K0).
> - **⚠ TWO DIGEST DISCREPANCIES TO FLAG AT THE GATE (neither changes substance):** (1) **trait-count drift between digests** — `326c33dd` says the `Trait` union is **55** traits and `0dc883c4` says **60**; the code is **55** (awk count of `| '...'` lines over `types.ts:62-117`). Both agree "Disgraced"/"Illicit" are absent; only the roster size differs. Use **55**. (2) the b56 lead already records `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` consumer at `phaseRunners.ts:1638-1656` (b56 cited it as the alignment-drift bump); this batch's #294 reading is consistent — the SAME pass, just read for its direction. No conflict, noted for cross-ref hygiene.

> **★★★ Batch-56 change (5-DIGEST batch, all DIGEST-ONLY — `70a67176`-sc-nomination-confirmation/#66+#270+#326+#20 (+#52+#275), `0af55af3`-party-shift-inevitability/#4+#171+#298+#302 (+#314+#306+#263), `b15af728`-game-start-date/#86+#115+#186+#92 (+#263), `2b729148`-california-uc-lc-split/#325 (+#294+#295+#34+#55+#313), `92202e9a`-jeannette-rankin-suffrage/#238+#258 (+DH-14); I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-30 and am the build-state authority; SIX new debt rows #225-#230 (continue after b55's max #224); references this batch's NEW gap rows #325/#326 + sharpens #66/#270/#20/#4/#171/#298/#302/#314/#306/#263/#86/#115/#186/#92/#294/#295/#34/#238/#258/DH-14; updated game-mechanics §30.46. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1. ★ This batch adds NO shipped behavior; every delta is a DESIGNER-DECISION (open A/B/C fork) or a bounded ADDITION/FOUNDATIONAL-prereq onto already-shipped engines — re-verified. ALL FIVE sources are DESIGN-Q&A / poll / deep-dive threads (no historian ran the batch). ★★ THE HEADLINE for the planner: TWO of this batch's items are DESIGNER-DECISIONS that must route to vcczar BEFORE any build — (A) the **SC auto-confirm-vs-always-vote fork** (`70a67176` POST 2/5-9; unresolved in-thread, weight leans always-vote; EITHER/OR-coupled to the automated court-DECISIONS #270 — if you always-vote, you can't also auto-decide), and (B) the **post-1772 start-phase-entry A/B/C** (`b15af728`; team leans B = begin at 2.1.2). NEITHER is buildable until the user picks. Everything else is a bounded addition or a foundational prereq — see the size ledger. ★★★ The build-state corrections the planner needs: (1) the party-shift INEVITABILITY ENGINE is SHIPPED (`factionCenter` + `resolveIdeologyShift`, NOT 0%); only its GATING is unbuilt. (2) the 1856 scenario is the DEFAULT New-Game and boots as a HYBRID (enters 2.1.2, pre-seated incumbents) — the thread's "only 1772 ships" is STALE.
>
> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority — FIVE line-drift / path mis-citations to flag at the gate, plus all 5 digests' shipped-state claims re-confirmed; NO digest/gap-log/§30.46 CLAIM contradicted on substance):**
> - **★★ SC nomination-CONFIRMATION — CONFIRMED auto-pick, NO vote, NO age floor.** Justices are auto-REPLACED on retirement, never nominated-by-faction and never voted: the reactive filter is `!currentOffice && !deathYear && !retiredYear && partyId === president.partyId && skills.judicial >= 2`, sorted by highest `judicial`, top one seated and logged "confirmed" (`phaseRunners.ts:3661-3667`). `grep` confirms this is the ONLY justice-pick site. Retirement is `age >= 75 && chance(0.15)` (`:3655`) — `age>=75` only TRIGGERS the vacancy, it NEVER gates the appointee, so a 20-yr-old J2 extremist is seatable TODAY (the live ★7 bug + #326). The CABINET "mirror" is likewise auto-SCORED: `CABINET_SEAT_SCORING[seat]` (`:2167`) → `scoreCabinetCandidate` + cross-party penalty → sort → `pick = scored[0]` set into seat + logged "confirmed" (`:2191-2198`), with NO confirmation vote. Court DECISIONS = a 50% coin-flip `chance(0.5)` (`runPhase_2_5_3_Court` `:3397`) over 4 hardcoded case titles (`:3398-3403`). So both the cabinet "mirror" #66 wants AND the SC nomination it would extend are un-voted today — #66 (the cabinet committee→floor vote) is the unbuilt root.
> - **★★ PARTY-SHIFT INEVITABILITY ENGINE — CONFIRMED SHIPPED (correcting any "0%" read).** The ideology-drift-toward-faction-center engine is BUILT: `factionCenter(snap, factionId)` (`phaseRunners.ts:704-727`, living-member mean ideology index + expertise-lean bias, clamped 0-6) + `resolveIdeologyShift` (`:768-794`, runs only in phase `2.1.5`, drifts a subject toward the ACTOR faction's center via `stepToward`, one attempt/subject/turn, capped per turn). This IS the mechanism that makes the modern shift "practically guaranteed" (the digest's claim). What is UNBUILT is the GATING around it: the era-banded `eligibleIdeologies` ships **1772-inaugural-ONLY** — per-faction `eligibleIdeologies: Ideology[]` (`factions1772.ts:12-22`, e.g. `fact_red_lw_1772` :19) read by `getEligibleIdeologies` (`:30`) behind the `isExpansion1772` gate (`scenarioId === '1772' && year === startYear`, `:38`); the 1892/2012 columns of the eligible-ideology MATRIX are absent. `grep canBeIndependent`=0 and `grep draftRestriction`=0 across `src/` — neither the starting-party draft gate, the "can be independent" tag, nor the restriction settings toggle exists.
> - **★ START-DATE / SCENARIO-BOOT — CONFIRMED 1856 ships, as a HYBRID, as the DEFAULT (correcting "only 1772 ships").** `scenario1856.ts` builds `phaseId: '2.1.2'` (`:153`, "start past draft for first turn"), with incumbents PRE-SEATED from the seed roster (president/VP/cabinet/court/general found by `currentOffice.type`, `:123-141`), `constitutionRatified: true` + `governorsExist: true` + `currentEra: 'nationalism'`. `NewGameScreen.tsx:25` defaults `useState<ScenarioId>('1856')` — 1856 is the DEFAULT New-Game scenario, so the thread's "only 1772 ships" (POST 6) is STALE. Each scenario HARDCODES its own entry phase in its builder (`scenario1772.ts:73`='2.1.1', `scenario1856.ts:153`='2.1.2'); there is NO configurable A/B/C boot and no shared entry-phase constant. The 1856 boot matches NEITHER poll option A (start at draft) NOR B (start at 2.1.2-with-faction-leader): it skips the draft AND there is no 2.2.4 Party-Leaders step before nationalism play (2.2.4 is independence-skipped, `phases.ts:86-87`), so the shipped hybrid needs reconcile against whatever A/B/C the designer picks.
> - **★ CALIFORNIA UC/LC SPLIT-STATE — CONFIRMED 0% shipped; `altState` is NOT it.** CA and TX exist as SINGLE states only: `tx` (`states1856.ts:24`, EV=4) / `ca` (`:35`, EV=4); no `upper_cal`/`lower_cal`/`west_texas`/`north_texas` anywhere in `src/`. The ~150-entry annexable registry (`expansionStates.ts`) has Mexican/western carve-ups but NO CA/TX split, and stamps a flat hardcoded `electoralVotes: 4` on every expansion state (`:178`). `State` (`types.ts`) has no `parentState`/`splitOf` field; no pol→split assignment of any kind (no die, no quartile roll, no ≥4-core-office fill). ⚠ `altState` (`types.ts:1256`) is the UNRELATED relocation/carpetbagger secondary-home field — region-distance-seeded once per pol (`altStateSeeded`) and consumed by `runPhase_2_1_4_Relocations` (`phaseRunners.ts:546,554,588,626-642,657-659`). Do NOT conflate it with the split-state system. (The `latin_columbia`/`LCO` entry `expansionStates.ts:46` is Latin America — a coincidental "LC" abbreviation, not Lower California.)
> - **★ JEANNETTE-RANKIN SUFFRAGE — CONFIRMED NO gender model at all.** `grep -i 'gender|female|suffrage|womens'` across `src/` = ZERO. `Politician` (`types.ts:1251`) and `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780`) carry no gender/sex attribute; there is no per-state women's-suffrage flag and no `women`/`suffrage`/`19th` in `src/data/`. The additive electoral-bias channel that COULD carry the proposed −5 exists: `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3684`) sums a `traitBonus` from `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS`/`TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS` (`types.ts:738`). The existing DH-14 row (`technical-guide.md:1920`, `drums` — "bill ideology impacts not era-aware → Women's Suffrage in 1916 is era-wrong") is the suffrage-adjacent content debt this gender prereq sits beside.
> - **⚠ FIVE LINE-DRIFT / PATH MIS-CITATIONS TO FLAG AT THE GATE (none change substance):** (1) brief said `calcStateVote phaseRunners.ts:3685` — the function signature actually starts at `:3684` (off-by-one). (2) brief implied `src/pages/NewGameScreen.tsx:25` — the file is `src/components/NewGameScreen.tsx:25` (line correct, directory differs). (3) brief said the entry phase is hardcoded at `phases.ts:65-67` — it is actually hardcoded in each SCENARIO builder (`scenario1772.ts:73`, `scenario1856.ts:153`); `phases.ts:64-66` is the `shouldRunPhase` 2.1.1 first-turn-draft special-case (related, not the entry-phase source). (4) brief said `scenario1856.ts:124-193` — the pre-seat + game block is ~120-195 (phaseId is the `:153` line). (5) brief said `resolveIdeologyShift phaseRunners.ts:704-794` — `:704` is `factionCenter`; `resolveIdeologyShift` itself starts at `:768` (the `704-794` span correctly brackets BOTH functions). All five are citation hygiene only — every claimed BEHAVIOR is confirmed.

> **★★ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (batch-56 deltas) — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1.** This batch adds NO keystone and re-sequences nothing at the top; it is mostly DESIGNER-DECISIONS + bounded additions/foundational-prereqs onto already-shipped engines.
> 1. **★★ TWO DESIGNER-DECISIONS must route to the designer BEFORE any build — they are the headline. (A) The SC AUTO-CONFIRM vs ALWAYS-VOTE fork (debt #225)** is unresolved in `70a67176` (weight leans always-vote) and is EITHER/OR-coupled to the automated court-DECISIONS (#270) — decide them TOGETHER; the always-vote BUILD then rides #66 (the cabinet committee→floor vote) and the 2.3 confirmation rebuild (#275), NOT near-term. **(B) The post-1772 START-PHASE-ENTRY A/B/C (debt #228)** is a poll (leans B); the shipped 1856 hybrid matches NEITHER A nor B, so the ruling + the hybrid reconcile fold into the EXISTING scenarioBoot keystone (K3/K4 + scenarioBoot, #86/#115/#186) as one more knob — not a new keystone.
> 2. **★ #326 (SC age / Judicial seating gate, debt #226) is the ONE clean near-term FIX this batch — schedule it behind K0/#296.** It is a SMALL bounded patch to the live auto-pick under-age/extremist bug: add an age floor + Judicial-keyed eligibility table to the justice-appointment filter (`phaseRunners.ts:3661`). It is DISTINCT from the #225 vote fork (this is seating-eligibility, that is the vote) and pairs with #66's compromise-candidate rule to fully fix the ★7 bug. Behind K0 because any "penalty under N" form is a roll.
> 3. **★ The PARTY-SHIFT INEVITABILITY ENGINE is already SHIPPED (`factionCenter` + `resolveIdeologyShift`) — do NOT plan it as new work.** Only its GATING is unbuilt (debt #227): the later-era `eligibleIdeologies` matrix columns (#4 — this is the 3rd witness), the "can be independent" draft-party tag, and the restriction settings toggle (#171/#263). All three RIDE the existing #4 draft-profile epic (b52 #202's generalize-the-eligible-set work) — not a standalone item, not near-term.
> 4. **★ #325 (split-state pol-assignment, debt #229) RIDES the alt-states / `expansionStates` work + the #295 era-aware draft pool — and is GATED on the split alt-states existing as real states + K0/#296 (the seeded assignment die).** Do not start it until UC/LC + the 4 TX splits exist as states with a `parentState`/`splitOf` field; then the per-draft-class die (CA 50/50, TX quartiles) + the ≥4-core-office fill are a bounded M. Use `src/rng.ts`, NOT `Math.random`. Same minimize-generated-pols design family as the b53 #313 rejection.
> 5. **★★ The GENDER ATTRIBUTE (#238, debt #230) is a FOUNDATIONAL demographic prereq — flag it EARLY, exactly like the b55 #319 backroom reconciliation.** It is small but MODEL-TOUCHING (the core `Politician`/`ImportedDraftee` types + the dataset generator + a save migration), and it BLOCKS the entire suffrage gate. Once it exists, the per-state women's-suffrage flag is a NEW #258 predicate class and the −5 rides the `calcStateVote` traitBonus channel. INERT until a 1916+ era/scenario ships (rides #92/#301; couples to the existing DH-14 era-aware-bill debt). Schedule the field with the other foundational type-touches; defer the suffrage GATE to the 1916+ era unit.
> 6. **★ K0/#296 is RE-CONFIRMED as the gate for the roll-based bits this batch:** the #326 under-age penalty roll (debt #226) and the #325 per-draft-class assignment die (debt #229). Keep QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1; nothing here re-orders the top of queue.
> - **★ One-line for the planner: keep QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4+scenarioBoot → E1; this batch adds NO keystone — its headline is TWO DESIGNER-DECISIONS (SC auto-confirm-vs-always-vote #225, coupled to court-decisions #270, build rides #66/#275; post-1772 start-phase A/B/C #228, folds into the scenarioBoot keystone), ONE clean near-term fix (#326 SC age/Judicial seating gate #226, behind K0), ONE foundational prereq (#238 gender attribute #230, flag early like b55 #319), and TWO rides-existing-epic items (#227 party-shift gating on the #4 draft-profile work — the inevitability ENGINE is already SHIPPED; #229 split-state on the alt-states + #295 + K0 work).**

> **★★★ Batch-54 change (5-DIGEST batch — `410ed3c8`-cpu-issues/#314+#315 (+#247/#74/#313-annot), `8b4f8164`-most-difficult-faction/#293+#92 (+#247/#262/#306/#114), `ef3301e4`-playtester-question/#317+#318+#315 (+career/election/officer confirms), `3a0e70be`-ampu-things-for-biden-presidency/#270+#316+#169+#206+#20, `f1209123`-era-evos-corporate-flavor-events/#221+#248+#294+#261+#206; I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-29 and am the build-state authority; FIVE new debt rows #214-#218 (continue after b53's max #213); references this batch's NEW gap rows #314-#318 + sharpens #247/#293/#92/#270/#169/#206/#316/#221/#248/#294/#261/#74/#314; updated game-mechanics §30.44. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1. ★ This batch adds NO shipped behavior; every delta is DESIGN-ONLY/RECONCILIATION (0% shipped OR a designed-vs-shipped divergence), re-verified. ★★ THE HEADLINE for the planner is a DESIGNER RECONCILIATION DECISION, not a new build: #314 — the forum-canonical CPU ideology-shift "Movement" rule (D-100×3-at-20% → 60% state-preferred / 40% party-highest-enthusiasm, ONE step, AVOID two-faced, count-3) DIVERGES on EVERY axis from the SHIPPED phase-`2.1.5` (`resolveIdeologyShift` `phaseRunners.ts:767-809`, CPU pass `:845-884`), which steps toward the actor FACTION's mean-ideology CENTER (`factionCenter` `:704`, `:773-775`) at 30%/65% (self) & 10%/15% (opposed) capped by `IDEOLOGY_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` (`types.ts:263` — the build coincidentally encodes the STALE "5" the thread rejected) with an OFFENSIVE flip-flop ATTACK on the victim (`ffRisk:0.5`, `:790-792`), NOT a self-avoidance gate. This is a DESIGNER DECISION (ratify the forum 60/40 model OR ratify the shipped faction-center model) — it BLOCKS the #247 geo-ideology data layer (the 60%-target needs a per-state preferred-ideology field that does NOT exist; only `bias: number` `types.ts:1324`, grep `preferredIdeology|geoIdeolog` = 0) and, if the forum model wins, is DOWNSTREAM of K0/#296 (the D-100×3 rolls draw seeded RNG) + needs the enthusiasm→shift wiring (un-built — `Enthusiasm` `types.ts:1415` is read only by elections `:3696,3709`). ★ Second: #293 faction-difficulty is a SIZEABLE MODEL ADDITION (no faction×era favorability term in `calcStateVote` `:3709`; `Faction.personality` is a 3-bucket `'LW'|'Center'|'RW'` `types.ts:1297` with no Red1–Red5 axis; the `Era` enum's 4 values `types.ts:1337` can't represent Reconstruction/Gilded/New-Deal/post-1974) — it DEPENDS on the era-granularity reframe (#92) + a Red1–Red5 axis, NOT near-term. ★ Third: #317 (faction-leader-only-presidential-candidate NOT enforced — `runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` `:3725-3749` takes any command≥2 party member 35-80, no `factionLeaderOf`/Celebrity gate) is a SMALL code change BUT decision-gated (needs a human ruling that the rule is canonical, not table-shorthand). ★ Fourth: the modern catalog (#169/#206/#270/#316) is blocked by the structural no-modern-scenario prerequisite; #270's N-groups-per-ruling is a SMALL type-widening (`SupremeCourtCase.effect?` `types.ts:1555` → a Yea/Nay LIST), the new Environmentalists/Big-Business interest groups are small enabling adds (absent from `InterestCardId`/`LobbyCardId` `types.ts:310-320`), #316 is author-time-only (no code surface). ★ Fifth: #318 inter-party cooperation REWARDS is a real new mechanic (legislation passes by tallies only; must model DEFECTION); #315 draft skill-tie tiebreak is small (`pickBestForFaction` `:33-53` scores PV+ideo only); the Era-Evo "corporate flavor" line is content-engine-adjacent (model corps as `{homeState, industrySector}` TAGS, NOT an entity — `EraEvent` `types.ts:1466` has no `isFlavor`/category field; > **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority — NO contradictions to flag this batch: all 5 digests, game-master §30.44, and gap rows #314-#318 are accurate against `src/` HEAD):**
> - **★★ #314 CPU ideology-shift MOVEMENT rule — the SHIPPED `2.1.5` system CONFIRMED + the per-axis DIVERGENCE confirmed.** `resolveIdeologyShift` (`phaseRunners.ts:767-809`) steps `p.ideology = to` where `to` comes from `ideologyShiftOdds(... center ...)` → `stepToward(fromIdx, actorCenter)` (`:740-741,:752-753`) and `center = factionCenter(snap, actorFactionId)` (`:773`) — **TARGET = actor faction's mean-ideology center, NOT state-preferred(60)/party-enthusiasm(40).** Odds: `IDEOLOGY_SHIFT_ODDS.attempt = { self: 0.65, opposed: 0.15, ffRisk: 0.5 }` (`types.ts:255`) + CPU gates `cpu: { selfGate: 0.3, selfBudget: 3, opposedGate: 0.1, opposedScan: 10 }` (`:261`) — **30%/65% & 10%/15%, NOT 20%-via-D100×3.** Cap `if ((counts[actorFactionId] ?? 0) >= IDEOLOGY_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN) return null` (`:778`), `IDEOLOGY_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` (`types.ts:263`) — **the STALE "5", not canonical 3.** Two-faced: `if (kind === 'opposed' && chance(ffRisk)) { p.flipFlopperPenalty += 1 }` (`:790-792`) — an **OFFENSIVE flip-flop INFLICTED on the victim on success**, NOT a self-avoidance gate. Passive drift (`:886-930`) rates `drift: { faction: 0.08, stateBias: 0.04, stateBiasMin: 1.0, residual: 0.01 }` (`types.ts:254`) toward faction-center / state-`bias`-sign — the closest "D100×3, one move" SHAPE analog but per-pol drift, not the CPU action.
> - **★ #314 data layer + enthusiasm wiring — CONFIRMED ABSENT.** `grep preferredIdeology|leastPreferred|mostPreferred|geoIdeolog|stateIdeology src/` → **ZERO.** `interface State` (`types.ts:1318-1335`) carries only `bias: number` (`:1324`) — a single scalar (positive = red, `calcStateVote` `baseLean` `:3697`), no most/least-preferred ideology pair → no data home for the 60%-target (sharpens #247). `Enthusiasm` (`types.ts:1415`) is read by `calcStateVote` (`:3696,:3709`) + the EnthusiasmPage meter UI but NO shift/drift path consults it → the 40%-target is un-wired.
> - **★ #293/#92 faction-difficulty — CONFIRMED no surface.** `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3713`) score = `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8` (`:3709-3711`) — `baseLean = state.bias` (per-state STATIC), **NO faction-ideology × era favorability term**; era enters only via `applyTraitElectionBonus(..., { era, ... })` (`:3708`). `electionFactionBias` (`:1539-1561`) is card-driven (clamped ±3), no era/ideology weight. `Faction.personality: 'LW' | 'Center' | 'RW'` (`types.ts:1297`) — **3-bucket, NO Red1–Red5 axis**; `Faction` (`:1293-1304`) has no `difficulty`/`favorability`/per-era field (the only `difficulty` is the Rev-War battle tier `types.ts:1363`). `Era` = `'independence'|'federalism'|'nationalism'|'modern'` (`types.ts:1337`) — **the calibration's eras (Reconstruction/Gilded/New-Deal/post-1974) CANNOT be expressed.** Lobby acquisition gate `ALIGNMENT_RULES.interestCardBucket[proxy] === bucket` (`:1798-1801`) — coarse 3-bucket match, no Red1-vs-Red5 ease; modern lobby names are interest-group STRINGS `WallStreet`/`BigOilGas`/`BigAg`/`RWMedia`/`BigTech`/`MilitaryIndustrial` (`factions1856.ts:24-31`), NOT `LobbyCardId` values (`types.ts:316-320` = 1772/1856 historical set only).
> - **★ #317 faction-leader presidential gate — CONFIRMED NOT enforced.** `runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` (`phaseRunners.ts:3725-3749`) filters `p.partyId === partyId && !deathYear && !retiredYear && age >= 35 && age <= 80 && command >= 2` (`:3729`), ranks `pvCache + command*5 + totalBonus` (`:3733`) — **NO `factionLeaderOf` gate, NO `Celebrity`-trait gate.** The faction-leader machinery EXISTS (`Politician.factionLeaderOf` `types.ts:1290`; the 2.2.3 leader election `:1969-2018`) but is **NOT referenced anywhere in the primary** — confirmed unwired to candidacy.
> - **★ #318 / #315 — CONFIRMED.** Legislation passes by vote tallies (no cross-aisle logrolling reward/defection model anywhere); Speaker/PPT auto-appointed to the top-PV majority member (`runPhase_2_2_1` `:1866-1888`) — no contest/concession (#318). `pickBestForFaction` (`:33-53`) scores `p.pvCache + (ideoMatch ? 25 : 0) + (eligIdeos?.includes ? 50 : 0)` and `scored.sort(...)` (`:45-52`) — **no skill-tie tiebreak, no admitted-state check**; territory-origin draftees implicitly exist (`recordDraftPick` `:55-75` retains `state` with no admitted-state filter) so #315's rule is satisfiable (#315).
> - **★ #270/#169/#206/#316 modern content — CONFIRMED.** Only `scenario1772.ts` (`:69`) + `scenario1856.ts` (`:149`) exist; the `modern` `Era` value (`types.ts:1337`) has **no scenario** (#169/#206). Court `runPhase_2_5_3_Court` (`:3397-3415`) = `chance(0.5)`/turn, 4 hardcoded strings, justice-headcount, ±0.1 `partyPreference`. `SupremeCourtCase` (`types.ts:1548-1556`) has a **single `effect?: EraEventResponseEffect`** (`:1555`) — needs a Yea/Nay LIST for WV-v-EPA's N-groups-per-ruling (#270); `pendingCourtCases` seeded `[]`, never read. `InterestCardId` (`types.ts:310-314`) + `LobbyCardId` (`:316-320`) have **no Environmentalists, no Big Business** (only the `planet` national meter `:1399-1407` is green-adjacent) — both genuinely new (#316/#206). `EraEventResponseEffect` (`:1448-1457`) carries `meters?`+`interestGroups?`+`enthusiasm?` — the dual-payload channels exist; the case SHAPE + the new groups are missing. No gov/pres-action system (`grep govAction|presAction|executiveAction src/` = 0). #316 inclusion bar = author-time, no code surface.
> - **★ #221/#248/#294/#261 Era-Evo flavor — CONFIRMED 0% shipped.** `EraEvent` (`types.ts:1466-1477`) is a single uniform shape (`id/year/title/description/responses/decider/...`) with **NO `isFlavor` flag, NO category/subtype field**; `grep isFlavor|flavorEvent|corporation|companyId|industrySector src/` = **ZERO**. `State.industries: Record<string, number>` (`:1328`) keys are ad-hoc (shipping/finance/tobacco/cotton/manufacturing — no steel/oil/auto/tech/media sector taxonomy). ⚠ The #294 TERMINOLOGY COLLISION is real: "era-evo" means an industry/lobby UNLOCK SCHEDULE on #294 vs a FLAVOR-EVENT CATEGORY in `f1209123` — same label, different construct; keep distinct when building.
