> **★★ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (batch-54 deltas) — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1.** This batch adds NO keystone and re-sequences nothing at the top; its headline is a DESIGNER DECISION that GATES a data layer. Explicit calls (LEAD with #314):**
> 1. **★★ #314 IS THE HEADLINE — a DESIGNER RECONCILIATION DECISION (forum 60/40 model vs shipped faction-center), and it BLOCKS the #247 geo-ideology data layer.** The forum-canonical CPU "Movement" rule (D-100×3-at-20% → 60% state-preferred / 40% party-highest-enthusiasm, one step, avoid two-faced, count-3) DIVERGES on EVERY axis from the SHIPPED `2.1.5` (steps toward faction CENTER at 30%/65% & 10%/15%, cap 5, offensive flip-flop attack). **Before ANY build, the designer must pick the canonical spec.** If the SHIPPED faction-center model is ratified → XS (retire the forum spec; optionally reconcile cap 5→3). **If the FORUM model is ratified → it is DOWNSTREAM of K0/#296** (the D-100×3 rolls draw seeded RNG) **AND it BLOCKS / co-requires the #247 per-state preferred-ideology data layer** (the 60%-target needs a most-/least-preferred ideology field on `State` that does NOT exist — only `bias: number`) **AND it needs the enthusiasm→shift wiring** (the 40%-target — un-built) **AND a self-avoidance gate replacing the offensive flip-flop attack.** So the sequence for the forum path is: K0/#296 → (#247 data layer + the enthusiasm wiring) → the 60/40 D-100×3 shift rewrite. Do NOT build the #247 geo-ideology data layer speculatively until #314 is decided — it is decision-gated ON #314. DISTINCT from #74 (legislation-voting CPU; cross-link only).
> 2. **★ #293 FACTION-DIFFICULTY is a SIZEABLE MODEL ADDITION dependent on the era-granularity reframe (#92) + a Red1–Red5 axis — NOT near-term; situate it behind K3/K4.** The forum's per-faction era-favorability WINDOWS + the lobby-access-by-faction asymmetry are real but UNMODELED: there is no faction×era favorability term in `calcStateVote`, `Faction.personality` is a 3-bucket axis (no Red1–Red5), and the `Era` enum's 4 values can't represent the calibration's eras (Reconstruction/Gilded/New-Deal/post-1974). So #293 needs, in order: (a) the #92 era-model reframe (K3/K4) FIRST; (b) a Red1–Red5 ideology-ladder axis on `Faction`; (c) a faction-ideology × era favorability term in `calcStateVote`; (d) optionally faction-ideology-weighted lobby acquisition. It also NEEDS the modern eras/lobbies (call 4) to express the post-1974 half. Sequence as a downstream election-model + faction-structure epic, NOT a quick win. The single-player-hardest-faction-enjoyment question is an open #114 balance/UX risk (human call).
> 3. **★ #317 faction-leader presidential gate — DECISION-GATED; do NOT build before the human ruling.** The code change is S (add a `factionLeaderOf || Celebrity` gate to `runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` candidacy), but the rule is asserted-as-canonical-by-a-player-yet-possibly-table-shorthand — get the ruling first. If ratified, it is a self-contained quick win (the `factionLeaderOf` machinery already exists); playtest the field-narrowing effect.
> 4. **★ MODERN CONTENT (#169/#206/#270/#316) — the no-modern-scenario is the PREREQUISITE for the whole modern catalog; the enabling changes are small.** A modern scenario + the K3/K4 era-model reframe is the structural prerequisite (everything Biden-era has no era to live in). Riding on it: #270's list-of-groups widening (`SupremeCourtCase.effect?` → a Yea/Nay LIST per side — S, on the already-tracked SC-generator debt #210); the new Environmentalists + Big-Business interest groups (S enabling adds — WV-v-EPA can't be authored faithfully without them); #316 is author-time only (no code). Do NOT mint a modern-content epic separate from the K3/K4 era-model + the SC-generator (#270) — route the catalog through them.
> 5. **★ The SMALL/CONTENT-ADJACENT items: #318 inter-party cooperation rewards (M, a real new mechanic — must model DEFECTION) + #315 draft skill-tie tiebreak (S) + the Era-Evo corporate flavor line (content-engine-adjacent).** #318 is a real cross-party logrolling mechanic (pairs the contested-officer concession dynamic; feeds #74) — schedule it as a mid-size legislation/officer-deal feature, behind the content/CPU work. #315 is a small additive tiebreak in `pickBestForFaction` (needs a skill comparator). The Era-Evo corp list (`f1209123`) is content for the #221 flavor tier / #248 subtype / #294 industry layer — model corps as `{homeState, industrySector}` TAGS, not an entity; ⚠ flag the #294 terminology collision (flavor-category vs industry-unlock-schedule) + the IP/real-brand-name human call. None re-sequence the top of queue.

> **★★★ Batch-55 change (5-DIGEST batch, all DIGEST-ONLY — `6b680261`-secession-and-civil-war/#319+#321+#324 (+#56/#58/#288/#156/#157/#284/#45/#34/#121), `d1058b12`-cpu-rules-for-presidential-conventions/#185+#71+#72+#181+#183+#20+#74+#10, `87c94e25`-abilities-expertise-traits-reference/#319+#320+#321 (+#216/#294/#66), `26b112e5`-1892-era-of-progressivism/#124+#199+#92+#301+#40+#306+#293+#30, `1bb2ac6b`-working-towards-automation-scripts/#322+#323+#134+#20+#296; I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-29 and am the build-state authority; SIX new debt rows #219-#224 (continue after b54's max #218); references this batch's NEW gap rows #319-#324 + sharpens #56/#58/#288/#156/#157/#284/#45/#34/#121/#185/#71/#72/#181/#183/#20/#216/#294/#66/#124/#199/#92/#301/#40/#306/#293/#30/#134/#322/#323/#296; updated game-mechanics §30.45. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1. ★ This batch adds NO shipped behavior; every delta is DESIGN-ONLY/RECONCILIATION (0% shipped OR a designed-vs-shipped count/roster divergence), re-verified — FOUR of the five sources are canonical rules-DOCS (no historian ran the batch; only `26b112e5` narrates any in-game play). ★★ THE HEADLINE for the planner: TWO LARGE epics are now FULLY-SPECCED-but-~0%-shipped — (A) the **secession / Civil-War / Reconstruction ENGINE** (the vcczar-approved "AMPU v1.3" ruleset folded into Union rules 3.0.32/3.0.35; `isSlaveState` `types.ts:1329` ships but is read by ZERO secession code — only a PR6 "Secession Winter" cabinet-defection EVENT exists, and the Civil War it can start is a NAME-ONLY `War` in `snap.wars`), and (B) the **CPU PRESIDENTIAL-CONVENTION system** (2.9.2 is a one-line log stub `engine.ts:69`; no delegate/ballot/momentum/promise/platform model; `vicePresidentId` is never SET by the election flow). BOTH sit in the era / E-phase work, NOT near-term. The smallest near-term ATOMS the secession epic decomposes into are **#288** (wire `isSlaveState` into a secession trigger) and **#324** (the 5-step citizenship-strip ladder = a self-contained legislation/exec-action set). ★ Third: a MODEL-DEFINING reconciliation — **#319** (`SkillKey` has 7 keys incl. `backroom` `types.ts:24-41` vs canon's 6 abilities, backroom track-only) is LOW-RISK but touches the core `Skills` type the whole stat system uses — flag it as a FOUNDATIONAL early reconciliation. ★ Fourth: the bounded near-term-able items are all behind K0/#296 (every one is roll-based): **#134** (Lingering is 3 of 4 parts short — volatility / tax-tariff decay / meter→meter), **#322** (committee +1-ability roll + per-member pass uncoded), **#124/#199** (cabinet-appointment-enthusiasm 20/10/5% roll + rejected-confirmation→Controversial branch), **#323** (CPU bill-SELECTION scoring formula). ★ Fifth: the **1892 "Era of Progressivism"** start rides the EXISTING #92/#301 era-granularity / scenario-flexibility work (K3/K4) — no new keystone. ★★ BINDING DESIGN CONSTRAINT (carry to the planner): the multi-ballot CONVENTION slow-roll is INTENTIONAL (the "single biggest game-mechanics argument" on the forum, settled compromise) — do NOT build more-aggressive CPU conventions without a designer mandate (`d1058b12#POST 7, 10`).):**
>
> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority — ONE gap-ID MIS-CITATION to flag at the gate, plus all 5 digests' shipped-state claims re-confirmed):**
> - **★★ SECESSION / CIVIL-WAR / RECONSTRUCTION engine — ~0% shipped, CONFIRMED.** `State.isSlaveState: boolean` (`types.ts:1329`) is WRITTEN at statehood (`isSlaveState: t.region === 'South' || t.region === 'Border'`, `phaseRunners.ts:3175`) and **read by NO secession/CW logic** (only the display `StatesPage.tsx`) — confirms #288's ships-but-unread. `State.region` (`types.ts:1322`) is a coarse fixed enum (`'Northeast'|'Midwest'|'South'|'West'|'Border'|'Caribbean'|...`) with **NO "Upper South"/"Deep South"/"Southwest"** granularity the politician-secession stay-% table needs (#58). The ONLY secession mechanic that ships is the PR6 **"Secession Winter" EVENT** — cabinet-officer defection rolls + a meter-band multiplier (`secessionWinterBand` `phaseRunners.ts:3001`, defections `:2974`) that, on N≥2 defectors, injects `out.startWar = { name:'American Civil War', against:'Confederate States' }` (`:2980-2981`). That war becomes a **NAME-ONLY generic `War`** in `snap.wars` via `applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3240-3253`) — `{ id, name, enemy, startYear, warScore:0, generals, battles:[] }`, **no battle engine, no theaters, no two-theater win condition.** The actual battle engine `revolutionaryWar.ts` is **single-front** (`groundWinsNeeded`/`currentGroundWins`, win/loss at `:254-263`), runs ONLY on `game.revolutionaryWar` (gated `phases.ts` 2.7.2), and the `RevolutionaryWar` type (`types.ts:1371-1387`) has **NO `theater`/`isCivilWar` field**. The loyalty traits **`Union Loyalist`/`Southern Unionist` are ABSENT** from the `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117` = 55 traits); `Provincial` exists ONLY as an election-modifier (`types.ts:107`, bands `:916-922`), NOT the POST-9 "100% secession-or-loyalty by region" rule. `grep -rE 'readmission|[Rr]econstruction|militaryDistrict|secessionConvention' src/` → **ZERO** — no Reconstruction phase, no readmission plans, no #324 citizenship-strip ladder.
> - **★★ CPU PRESIDENTIAL-CONVENTION system — ~0% shipped, CONFIRMED.** 2.9.1 Primaries (`runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` `phaseRunners.ts:3725-3749`) filters each party to `command >= 2 && age 35-80`, sorts ONCE on `pvCache + command*5 + traitBonus`, and **picks the top one** — no factions-running-candidates, no delegates, no nominators, no minor/celebrity path. 2.9.2 Conventions is a **one-line log stub** — `engine.ts:69`: `case '2.9.2': addLog(... 'Party conventions ratify the primary winners.'); return {};`. `grep` of `src/types.ts` for `delegate/ballot/momentum/darkHorse/presidentialPromise/threshold/keynote/platform/runningMate` → **nothing** (only Continental-Congress delegates + the `ConstitutionalConvention`). `vicePresidentId` (`types.ts:1568`) is **never SET by the election flow** — only read for display + cleared on VP-vacate (`phaseRunners.ts:2450`); the pres-general (`:3752`) runs a head-to-head EV race with NO running mate and resets the cabinet to null post-election. ★ `pendingConvention` is the CONSTITUTIONAL Convention (`constitutionalConvention.ts`), a wholly separate founding-era system — do NOT conflate.
> - **★ STAT-SYSTEM reconciliation — CONFIRMED.** `SkillKey` (`types.ts:24-41`) = **7 keys** `admin/legislative/judicial/military/governing/backroom` vs canon's **6 abilities** (Backroom is a `CareerTrack` `:50`, `TRACK_SKILL.Backroom='backroom'` `:202`, NOT a 0-5 ability) → code has 7 of the canonical 6 (#319; `command` is a separate `Politician.command` field `:1281`). `Expertise` (`types.ts:182-192`) = **19 tags, EXACT match** to canon (#320); all 3 canonical USES wired but `EXPERTISE_IDEOLOGY_LEAN` carries a lean on only **3 of 19** tags (`types.ts:421-425`, faction-center calc `phaseRunners.ts:717-718`), the re-elected-governor expertise grant is **unimplemented**, and the gov-action-gating USE has **no consumer** (no gov-action system). `Trait` union = **55** (`types.ts:62-117`) vs canon **57** — a DIFFERENT roster (41 shared / 15 canon-only incl. `Easily Overwhelmed` [referenced in ability-loss design but absent from the union — a dangling ref], `Lawful/Cop`, `Southern Unionist`; 14 code-only) (#216/#320). `LobbyCardId` (`types.ts:316-320`) = **15 scenario-flavored lobbies**, `LOBBY_EXPERTISE` (`:373-391`) keys only **7 of 15** vs the canonical 18 modern lobbies (#321). Cabinet: `OfficeType` (`types.ts:1111`) + `cabinetSeatsForYear` (`:1196`) ship the Big-4 + Navy/Interior/PMG/General/Admiral/Ambassador but MISS the Cabinet-Level tier (FBI/CIA-DNI/Fed-Chair/UN-Amb/NSA), the special non-meter Ministers (Indian Sovereignty / Confederate States / Northern Confederacy / Western America), and Key Advisor; confirmation is auto-scored, not a Committee/Floor vote (#66).
> - **★ 1892 + CABINET-ENTHUSIASM — CONFIRMED.** No 1892/Progressivism scenario — only `scenario1772.ts:69` + `scenario1856.ts:149`; the `Era` enum = 4 values `'independence'|'federalism'|'nationalism'|'modern'` (`types.ts:1337`), no Gilded/Progressive band (#92/#301). `Enthusiasm` (`types.ts:1415-1418`) = per-IDEOLOGY→party (`{ [ideology]: { BLUE; RED } }`, −5..+5), built once at scenario start, moved ONLY by era-event effects — there is **NO per-FACTION enthusiasm value** (`Faction` `types.ts:1293-1303` has no enthusiasm field), **NO interest-group-card→faction binding for enthusiasm, NO appointment-tier 20/10/5% roll** anywhere in the engine (#124). No rejected-confirmation→Controversial+pref-shift branch (`grep reject/Controversial` in `src/engine` finds only resolver-bool comments) (#199).
> - **★ AUTOMATION / LINGERING — CONFIRMED.** `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260`) does **part (1) ONLY** — cabinet-skill-driven per-meter drift + per-seat expertise bonus + per-trait modulation + national-debt update; **NO volatility, NO tax/tariff decay, NO meter→meter cross-effects** (the designed 4-part spec is 3 parts short, #134). `runPhase_2_2_2_Committees` (`phaseRunners.ts:1894`) grants the **4 chairs** command+1/legislative+1/expertise but has **NO small-chance +1-ability roll, NO per-member (non-chair) pass** — though the chance-gated +1 PRIMITIVE exists in career-track `rollThreshold` (`phaseRunners.ts:299`), committee just doesn't use it (#322). No CPU bill-SELECTION scoring formula (#323, distinct from #74's vote heuristic). `src/rng.ts:1-5` still uses `Math.random` (un-seeded) — every roll-based spec above is gated on K0/#296.
> - **⚠ ONE GAP-ID MIS-CITATION TO FLAG AT THE GATE (game-mechanics §30.45 sub-headers):** the §30.45.2 header titles the CPU-convention spec **"(#319)"** and the §30.45.4 header titles the cabinet-enthusiasm calculator **"(#322)"**. Per the canonical gap-log (`game-context.md:1332-1336`), **#319 = the backroom 7th-ability discrepancy** and **#322 = the committee +1-ability roll** — NOT the convention spec / cabinet-enthusiasm. The CPU-convention spec sharpens the EXISTING **#185** (+#71/#72/#181/#183/#20/#74/#10); the cabinet-enthusiasm calculator sharpens the EXISTING **#124** (+#199). The §30.45 intro at `game-mechanics.md:24001` itself lists the correct sharpen-IDs (#185 / #124), so the two child sub-headers are internally inconsistent with both the intro and the gap-log — a header-only mis-label, not a content error. I use the gap-log numbering throughout this block.

> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed/corrected at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority — ONE discrepancy to flag at the gate):**
> - **★★ #307/#308 vs the EXISTING §29.5/#62/#191 House model — RECONCILED (the top call).** Code-verified the SHIPPED House representation is a flat per-state `representativeIds: string[]` (`types.ts:1327`) seeded ONE politician PER SEAT via `const reps = Math.max(1, s.electoralVotes - 2)` (`scenario1856.ts:93`, the exact EV−2 arithmetic the ruleset names) — with **NO EV→count ladder, NO per-seat vote-weight split, NO census recompute, NO bias-typed seats** (grep `influentialRep|stateSize|sizeClass` = ZERO). The game-mechanics §29.5 already documents the canonical resolution: the `housepoll` "3.0.16" 1-10 EV-derived ladder (≤5→1 / 6-10→2 / … / ≥46→10) **explicitly "SUPERSEDES the flat 3/2/1"** (§29.5, "Why the old flat 3/2/1 was wrong: it was BACKWARDS in some eras… a single NY rep could hold ~20 of 30 state votes"), the per-focus-rep vote power = `floor(stateUSReps/focusReps)` + remainder→highest-`legis` (= 3.0.24's (EV−2)÷count + leftover→highest-`legis`, **the same formula generalized off the ladder**), and the per-seat partisan bias = the #191 deviant-bias engine (`housepoll` authored the "+2 minority / ≥20% Red in CA" guarantee + the new-seat/seat-gain/seat-loss bias rolls). **VERDICT: #308 is the ORIGIN, NOT a standalone build — it folds into #62 (delegation weighted vote) + #191 (per-seat bias) + §29.5 (the ladder). #307's Big/Med/Small is the EARLY size-label form of that ladder, NOT a net-new primitive.** The ONLY genuinely-new deltas: the (EV−2)÷count↔ladder count is already tracked; what is net-new is the **era-gated House CAP** (post-1929 `House Cap + states×2`, DC/Moon/Mars-excluded reconciliation — no `435`/`houseCap` const, grep 0) and the **output-consumer WIRING** (the census recompute must drive the §29.5 ladder + #191 seats, which today don't recompute at all).
> - **★ #34/#305/#294/#307/#308 (census) — CONFIRMED 0% shipped, re-verified b53 (matches b52).** `interface State` (`types.ts:1318-1335`) has `electoralVotes`/`bias`/`industries`/`isSlaveState`/`admissionYear` — **NO `population`, NO `houseSeats`, NO `sizeClass`, NO per-seat bias.** `grep -i census|apportion|reapportion|decennial|houseCap|houseSeats|influentialRep|stateSize|sizeClass|435 src/` → ZERO. > - **★ #20/#294 (gov-action overhaul) — CONFIRMED token nudge.** `runPhase_2_5_2_Governors` (`phaseRunners.ts:3382-3392`) is the ENTIRE gov-action system: for each state with a `governorId`, a flat `chance(0.3)` to nudge `s.bias` by `(gov.skills.governing-1)*0.05` toward the gov's party. **Verified it reads NEITHER `gov.traits` NOR `gov.expertise` NOR `s.industries`** — no player choice, no action menu, no success/fail roll, no extra-attempt. `grep govAction|governorAction src/` = ZERO. `GovernorsPage.tsx` (34 lines) is READ-ONLY (a `<table>` over `snapshot.states`: State/Region/Governor/Party/Ideology/Gov Skill; zero `onClick`/`<button>`/`dispatch`). The axes ask B wants are both present + unconnected: `Expertise` (`types.ts:182-186`, 19 tags incl. `'Agriculture'`/`'Transportation'`/`'Energy'`/`'Business'`) ↔ `State.industries` (`types.ts:1328`, `Record<string,number>`); the ONLY industry mutator is the PR7 lobby nudge (`phaseRunners.ts:1644-1655`), which never reads `s.governorId`. Bills come from the 8-row inline `BILL_TEMPLATES` (`phaseRunners.ts:3420-3429`) — NO generic-fallback/fluff legis primitive (grep 0).
> - **★ #270 (SC case generator) — CONFIRMED 0% + the payload type ALREADY ships.** `runPhase_2_5_3_Court` (`phaseRunners.ts:3397-3415`) = `chance(0.5)`/turn picks a `title` from **4 hardcoded strings** (`:3399-3404`), rules by raw justice-ideology HEADCOUNT (`:3407-3409`), nudges `partyPreference` ±0.1 (`:3412-3413`). `SupremeCourtCase` (`types.ts:1548-1556`) has a SINGLE `effect?: EraEventResponseEffect` (no Yea/Nay split, no `points`, no 25%-chance wrapper). `pendingCourtCases` (`types.ts:1587`) seeded `[]` in BOTH scenarios (`scenario1772.ts:95`, `scenario1856.ts:175`) and NEVER read/written — `runPhase_2_5_3_Court` ignores it. ★ The per-side payload IS buildable on existing types: `EraEventResponseEffect` (`types.ts:1448-1457`) carries `meters?: Partial<NationalMeters>` + `interestGroups?:{id;delta}[]` + `enthusiasm?:{ideology;party;delta}[]` — the three reward kinds the generator needs; meters map cleanly (`rev/budg→revenue`, `honest gov→honest`, `quality of life→quality`, `NationalMeters` `types.ts:1399-1407`). Missing: the GENERATOR, the Yea/Nay DUAL-effect case shape, `points`, the 25%-chance meter wrapper.
> - **★ #309 (state-abolition → appointed governor) — CONFIRMED absent; #309's gap-row anchor is ACCURATE.** Governors are ELECTED only: `runPhase_2_9_5_Governors` (`phaseRunners.ts:3816`) builds a 2-candidate list then runs `calcStateVote(snap, s.id, list, 'governor')` (`:3825`) and assigns `s.governorId` to the winner — confirming the #309 row's "`runPhase_2_9_5_Governors` runs `calcStateVote(...,'governor')`" (I verified the inner call; the row is correct). `grep -i aboli src/` returns only the **Abolitionists** interest group / **Abolition** ideology card (slavery-era, unrelated). NO abolish-state action/flag, NO President-appoints-governor path, NO power-vacuum consequence.
> - **★ #310 (event TARGET + name-template) — CONFIRMED absent.** `EraEvent` (`types.ts:1466`) has a `decider` field (`'president'|'congress'|'cabinet'|'cc-president'|'auto'` — who RESOLVES it) but **no `target`/`recipientId`/`goesTo`** and no faction-leader-recipient concept; `EventEntry` (`types.ts:1439`, the log record) likewise has no target. NO `{name}` substitution on event/effect text (static strings). There is no separate "General Event" type (ships `EraEvent` + `anytimeEvents` + `anytimeNationalEvents`).
> - **★ #258 (legis deactivation-trigger) / #221 (1,000+ legis-props) / #311 (tutorial) / #312 (state-history log) — CONFIRMED absent.** `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506`) is a runtime INSTANCE record (sponsor/votes/status/`effects`) with NO `deactivationDate`/`expiry`/`repeal`/`turnsOff` field (only expiry-like field = the unrelated `failedBidExpiresYear` `types.ts:1289`); no era-keyed authored Legis-Prop catalog (bills from the 8-row inline `BILL_TEMPLATES`). No tutorial/onboarding/tip system in `src/pages`/`src/components`. No per-state narrative-history store (`src/engine/log.ts` is system event/phase logging).
> - **★ #313 (territorial offices) — CONFIRMED REJECTED + the underlying gap.** `OfficeType` (`types.ts:~1111`) has no Territorial Delegate/Governor (`floorVote`/`nonVoting` greps = 0). `admitState` (`territories.ts`) is a one-shot seed-copy — territories have ZERO pre-statehood gameplay; no statehood-admission legislation type (statehood via era-graph nodes / God Mode / Territories page only). `game.meters.domestic` substrate EXISTS (clamped −5..+5, written by era-event effects) but no low-stability independence trigger is wired. `expansionStates.ts` ~150 annexable seeds = the roster cost vcczar cites. **Logged REJECTED; no build sequencing.**

> **★★ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (batch-53 deltas) — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1.** This batch adds NO keystone and re-sequences nothing at the top; its single most-valuable output is a RECONCILIATION that REMOVES a would-be build. Explicit calls:
> 1. **★★ THE #307/#308-vs-§29.5/#62/#191 RECONCILIATION (the top call) — #308 is NOT a standalone House-representation build; it FOLDS into the already-tracked #62/#191/§29.5 cluster.** The census thread's flat **3/2/1 influential-Rep count is the Apr-2022 ORIGIN spec, SUPERSEDED by the `housepoll` 1-10 EV-derived ladder already documented in §29.5** (#219/#55). Code-verified the mapping: 3.0.24's (EV−2)÷count vote-power = §29.5's `floor(stateUSReps/focusReps)` + remainder→highest-`legis` (one formula generalized off the ladder); 3.0.24's ahistorical-seat bias (add 25/25/25/25, subtract protect-the-bias-seat) = the #191 deviant-bias engine; the single weighted delegation vote = #62. **#307's Big/Med/Small is the EARLY size-label form of the §29.5 ladder, NOT a net-new state-size primitive** — build it AS the ladder, not a parallel 3-bucket class. So the planner should NOT mint a new #307/#308 House-representation epic: route the count to §29.5, the per-seat bias to #191, the delegation vote to #62. The ONLY genuinely-new deltas this thread adds are the **era-gated House CAP** (post-1929 `Cap + states×2`, DC/Moon/Mars-excluded reconciliation — no const today) and the **output-consumer WIRING** (the census recompute must DRIVE the §29.5 ladder + #191 seats each decade, which today don't recompute at all) — both rolled into debt #207.
> 2. **★ The CENSUS ENGINE internal order REFINES b52's: state-population primitive (#305, debt #206) → census recompute (#34) → state-size (#307) → House-representation (#308 = the §29.5 ladder).** #305 is the FOUNDATIONAL prerequisite (established b52 — `State` has no `population` at all); the #34 recompute (debt #207) is downstream of it; #307 size-classification is a derived output of the recompute (build it AS the §29.5 ladder per call 1); #308 House-representation is the §29.5/#191/#62 cluster the recompute WIRES into. ★ The census PROBABILISTIC rolls — the Step-3 10%/25% industry %s and the ahistorical-seat 25/25/25/25 bias — sit behind **K0/#296** (seeded RNG) for reproducibility/tunability. The era-gated 435 House cap is a NEW const + reconciliation step inside the recompute. Large PV impact (EV drives the EC tally) — the recompute must run BEFORE the election phase reads `electoralVotes`.
> 3. **★ The GOV-ACTION OVERHAUL (#20, debt #209) is a real GREENFIELD epic — and the designer's own "token afterthought" admission makes it the explicit priority, esp. for CPU.** Trait-gating (reads `gov.traits`) + industry↔governor-EXPERTISE coupling (reads `gov.expertise` → writes `State.industries` — the FIRST governor→industry path, and the SAME axis surfaced on #294) + an extra action + a tunable success rate. The axes (Expertise tags, `State.industries`) both ship + are unconnected. ★ The CPU selection pass is DOWNSTREAM of the deterministic-CPU/K0 work (the designer wants it "esp. for CPU"); the player-facing depth is contested (vcczar would automate SC/Gov/Mil). Sequence on the gov-action/CPU track, behind K0. The (B) expertise→industry coupling overlaps #294's expertise axis — build once.
> 4. **★ The SC CASE GENERATOR (#270, debt #210) is buildable on EXISTING types — relatively SELF-CONTAINED.** `EraEventResponseEffect` already carries meters+interestGroups+enthusiasm, so the per-side payload needs no new effect plumbing — the build is the GENERATOR + a Yea/Nay dual-effect case shape + `points` + a 25%-chance meter wrapper + populating/consuming the dead `pendingCourtCases`. ★ Its 25%-chance meter rolls + random name/meter/group draws are behind **K0/#296**. Mostly independent of the content-engine epic (it's a court-phase + a small type change). M.

Re-open ONLY if all three deferral conditions are met. The three salvageable sub-gaps (pre-statehood territory gameplay; no statehood-admission ACT, ties #43; low-`domestic` independence trigger) are FLAGS, not builds — do not pursue absent a fresh design ruling.
> - **★ One-line for the planner: keep QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4+scenarioBoot → E1; this batch adds NO keystone and its single most-valuable output REMOVES a build — #308 (influential-Rep model) is the Apr-2022 ORIGIN of, and FOLDS INTO, the already-tracked #62/#191/§29.5 House cluster (the flat 3/2/1 is SUPERSEDED by the `housepoll` 1-10 ladder; the (EV−2)÷count vote-power = §29.5's `floor(stateUSReps/focusReps)`; the ahistorical-seat bias = #191), and #307's Big/Med/Small is the EARLY size-label form of that ladder — so do NOT mint a new House-representation epic, route count→§29.5/bias→#191/vote→#62; the census engine order is state-population (#305, b52 debt #206) → census recompute (#34, debt #207, the only NEW pieces being the era-gated House cap + the output-consumer wiring) → state-size (#307 = the ladder) → House-rep (#308 = the cluster), with the probabilistic 10%/25% + 25/25/25/25 rolls behind K0/#296; the GOV-ACTION overhaul (#20, debt #209) is a GREENFIELD L epic (trait-gate + expertise↔industry coupling + extra-action) whose CPU pass is behind K0 and whose expertise→industry axis overlaps #294; the SC generator (#270, debt #210) is a self-contained M buildable on the shipped `EraEventResponseEffect`, behind K0 for the 25%-rolls; the small items #309/#310/#311/#312 slot into the content-engine-adjacent + UI lanes; and #313 is REJECTED with no sequencing.**

> **★ SIZE LEDGER (this batch): #202 #4-CORE M (UNBLOCKED — generalize the soft eligible-set to base `Faction`+all eras; #306/#262/#293 are later UI/balance/settings layers) · #203 #302 L SPLIT (DATA layer gates it; SIBLING of relocation #38 — one start-date/era-model epic feeds both) · #204 Trade: foothold SHIPPED, #303 magnitude S-M + #304 routing-rule XS-S — EXTEND the content engine, don't move it · #205 #85 war-casualty TUNE S (near-term, but bundle the `:89,:97` `Math.random` with K0); additive features M each; FRAIL/HALE decision-gated · #206 #34+#305 L with a HARD INTERNAL SEQUENCE — #305 population PRIMITIVE FIRST, then the #34 census engine (also behind K0 for the probabilistic rolls).**
>
> **★★ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (batch-52 deltas) — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1.** This batch adds NO keystone and re-sequences nothing at the top; it slots the deltas into EXISTING lanes and surfaces ONE hard internal sequence (census). Explicit calls:
> 1. **★ CENSUS (#34) has a HARD INTERNAL PREREQUISITE — the state-POPULATION primitive (#305) must land FIRST.** Sequence: **population-model (#305) → census-engine (#34) → EV/House recompute.** #305 is a FOUNDATIONAL missing primitive (`State` has no `population`/`houseSeats` at all); #34 cannot compute apportionment without it. Situate #305 WITH the expansionStates-EV + alt-states cluster (the b50 EV=4 finding / #278) — per-state EV authoring + the population field are the same data-layer pass; the census engine follows. The census engine's probabilistic 10%/25% EV-change rolls put it ALSO behind K0/#296 (reproducible/tunable). Large PV impact (EV drives the EC tally) — the recompute must run before the election phase.
> 2. **★ PARTY-SWITCH (#302) is the SIBLING of relocation (#38) — scope TOGETHER, do not merge.** Both need a per-pol historical-date DATA layer (dataset + pipeline + save-migration) AND the same start-date/era model (party-as-of-start-date ↔ location-as-of-start-date). Build ONE start-date/era-model epic that feeds both; the auto-historic MODE is a #263 realism-settings toggle (no settings framework today). The player-driven conversion MODE already ships (2.1.6, #76) — keep it. DATASET-dependent → not near-term; do it on the relocation track.
> 3. **★ The DRAFT/FACTION-IDEOLOGY MATRIX (#4) is the concrete spec now — generalizing the 1772-inaugural-only soft eligibility is the UNBLOCKED CORE.** M, pure-engine, no migration (new optional `Faction` field): lift `eligibleIdeologies` onto base `Faction`, add the per-era dimension, consult it for ALL draft-years (not just `isExpansion1772`), author the intervening-era cells, ship the unrestricted-mode as the toggle OFF state (#171). Keep the SOFT two-tier (the "least-fun-when-mandated" empirical verdict is why). #306 (10-faction structure/naming/parties-only mode/awards) + #262 (fringe scoring) + #293 (difficulty=faction×era) are LATER UI/balance/settings layers — #306 needs a settings framework that doesn't exist; #262 needs `computePV` (or a separate election-score path) to first gain an ideology/rarity term. #298 conformance is the decision-gated sibling (debt #198). Sequence the #4 CORE on the draft/ideology track (#4/`8189b724`/#171).
> 4. **★ The WAR-CASUALTY TUNE (#85) is SMALL and near-term-able — but bundle its determinism fix with K0.** The casualty chart is SHIPPED and undertuned (`easy`≡`moderate`); lifting the counts (easy 1-2 / medium 2-3 / difficult 3-4, distinct tiers) is S. BUT it touches `revolutionaryWar.ts:89,:97` (`Math.random` victim-picks) — the #296/K0 violation — so do the TUNE as part of (or immediately after) K0's seeded-RNG swap in this file, NOT a re-tune on `Math.random`. The additive features (−1-MIL wound, first-class WOUNDED state, combat-trait grant, ALL-HANDS mobilization) are M each and rev-war-only; the FRAIL/HALE direction is decision-gated (shipped is the OPPOSITE of the design wish). A generic war engine for Mexican-War/1856+ is the separate #45/#56 epic; #300 (firearms-tech, debt #200) is the co-located but independent extension.
> 5. **★ TRADE #303/#304 BUILD ON the shipped diplomacy/tariff foothold — they EXTEND the content engine's program-state + scripted-event lists, they do NOT move its queue position.** Trade is the FIRST content genre with a real shipped foothold (do NOT mark it 0%). #303 (numeric tariff-rate magnitude program-state) is S-M and should UNIFY with the SAME graduated-intensity pattern as Taxation's fiscal regime (`1bf19872`) + Regulations' Level-N tiers (`d4cd2ee6`) + the Gilded tariff-%-rate spec (§31.1) — one program-state model. #304 (policy-vs-scripted-event routing rule) is XS-S as an authoring RULE, then content on a modern era graph that doesn't exist. The named trade content + the #66 USTR/Ex-Im agencies + the #206 outer-space rows remain content-engine-gated (behind K2 + #186/#173), like NatSec/Guns (debt #199).
> 6. **K0 (#296 seeded RNG) keeps its gating role — re-confirmed from this batch via TWO call-sites.** The war-casualty victim-picks (`revolutionaryWar.ts:89,:97`) and the census probabilistic EV-change rolls (#34, when built) both REQUIRE seeded RNG to be tunable/reproducible. This does NOT add or move a keystone — it hardens K0's role for the war-balance cluster (#205) and the census engine (debt #206).
> - **★ One-line for the planner: keep QW0 → K0/#296 → K2 → K3/K4+scenarioBoot → E1; this batch adds NO keystone but surfaces ONE hard internal sequence — CENSUS (#34) is blocked on the state-POPULATION primitive (#305), so population-model → census-engine → EV/House-recompute, sited with the expansionStates-EV/alt-states cluster and behind K0 for the probabilistic rolls; party-switch (#302) scopes WITH relocation (#38) as one start-date/era-model + dataset epic; the draft/faction-ideology MATRIX (#4) CORE is the unblocked M pure-engine generalization (1772-only → all-eras+base-`Faction`), with #306/#262/#293 as later UI/balance/settings layers; the war-casualty TUNE (#85) is a small near-term BALANCE item to bundle with K0's `revolutionaryWar.ts` RNG swap; Trade #303/#304 EXTEND the content engine (magnitude program-state + scripted-event routing) without moving its queue position — and Trade already has a real shipped foothold (the diplomacy meter), so do NOT mark it 0%.**
> game-mechanics §30.42; gaps #4/#306/#171/#298/#262/#293/#302/#76/#40/#38/#237/#221/#303/#304/#258/#248/#66/#206/#85/#45/#56/#300/#296/#34/#305/#294/#263.

> **★★★ Batch-51 change (5-digest batch — `b5b0ace9`-faction-leader-bonus-focus-race/#297, `c2bea165`-national-security/#221+#262+#258+#236+#66+#206, `067233f3`-guns/#221+#258+#248+#66+#237+#300, `8a0a14a1`-radically-different-governing-structure/#74+#301, `567b03c8`-ideology-cards-poll/#298+#299+#4+#171; I [tech-lead] re-pinned EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-29 and am the build-state authority; FIVE new debt rows #197-#201 (continue after b50's max #196); references this batch's NEW gap rows #297-#301 + sharpens #221/#262/#258/#248/#66/#236/#206/#237/#74/#296/#4/#171; updated game-mechanics §30.41. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence — top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 → K0/K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1. ★ This batch is mostly DESIGN-ONLY corroboration: no item ships shippable behavior, and the two genuinely NEW engine surfaces (#300 firearms-tech combat tree, #74/#301 CPU-faction-score-vote + reproducible counterfactuals) are BOTH DOWNSTREAM of the seeded-RNG keystone (K0/#296) — they cannot be built testably before it. ★ The HEADLINE is the seeded-RNG dependency being re-confirmed from a THIRD angle (#74/#301 reproducible-Convention-playtest + #300 combat-roll) — not a new item, a hardening of K0's gating role. Second: the NatSec/Guns catalogs (debt #199) are MORE content for the already-sequenced content engine (#221/#258/#237/#248) — they DON'T move the engine in the queue but they SHARPEN the predicate-class list (add the policy-active/passed/repealed counter-repeal leaf + the weapon-class taxonomy sub-model) and raise the content-volume estimate. ★ #297 election-bonus (debt #197) is a SMALL additive change onto the ALREADY-SHIPPED trait-bonus channel, but it is PROCESS-BLOCKED behind the designer's "needs several historical examples" gate AND a missing party-leader flat-bonus baseline — classify it PARKED / quick-win-IF-ratified, not a near-term build.):**
> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed/corrected at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority — and TWO brief/gap-log claims are HALF-WRONG, flagged ★CORRECTION below):**
> - **#297 leadership→elections is PV-ONLY — CONFIRMED, with the shipping vehicle MORE built than the brief implied.** Faction-leader/party-leader are ROLES that grant PV: `FactionLeader: 8` / `PartyLeader: 14` office-prestige (`pv.ts:25-26`) + `p.factionLeaderOf != null → +8` (`pv.ts:83`); fields are real (`Politician.factionLeaderOf` `types.ts:1290`, `Faction.leaderId` `:1301`, `Party.leaderId` `:1309`). PV reaches elections ONLY via `pv * 0.1` in `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3699,3709`) and `pvCache` in the primary scorer (`:3733`). NEITHER scorer reads `factionLeaderOf`/`leaderId` → NO flat leadership-keyed term. NO focus-race concept (`grep focusRace|campaignFocus|donor` over `src/` = ZERO). **★ But the candidate shipping vehicle — the additive trait-bonus channel — is ALREADY FULLY WIRED, not just present:** `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` (`types.ts:738`, 119 rows / 19 distinct traits), `TraitElectionRule` (`types.ts:727-736`), `TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS = {SMALL:2,MEDIUM:4,LARGE:8}` (`types.ts:721-725`), `ElectionContext` (6 values, `types.ts:697-703`), and `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts:16`) is summed into BOTH the general scorer (`phaseRunners.ts:3708`) AND the primary scorer (`:3732`). So #297 reduces to ADDING table rows + (for focus-race) a per-faction designation/die input the channel doesn't yet read — the engine plumbing exists.
> - **★CORRECTION #1 — `Obscure` DOES have `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` rows.** The brief AND gap-row #297's framing say "`Leadership`/`Obscure` have no trait-election-effects row." Verified: `Leadership` has ZERO rows (correct), but **`Obscure` has FIVE rows** (`types.ts:845-849`: presGeneral −4, presPrimary +2, house −4, governor −4, internalParty −2). So 10centjimmy's catch-all "not obscure AND Leadership" would have to RECONCILE with `Obscure`'s existing penalty rows, not assume a clean slate. (The game-master's §30.41.1 also loosely says "Obscure is in no leadership/election path" — true only in the narrow sense that it isn't part of the LEADERSHIP path; it IS in the election-effects table.)
> - **NatSec + Guns content + Pres/Gov-Action primitives — CONFIRMED 0% shipped.** `grep` over `src/` for govAction/governorAction/presAction/presidentialAction/policyGenre/legislationGenre/policyNode = **0 hits** (the gov-actions the digests cite are effectively unshipped — confirmed; the only "gun" in `src/` is a `future-foot-gun` comment, `phaseRunners.ts:2303`). `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has only the 4-value `committee` (`Domestic|Foreign|Economic|Justice`, `:1513`) — NO `subtype`/`policyGenre`/weapon-class/prereq field. The entire bill library is **8 generic `BILL_TEMPLATES`** (`phaseRunners.ts:3420-3429`), randomly drawn. So both catalogs corroborate the existing content-engine debt (b47 #172/#173, b49 #186) with TWO new genres.
> - **The counter/repeal predicate — CONFIRMED ABSENT.** `Predicate` (`types.ts:1487-1504`) variants = `all`/`any`/`not`/`yearAtLeast`/`yearAtMost`/`eventCompleted`/`eventChose`/`meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost`/`interestAtLeast`/`diplomacyAtLeast`/`warActive`/`warOutcome`/`stateAdmitted`/`officeControlledByPlayer`/`rosterHasSkill`/`flag`. There is **NO law-active / policy-passed / policy-repealed / agency-exists / policy-active-in-state-B leaf.** The Privacy Amendment ("disable most National Security legislation") is the corpus's clearest policy-DISABLES-policy demand; the Guns manufacturer-liability shield ↔ open-to-lawsuits is the opposed-counter specimen; state reciprocity needs a cross-state policy-active linkage `Predicate` entirely lacks (only `stateAdmitted` is cross-state-ish). All unrepresentable today. Folds straight into the b49 debt #186 active-policy-tracking keystone-within.
> - **The weapon-class taxonomy + the Drugs substance-class enum — BOTH greenfield (★CORRECTION #2 to the brief's "sibling of THE Drugs substance-class enum").** `grep` for substanceClass/weaponClass/marijuana/narcotic over `src/` = **0 hits.** There is **no shipped Drugs substance-class enum** for the weapon-class taxonomy to be a sibling OF — b49 debt #187 logged the Drugs substance-class enum as itself a DESIGNED, un-built sub-model. So the weapon-class taxonomy (handgun/long-rifle/shotgun/machine-gun/assault-weapon/ghost-gun/bump-stock/large-cap-magazine) is a sibling-BY-DESIGN of the (also-unshipped) Drugs substance-class enum — both are NEW data tiers the content model would need, not one extending an existing one.
> - **The "leave gun laws to the states" DESIGN-ASSERTED-SHIPPED discrepancy — CONFIRMED no gun-devolution policy exists.** The only "states-rights" construct in `src/` is the `StatesRights` **ideology card** (`IdeologyCardId`, `types.ts:323,361`; on factions in `factions1772.ts:16`, `factions1856.ts:5-6`) — a faction-alignment tag, NOT a gun-specific (or any-genre) devolution POLICY. vcczar's POST 6 "The game has that" refers to the master design list / federalism ideology framing, not shipped Guns content. Log as DESIGN-ASSERTED-SHIPPED, build-UNVERIFIED.
> - **#300 firearms-tech combat tree — CONFIRMED 0% built + DOWNSTREAM of K0.** The ONLY combat system is `revolutionaryWar.ts`. Ground target = `planning + generalScore(military×10) + (frenchAlliance ? 25 : 0) + diffMod`, then `win = d100() <= baseTarget + diffMod` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:212-218`). Naval target = `planning + adminScore(military×10) + 30`, `win = d100() <= target + 30` (`:186-192`). There is **NO weapon-tech/equipment term in either target**, and **NO military-tech tier field** on the `RevolutionaryWar` interface (`types.ts:1371`, 16 fields, none weapon/tech/tier) or anywhere on the snapshot. Both rolls use `d100()` from `rng.ts` → so a tier-scaled success term MUST draw from the seeded generator (#296) to be tunable: **#300 is DOWNSTREAM of K0.** Cross-link the `145db158` weapon-testing power-bump axis — design as ONE military-tech subsystem, not two (the firearms ladder is the deterministic-tier sibling of that stochastic power-bump).
> - **#74 CPU vote model — CONFIRMED flat-affiliation, no faction-score term.** `voteCC` (`continentalCongress.ts:211-215`) decides each delegate aye/nay by a flat probability keyed on sponsor relationship: `sameFaction ? 0.92 : sameParty ? 0.6 : 0.2`, nudged by `cardVoteBias(...)` over the bill's interest cards (`:214`). NO computation of the bill's projected effect on the voting faction's SCORE — identity is the proxy. The pro-historical bias exists ONLY in the era-event graph (`pickAIResponse` first-response default + `aiBias` lookup, `eraGraph.ts:85-103`) — NOT in the Convention CPU: `preferredOption` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:102-123`) picks each article by `partyId` + `ideology` + flat `chance()` coin-flips, no historical weighting, no faction-score input. The historical-bias × faction-score COMPOSITION is undefined (the open question for the deterministic-CPU spec).
> - **#301 scenario structural flexibility — CONFIRMED recorded-but-INERT.** `legislature: 'bicameral'|'unicameral'` IS stored (`constitutionalConvention.ts:8-15`, `ConstitutionalArticles.legislature` `types.ts:1390`, written by `applyConvention` `:137` with `?? 'bicameral'` fallback) but is **NEVER READ for gameplay** — the only consumers are the `ConventionModal.tsx:5` UI label, the `applyConvention` write-back, and the CPU's own `preferredOption` choice (`:105`); **no House/Senate construction branches on it** (`grep unicameral` over `src/engine`/`src/pages` finds only the convention-setup option + the CPU choice — zero gameplay reads). 1856 hardcodes `'bicameral'` (`scenario1856.ts:183`). `State.region` is a FIXED closed 10-value enum (`types.ts:1322`), statically assigned, consumed-not-configurable. `termLimits` offers only `two_terms|no_limits` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:60-66`) and the executive article tops out at elected/congressional/council (`:18-24`) — no appointed-for-life option, and `termLimits` is not evidently enforced on presidential re-eligibility. Reproducible counterfactual runs need seeded RNG → **#74/#301's CPU-faction-score + counterfactual half is DOWNSTREAM of K0/#296** and feeds the deterministic-CPU engine (#20, debt #191) already tracked.
> - **#298 draftee conformance — CONFIRMED 0% built.** `recordDraftPick` (`phaseRunners.ts:55-79`) sets `p.factionId`/`p.partyId`/`p.draftedYear` but **NEVER mutates `p.ideology`** — a drafted Moderate stays Moderate. The "draftees take on the card ideology" half of poll options #2/#3 is unbuilt.
> - **#298's gradual-drift cousin DOES ship.** `resolveIdeologyShift` (`phaseRunners.ts:767-794`) mutates `p.ideology = to` (`:789`) — one graduated step toward the actor faction's center (`stepToward` `:741`), gated to phase 2.1.5, governed by `IDEOLOGY_SHIFT_ODDS`. So the design choice is INSTANT conformance-on-draft (#298) vs the shipped GRADUAL drift; the realism objection (instant is unrealistic) suggests the shipped drift may be the intended answer.
> - **#299 off-ideology vote penalty — CONFIRMED 0% built.** `computePV` (`pv.ts:67-89`) has **NO ideology term** (skills × office-weights, command, traits, prestige, age — nothing reads `p.ideology`); there is no per-pol vote-selection surface. The personal-ideology slot exists (`Politician.ideology` `types.ts:1266`) and is used in CC selection (`firstContinentalCongress.ts` `ideologyDistance`) — but never touches elections/PV.
> - **#4/#171 draft-eligibility gate — CONFIRMED partial (1772-inaugural ONLY).** `Faction1772.eligibleIdeologies: Ideology[]` (`factions1772.ts:7`, populated `:12-23`) is consumed by `getEligibleIdeologies` (`phaseRunners.ts:28`) + `pickBestForFaction` (`:33`) but ONLY when `isExpansion1772` (`scenarioId === '1772' && year === startYear`, `:38-39`); the soft two-tier filter is `strict = eligible.filter(...); pool = strict.length>0 ? strict : eligible` (`:41-44`). Every later draft-year + all of 1856 fall through to a soft `+25 PV` preference on the 3-bucket `personality` (`:45-52`) = option-#4-with-a-thumb-on-the-scale, NOT a hard gate. Base `Faction` (`types.ts:1293`) has no `eligibleIdeologies`. **★ TERMINOLOGY TRAP (heed):** this thread's "ideology card" = the 7-point PERSONAL-ideology slot (`Politician.ideology`), NOT the shipped `IdeologyCardId` named policy/alignment cards (`types.ts:322`) — a distinct mechanic that reused the name.

The top-of-queue framing is UNCHANGED — QW0 → K0/K2 → K3/K4 + scenarioBoot → E1 — but #296 (K0) hardens from "nice-to-have determinism" to "hard prerequisite for the #20 gov-action engine + OBJECTIVE-1 testability." ★ The HEADLINE is debt #191 (#20 deterministic CPU Gov-Action engine) — L, GREENFIELD: a Gov-Action library + an execution phase + the d100 decision hierarchy + the ~150-item Requirement List + protégé/+1-bonus candidate selection. It is BLOCKED on #296 (K0) and on exporting the team rules spreadsheet. > **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed/corrected at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority):**
> - **#296 (K0) — CONFIRMED.** `src/rng.ts` exposes `d100()` (`:12`) and `rollVs(target): {roll, success, margin}` (`:16`) — the EXACT d100-vs-threshold mechanic the CPU rules need — but `rand()` (`:4`) and every wrapper (`d`, `d100`, `rollVs`, `pickWeighted`, `pick`, `shuffle`, `chance`, `uid`) call **`Math.random()`** directly. Header comment is the smoking gun: *"We use Math.random by default… deterministic seeding can be plugged in later."* This is the existing debt #1/#2/#3 cluster's root; batch 50 ELEVATES debt #3 to the gating dependency for #20. Confirmed it is the prerequisite the CPU-rules OBJECTIVE-1 needs. Engine-wide raw-`Math.random` count: **14 calls** (11 `phaseRunners.ts`, 2 `revolutionaryWar.ts`, 1 `continentalCongress.ts`) + the scenario builders (`scenario1856.ts:83,99,113`, `politicians1856.ts` 12×, `politicians1772.ts`, `scenario1772.ts:109`).
> - **#20 — CONFIRMED 0% shipped.** `grep govAction|GovAction|governorAction` over `src/` = **0 hits**. `GovernorsPage.tsx` is a READ-ONLY `<table>` (registered passively `registry.ts:87`). The ONLY `industries` mutator is the PR7 passive lobby nudge at phase 2.1.8 (`phaseRunners.ts:1631-1656`) — for each state, each faction with a living resident member bumps each `LOBBY_INDUSTRY[card]` key **already present** on `s.industries` by +1 (clamp ≤5, deduped per (state,key)/year; `if (!(key in s.industries)) continue` — cannot ADD an industry). `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` (`types.ts:398`) maps lobby→industry (Merchants→`['shipping','finance']`, SmallFarmers→`['agriculture']`, NorthernIndustry→`['manufacturing','coal']`) as a side-effect, NOT a governor choice. **NO `electionBonus` field anywhere** (the "+1-election-bonus" candidate-selection expansion has no data target). The CPU-rule PATTERN exists for other phases (`chance(gate)` over RNG) but is not applied to gov actions.
> - **#294 — CONFIRMED taxonomy mismatch.** `State.industries` (`types.ts:1328`) is a free-form `Record<string, number>` with **NO eligibility/forbidden field** on `State` and no forbidden-industry structure in `src/`. Shipped keys are ad-hoc (`states1856.ts`: `manufacturing/finance/fishing/lumber/agriculture/coal/shipping/tobacco/cotton/sugar/cattle/mining`) vs the canonical 9-industry taxonomy (Finance/Maritime/High-Tech/Natural-Gas/Plantation/Mining/Manufacturing/Agriculture/Alt-Energy) — **no `plantation`, `maritime`, `high tech`, `natural gas`, or `alt energy` exists in shipped data.** No era-evo rollout; no Plantation→Ag 2:1 conversion at 1865 (#278).
> - **#295 — CONFIRMED not era-aware.** Procedural draft generator: `draftSize = factions.length * 2` (`phaseRunners.ts:184`, ~20 rookies, regardless of era). Dataset path drafts whoever has `draftYear === current year` whose home state is in the Union (`phaseRunners.ts:151`) — no per-era target count, no Constitution/alt-state pool modulation, no R/D-balance target.
> - **#292 — CONFIRMED four resolver absences.** `runPhase_2_9_4_PresidentialGeneral` (`phaseRunners.ts:3752`) instantiates only `[blueCand, redCand]` (`:3754`), tallies BLUE/RED per state, strict WTA `if (blue.pct > red.pct) blueEv += s.electoralVotes; else redEv += …` (`:3767-3768`). **NO faithless-elector logic** (0 `faithless` hits in `src/`); **NO congressional-district EV-split**; **NO third-party candidate** in the resolver. Primary filter (`phaseRunners.ts:3729`) checks only `age >= 35 && age <= 80 && command >= 2` — **NO natural-born gate** despite CC's `presidentialEligibility` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:52-55`, default natural_born ~85%). The `calcStateVote` jitter `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` (`:3711`) is the un-seeded die (= debt #1).
> - **#45 / DH-81 — CONFIRMED.** `revolutionaryWar.ts` uses a **count-threshold war-end** (`groundWinsNeeded:7`, `groundLossesRemaining:16` `:18-19`; checked `:254-263`) — **NO end-chance multiplier exists**, so the designer's 1.0→0.5 ruling has NO shipped multiplier to change (the win-model IS the count-threshold, consistent with b43/DH-81). Fixed **+25** French-alliance add on the ground target (`:214`, `war.frenchAlliance ? 25 : 0`); naval phase has a separate hardcoded **+30** "French edge" (`:191-192`). `navalWins` is incremented (`:202`) but **NEVER read into ground-battle success** (the `baseTarget` is `planning + generalScore + frenchAlliance` only) — a dead counter for land battles. Difficulty mod is `+15`/`0`/`−20` (`:216`), divergent sign/magnitude vs the forum Easy0/Mod−10/Hard−25.
> - **expansion EV — CONFIRMED EV=4.** `EXPANSION_STATES` seeds every state `electoralVotes: 4, bias: 0, industries: { agriculture: 2, trade: 1 }, admissionYear: 0` (`expansionStates.ts:178`). Design says default **3** + a census chart by decade.

> 2. **#20 deterministic CPU Gov-Action engine (debt #191) is the headline, but is L/GREENFIELD and DOUBLE-BLOCKED** — on #296 (K0) AND on exporting the **team rules spreadsheet** (the ~150-item action catalog + per-action predicates/verbs/effects/era-availability is partial in-thread). > 3. **#294 (debt #193) and #295 (debt #194) are BLOCKED on the census/dataset EXPORT.** #294's forbidden matrix + census chart are Flourish/spreadsheet IMAGES not in the text export; #295's per-era counts + R/D target live in the same draftable-pols reference. Neither can be data-authored until those sheets/images land — treat both as **author-time epics gated on the export**, with the SCHEMA halves (#294's `State` forbidden field; #295's era-target sizing pass) buildable ahead of the data.
> 4. **#292's four election absences (debt #195) FOLD into the existing #184/#292 election rework** — they are sub-items on the same `calcStateVote`/resolver functions, each S-M; do NOT spin them out.
> 5. **Near-term cheap-ish wins:** **#293 difficulty model (debt #192)** — S/M, independent, mostly a read-only advisory + onboarding default (no CPU tuning); and **#45/DH-81 rev-war reconcile (debt #196)** — S, contained, rides the K0 RNG migration. The FAQ items mostly attach to existing epics (#167 House-district for seat-bias, #176 stat-init for command-earning, handlers #2/#4 for block≠vote) — schedule them WITH their host epic, not separately.

> **★★★ Batch-49 change (5-digest batch — `6801b9f4`-meter-interpretation/#292, `5261c8ff`-bailouts/#258+#237, `c9b97b58`-unfinished-presidencies/#283, `faf2d8b3`-drugs/#237+#221+#248, `b9651d6d`-VP-blocks/#194+#216+#279+#240+#15; I [tech-lead] re-pinned EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-29 and am the build-state authority; SIX new debt rows #184-#189; updated gaps #292/#283/#258/#237/#221/#248/#262/#206/#66/#12/#124/#240/#216/#194/#279/#15; updated game-mechanics §30.39. NO new keystone, but #184 (#292) is a HEADLINE election-engine rework that RE-SCOPES E16 a THIRD time and folds debt #1/#71's `Math.random` die-fix in. ★ The HEADLINE is debt #184 (#292 election vote-score rework + meter→enthusiasm pipeline). Its KEYSTONE sub-part is the meter→enthusiasm DERIVATION at end-of-Lingering: it is the structural step the engine has never had, and it is UPSTREAM of #12 (bill→meter) and #124 (cabinet→meter) — without it those two never reach elections. Second: debt #186 (#258) — the content-PREDICATE extension (meter-BAND + policy-active/NOT-active + EVENT-active leaves + a `PolicyNode` content model), the b47 #172/#173 content-engine track's batch-49 corroboration from two new genres (Bailouts/Drugs).):**
> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed/corrected at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority):**
> - **`calcStateVote` `phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723` — CONFIRMED.** Per-candidate `score = 50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8`. State lean is `baseLean = ±state.bias` (CONTINUOUS/graduated, `:3697`), NOT a flat +5. `enthusiasm*2` (`:3696/:3709`) is applied UNCONDITIONALLY — no state-ideology gate. **MISSING: incumbent +1, media-card +1, president's-party midterm −1** (grep `incumbentBonus|midterm|mediaCard|presParty` = 0 hits). Die at `:3711` uses raw `Math.random` (violates the deterministic-RNG rule; = debt #1, folds into K0).
> - **Meter→enthusiasm DERIVATION — CONFIRMED ABSENT (the keystone gap).** `game.enthusiasm` is a STATIC authored table (`buildEnthusiasm()` per scenario, e.g. `scenario1772.ts:19`) mutated ONLY by era-event effects via `applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3229-3233`). `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260-3290`) drifts the 7 METERS but NEVER touches `enthusiasm`; nothing runs "at end of Lingering" to derive enthusiasm from meters. grep `refreshEnthusiasm|computeEnthusiasm|enthusiasmFromMeters|deriveEnthusiasm` = 0 hits. Meters and enthusiasm are decoupled.
> - **`State.preferredIdeology` — CONFIRMED ABSENT (#292 subsumes it).** `State` (`types.ts:1318-1335`) has `bias: number` (`:1324`) only — a single signed BLUE/RED scalar, NO ideology-preference field. So neither the enthusiasm-only-if-state-ideology GATE nor the +1 ideology-match bonus (75% design / guaranteed forum) can be expressed; the build has NEITHER variant.
> - **Era-gated QoL/Planet meters — CONFIRMED INVERTED.** Both `quality` + `planet` are permanent `NationalMeters` fields (`types.ts:1399-1407`) seeded LIVE from turn 1: 1772 `quality -1, planet 5` (`scenario1772.ts:15-16`); 1856 `quality 0, planet 4` (`scenario1856.ts:14-15`). No unlock/era-gate. `NationalMeters` has 7 meters; the design's 8th "Party Pref" is the SEPARATE `game.partyPreference` scalar (NOT a meter).
> - **Assassination `src/data/anytimeEvents.ts:222-238` — CONFIRMED (note path: `data/`, NOT `engine/`).** TWO templates, both `eras: ['nationalism','modern']`: `assassination-attempt-survived` (weight 2 → `grantTrait Charismatic` + `pvBump +8`) and `assassination-killed` (weight 1 → `death`) ⇒ implied ~67% survive-and-BUFFED / ~33% death. The **−1-all-abilities WOUND branch is wholly absent** — `AnytimeEventEffect` (`anytimeEvents.ts:21-28`) has `skillBump`/`commandBump` but NO uniform-all-abilities-wound kind. Era-varying via per-era `fireMult` (`ANYTIME_EVENTS_RULES.eraConfig`, `types.ts:1076-1085`) + per-template `eras[]`. Target selection is UNIFORM-random (no office/notability weighting); Hale/Frail NOT consulted for assassination. `grep -i pineapple src/` = 0 hits (no "Pineapple Primary").
> - **`economic` band reconcile — CONFIRMED.** `NationalMeters.economic` is a RAW `number` (`types.ts:1401`); bands are UI-ONLY display strings in `Meter.tsx:15` = `Depression · Severe Recession · Recession · Slowdown · Sluggish · Stable · Growth · Expansion · Boom · Strong Boom · Roaring`. The authors' "Stagnant"/"Panic" are NOT in this scale — author bands MUST compile to numeric `meterAtMost` thresholds. `Predicate` (`types.ts:1487-1504`) has only numeric `meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost` — NO meter-band, policy-active, or event-active leaf. `GraphFlagId` = `'loansEnabled' | 'warVictoryGuaranteed'` (`types.ts:1109`).
> - **Trait/data-model pins (#194/#216/#279) — CONFIRMED.** No `dynasty` field on `Politician` (`types.ts:1318`-region search). The `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117`) does NOT contain `Can be independent`, `Cop`, `Lawful`, `Pliable`, `Bookkeeper`, `Illicit`, `Disharmonious`, `Expansionist`, `Geostrategist`, or `Lackey`. VP figures in `public/standard-draft-classes.json`: SERVED figures are present as GENERIC auto-rows (no traits/expertise/dynasty applied — e.g. Agnew=`Conservative [Leadership]`, Colfax=`Liberal [Charismatic]`, Barkley/Bentsen/Curtis/`Richard Cheney`=`[]`); the NEVER-served blocks `Charles Bryan` + `Nicholas Butler` are ABSENT (and `Dick Cheney` resolves to the present `Richard Cheney` row). All 11 blocks are PROVISIONAL/abandoned (route via `seedDataset.mjs`, human-confirm).

> **★★★ Batch-48 change (5-digest batch — `3b46b086`-1960-stats/#289, `5e8dc484`-polgen/#290, `e86d8661`-top10-statesmen/#159+#153, `391df03b`-creditdebt/#258+#237, `036f0dce`-gabbard/#291+#279; I [tech-lead] re-pinned EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-28 and am the build-state authority; EIGHT new debt rows #176-#183; updated gaps #289/#290/#291/#258/#237/#124/#12/#221/#153/#104/#279/#49/#159; updated game-mechanics §30.38. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence. ★ The HEADLINE is debt #176 (#289 3-tier stat model + boot-stat-init) — the scenarioBoot-adjacent PRECONDITION that #124 (cabinet→meters) and #12 (legislation→meters) need to read sane inputs; it is L and MULTI-PART, with a DATASET-AUTHORING long pole (the `scripts/` pipeline authors ONE stat set today). Second: debt #181 — the Credit/Debt `debtExists`/`debtAtLeast` Predicate leaf, a SMALL high-value content-engine add (`game.nationalDebt` already ships).):**
> **★ Boot-state correction up front: a single-tier `Politician.skills` (`types.ts:1271`) + single `command` (`:1281`) is the WHOLE shipped stat model — there is NO `peakYear`, NO midway/peak/rookie tier on either `Politician` or `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780`), and the dataset pipeline (`scripts/legislatorsToDataset.mjs:319`) authors exactly ONE stat set per row. So the 1960 boot "everyone on rookie stats" failure (`3b46b086` HEADLINE 2) is structural: the multi-tier model the designer describes does not exist anywhere — model, data, and boot-back-fill are all unbuilt. This is why #289 is the headline and why its dataset-authoring sub-part is the long pole.**
>
> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (I confirmed/corrected each brief-listed pin against HEAD — file:line):**
> - **✓ CONFIRMED — `Politician` carries ONE `skills` block + one `command`; NO peak/midway/rookie tiers, NO `peakYear`.** `Politician` (`types.ts:1251-1291`): `skills: Skills` (`:1271`), `command: number` (`:1281`), `careerTrack`/`careerTrackYears` (`:1279-1280`). A `grep` for `peakYear|midway|midpoint|rookieStat` in `src/` = ZERO (every "peak" hit is "Speaker"/"speak"). `ImportedDraftee` (`types.ts:1780-1793`) likewise: one `skills` (`:1788`), one `command` (`:1789`). **The 3-tier model (#289) is wholly absent.**
> - **✓ CONFIRMED — `CAREER_TRACK_MAX_YEARS = 20` (`types.ts:236`) vs the thread's 16-yr "maxed".** In-game development IS built: the lingering pass advances `careerTrackYears += 2` and calls `rollThreshold` every 4 career-years up to the cap (`phaseRunners.ts:459-461`), plus mentor acceleration (`:466-467`). But the cap is **20**, while `3b46b086` POST 9 (Vols21, citing the sheet) says **"16 years which follows the career path logic"** for the boot back-fill (≥16 → peak, 8-15 → midway, <8 → rookie). **RECONCILE before authoring the boot: 16-yr sheet figure vs 20-yr code cap.** Boot applies NO stat progression today (no age-based back-fill exists) — only the in-game accrual path is built (#289 DELTA-1).
> - **✓ CONFIRMED (data-side blocker for #289) — the DATASET pipeline authors ONE stat set, no peak/midway fields.** `scripts/legislatorsToDataset.mjs:319` CSV header = `...,admin,legislative,judicial,military,governing,backroom,command,traits,expertise,party,wikiUrl,loyalty` — a SINGLE stat block + single command, NO peak/midway/rookie columns, NO `gender`/`race`. `seedDataset.mjs` emits the same single-set shape (`legislatorsToDataset.mjs:97-110` tenure→one-skill-set heuristic). The shipped `public/standard-draft-classes.json` rows confirm it (one `skills`+`command` per row; grep for `"peakYear"`/`"gender"`/`"race"` = 0). **So #289 is not just a `Politician`-type change — the author-time pipeline must be regenerated to emit midway+peak+peakYear per row. This is the LONG POLE.** (The `races` map in the script is election-RACE tallying, not ethnicity — `:169-195`.)
> - **✓ CONFIRMED — procedural fallback generator is a flat uniform stub on `Math.random` (#290).** `runPhase_2_1_1_Draft` source precedence (comment `phaseRunners.ts:143-146`): imported → standard → random rookie. The fallback (`:177-238`) builds `factions.length*2 ≈ 20` rookies with **15 hardcoded national first-names + 15 last-names** (`:181-182`), **uniform `pick(ideologies)`** over all 7 (`:186`), **random `pick(stateIds)`** (`:203`), **`partyId: null`** (`:205`), `interests: []` (`:217`), and **`Math.random`** for every roll (`:188-197`, `:203`, `:210`) — a determinism violation vs CLAUDE.md (the seeded `src/rng.ts` is NOT wired into this legacy path). **NO per-state quota, NO party split, NO gender/race/region.** `State.region` EXISTS (`types.ts:1322`, read by relocation/Secession-Winter) but the generator never reads it. **NO `gender`/`race`/`region` field on `Politician` (`:1251`) or `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780`).**
> - **✓ CONFIRMED + ★ CORRECTED the brief's debt hypothesis — `game.nationalDebt` SHIPS but is NOT a `MeterKey`; the Predicate union has NO debt leaf (#258/#237 Credit/Debt).** `game.nationalDebt: number` (`types.ts:1573`) is scenario-seeded ($0 in 1772 `scenario1772.ts`, $32M in 1856 `scenario1856.ts:161`) and mutated in EXACTLY one place — a passive turn-end paydown `nationalDebt = max(0, nationalDebt − meters.revenue·$1.5M)` (`phaseRunners.ts:3376`). But `MeterKey = keyof NationalMeters` (`types.ts:1409`) = `revenue/economic/military/domestic/honest/quality/planet` — **NO debt field**, so `meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost` (`:1495-1496`) CANNOT gate on the debt, and the `Predicate` union (`:1487-1504`) has **no debt leaf at all**. The `dutch_recognition` era event (`eraEvents1772.ts:248`, "John Adams secures a large loan in Amsterdam") sets the `loansEnabled` graph flag (`phaseRunners.ts:3144`; `GraphFlagId = 'loansEnabled' | 'warVictoryGuaranteed'` `types.ts:1109`) — a bespoke one-off, NOT a generic recognition predicate. The `revenue` meter HAS the named bands the laws want (Bankruptcy…Balanced…Surplus, display-only in `Meter.tsx:14`) but no predicate reads them. **So the brief's "no debt/budget state ships" is HALF-WRONG: the debt value + budget meter ship as raw numbers; what's missing is (a) the policy CONTENT layer and (b) a debt-queryable predicate leaf.**
> - **✓ CONFIRMED — CC has exactly 7 article votes, NO suffrage plank (#159).** `makeConvention` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:6-67`) builds votes for `legislature, executive, judiciary, slaveCompromise, amendmentProcess, presidentialEligibility, termLimits` — seven, no women's-suffrage / voting-rights plank (`grep suffrage|women.*vote|right to vote` in `src/` = 0). The DomStab consequence has a ready write channel: `domesticStability?: number` is a first-class `EraEventResponseEffect` arm (`types.ts:1453`), and CC honors already grant command (`:158,:168`). **So #159 = add the 8th plank + wire its "enacted" branch to a `domesticStability` hit.**
> - **✓ CONFIRMED — `Ideology` is a fixed 7-point scale; `PartyId = BLUE|RED`; `Can be Independent`/`Can Party Flip`/`Independent` are NOT in the `Trait` union (#291/#279).** `Ideology` (`types.ts:5-12`) = `LW Populist…RW Populist` (7 values, `IDEOLOGY_ORDER` `:14`); **no non-ideological-populist axis or flag**. `PartyId = 'BLUE' | 'RED'` (`:3`) — no INDEPENDENT value, no `caucus`/`switchYear`/`partySwitchDate` field anywhere. The `Trait` union (`:62-117`) has neither `Can be Independent` nor `Can Party Flip`; nearest is `Flip-Flopper` (`:98`), a NEGATIVE-electoral instability trait, semantically distinct. **#291 (ideology axis) is DEFERRED by the model's own author; #279 (can-flip/can-independent traits + Independent caucus + switch-date) is the interim trait/party-model work.**
> - **✓ CONFIRMED — CJCS / Joint-Chiefs / military-prep meter all absent; build ships 1772+1856 ONLY (#49 is modern-era-gated).** `grep jointChief|CJCS|chairman` = "Speaker" only; no military-prep meter. BUT the admin-on-appointment PRECEDENT the CJCS hybrid (POST 12) wants already exists: `OFFICE_ADMIN_GRANT` (`types.ts:597-605`) grants +1 admin per cabinet seat on appointment, layered via `ABILITY_EARN_RULES.cabinetConfirmAdmin` (`:565,:595`). So the "military skill + admin-gained-on-appointment" CJCS formula reuses a shipped pattern — but the whole modern-era cabinet/officer apparatus (and the CJCS seat) is unbuilt. **#49 is gated on modern-era scenarios EXISTING.**
> - **✓ CONFIRMED (Washington-Rule-can-fail, #153/#104) — the rule is a normal contested election; nothing forces a win.** Presidential resolution (`phaseRunners.ts:3755-3814`) is a strict EV tally over `calcStateVote(...,'presGeneral')` — a Washington-Rule-eligible candidate runs as a NORMAL candidate and can LOSE (matches `e86d8661`'s "first unsuccessful Washington Rule candidate," Arnold 4th). **So #153/#104's "Washington-Rule can fail" is ALREADY TRUE in code — this is a verify/XS, not a build.** The eligibility GRANT (Military Leadership + Celebrity → presidentially eligible) is the documented bootstrap (game-mechanics.md); the election itself was never a coronation.

> **★ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every digest/§30.37 pin confirmed or corrected at `file:line` — I am the build-state authority):**
> - **`BILL_TEMPLATES` `phaseRunners.ts:3420-3429` = 8 GENERIC templates — CONFIRMED.** The 8 are Tariff Increase / Tariff Reduction / Homestead Act / Internal Improvements / Naval Expansion / Fugitive Slave Act Strengthening / Personal Liberty Law / Pacific Railroad Bill. **ONLY `Tariff Increase` (`:3421`, `revenue:+1`) and `Tariff Reduction` (`:3422`, `revenue:-0.5`) touch the `revenue` meter** (Internal Improvements `:3424` and Pacific Railroad `:3428` touch `revenue` NEGATIVELY too as a build COST, but neither is a tax/currency instrument). **CONFIRMED: NONE of the ~37 Taxation / Currency / etc. genre rows from §30.37 exist** — there is no income-tax, excise, tariff-schedule, gold-standard, greenback, national-bank, or coinage bill in the build. The 8 templates are picked at random (`pick(BILL_TEMPLATES)` `:3444`) with a flat `effects: EraEventResponseEffect` payload — there is NO genre/subtype/prereq/effect-catalog dimension.
> - **`Predicate` union `types.ts:1487-1504` + `evalPredicate` `eraGraph.ts:12-47` = ERA-EVENT-ONLY — CONFIRMED, NO 7 missing classes.** The 18-arm union is: `all`/`any`/`not` (combinators), `yearAtLeast`/`yearAtMost`, `eventCompleted`/`eventChose`, `meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost`, `interestAtLeast`, `diplomacyAtLeast`, `warActive`/`warOutcome`, `stateAdmitted`, `officeControlledByPlayer`, `rosterHasSkill`, `flag`. **CONFIRMED ABSENT (all 7 §30.37 predicate classes):** (1) active-institution/active-policy (no program-state read), (2) named economic-meter BAND (`meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost` read the RAW number — see next pin), (3) program-state, (4) generic-war (`warActive` `:31` reads `revolutionaryWar?.active` ONLY — RevWar-scoped, NOT a generic `wars[]` check), (5) slavery-flag (no per-state flag clause), (6) policy-chain, (7) sequential segment-built. The `flag` clause (`:45`) reads `snap.game.graphFlags?.[id]` — a GAME-GLOBAL `Partial<Record<GraphFlagId, boolean>>` (`types.ts:1634`) with only TWO values (`GraphFlagId = 'loansEnabled' | 'warVictoryGuaranteed'`, `types.ts:1109`) — NOT per-state, NOT policy-mutable from an effect.
> - **`economic` is a RAW number, NO named Panic/Depression bands — CONFIRMED.** `NationalMeters` (`types.ts:1399-1407`) = `revenue, economic, military, domestic, honest, quality, planet`, all `number`. There is no band-classifier const, no Panic/Recession/Depression enum, no threshold table. The Currency tree's "economic-meter-band" predicate (§30.37) would be the FIRST band abstraction over a meter.
> - **`warActive` is RevWar-only; Reconstruction-active is UNREPRESENTED — CONFIRMED.** `evalPredicate`'s `warActive` (`:31`) and `warOutcome` (`:32`) both read `snap.game.revolutionaryWar?` exclusively. The generic `wars: War[]` array (with `startWar`/`endWar`) has NO predicate reader. There is NO `reconstructionActive` / occupation / martial-law state field anywhere (the b45 #154 martial-law-bias overlay is still unbuilt).
> - **★ #288 PINS — all THREE disconnected slavery reps + the missing flag-write channel CONFIRMED:** (i) `State.isSlaveState: boolean` EXISTS (`types.ts:1329`) and is populated per-state (TN=`true` `states1856.ts:26`; DE=`true` `:14`), but is **INERT for secession** — grep shows it is read only by display/data paths, never by the defection runner. (ii) `SLAVE_STATES_1856` (`types.ts:1152-1155`, the 15-state hand-rolled set) is used ONLY by John Brown event modulation (`phaseRunners.ts:2942`, `SLAVE_STATES_1856.includes(secWar.state)`). (iii) `runSecessionWinterDefections` (`phaseRunners.ts:3028-3054`) keys defection off **`state.region`** (`stateRow?.region ?? 'Northeast'` `:3037` → `LOYALTY_REGION_BASE`), NOT either slavery rep — the comment at `types.ts:1151` ("Secession Winter loyalty decay reads state.region directly, not this set") and at `:1322` (the `region` field is a multi-purpose enum) confirm the intent. So THREE disconnected representations of "is this a slave state" coexist (`isSlaveState` / `SLAVE_STATES_1856` / `region==='South'|'Border'`). **`EraEventResponseEffect` (`types.ts:1448-1457`) has NO flag-write channel** — its arms are `meters`/`partyPreference`/`enthusiasm`/`interestGroups`/`domesticStability`/`diplomacy`/`startWar`/`text` ONLY; an era-event response CANNOT today set a per-state flag (e.g. "this state abolishes slavery"). This is the missing write half of #288.

> - **#172 (#258 active-institution/active-policy Predicate class) — the CONTENT-ENGINE KEYSTONE move.** L, but the active-institution/active-policy class is the keystone-within-the-keystone (it delivers program-state + policy-chain as special cases). Era-scoped (1856); builds ON b46 #165 + b45 #159. **#288 (#171) is the near-term slice; the rest is a Phase-2 era epic** — sequence after K0 (deterministic cascade/migration) and inside the 1856-era epic.
